review_text:
Deckbuilding, pixel-fiddling, meta-progression, troll mechanics, tomatoes infused with the power of 1,000 suns. Why is there a coconut in my omelette? Who decides these recipes?!
Fun + cozy. Very replayable.

Crazy fox lady Kirsche says "Time for the omelet inspection!"

Best rouge lite to come out since balatro. There are so many viable,  Chaotic, delicious, and wacky strategies that each play through is a new experience. Love making potato lime crab gummy bear turnip cheddar chicken nugget jalepeno omelets.

Wonderful game packed with math combo chaos, silly animals and even sillier ingredients. The replayability of this game is insane. The core mechanics are really easy to pick up but you WILL sink hours into calculating which ingredients are the best for the highest scoring build. The game also has multiple levels of difficulty and a relaxed mode so every gamer can find their perfect speed. And to top it all off, the artstyle is so charming and full of personality. This game WILL  simply make you love omelets.

Great fun game, watched vtuber Kirsche play it on 2/7/26 and saw how much fun she had with it and so i had to try it.  Downloaded the demo and had a lot of fun, then bought it fully.  Can say, the full game is harder than the demo, for some reason, but it is still VERY fun.

"Omelet You Cook" is a cooking roguelike in which you play as the new canteen chef in Principal Clucker's school. The principal is a picky eater and expects high standards from you, so don't disappoint him!

The main gameplay is heaviliy inspired by Balatro. Every round you have to serve one member of the students or staff of the school. In order to satisfy your customer you have to place various ingredients from your pantry on the omelet you serve. These ingredients give you points and each ingredient has their own scoring mechanics. Some give you just some flat points, other give you more or less points depending if they touch another ingredient. Some ingredients can be placed under or on top of your ingredients, some "point" at something to give you a bonus and some simply earn you money. These ingredients are dispensed randomly on a conveyor belt and you have to pick one from a choice of three up. The game has two modes for this, a turn based mode in which you can take your time to pick up  afood and a real time mode in whioch the ingredients that have been dispensed slowly move to the left until they fall of the conveyor. This means players have to make quick decisions.
You can always move your ingredients around on the plate until you are happy unless a customer has a trait that disallows this. Speaking of customers, there are various customers that each have their own traits. They are designed to make your round harder by adding extra challenges. One custoimer might not lioke veggies, another might hate it if ingredients are on the edge of the omelet. One annoying trait is that a customer might try to snatch an ingredient from the plate if you don't smack away the fork in time. Customers also have extra wishes, these are not necessary to fulfill and won't reduce your score but if you manage to achieve them you get extra money at the end of the round.

Money can be spent between rounds in the shop. Here you can buy crates that offer you a selection of random ingredients. These crates are usually themed, so you have a vague idea what you might get, for example you can get a veggie or a baked crate, containing only food that is part of these groups. The first crate you buy in a shop also gives you an extra dispense for your rounds which allows you to put an extra ingredient on each omelet. 
There are also helpers, passive items that you can buy and you get a free legendary one after every third round.Helpers provide extra income, deactivate negative effects or provide extra points. They are also the most reliable source for multiplicators for your score, essential if you want to reach high scores. On higher difficutlies the max number of helpers you can have is however decreased.

By achieving enough points with different ingredients you will unlock new ingredients for you to play. This adds some nice variety and replayability to the game, ideal for a roguelike.

I also like the soundtrack, the game has a chill main theme that plays through an entire run but depending on which customer you have, the theme will sound different.
I would say the game is a bit easier than other roguelikes but that doesn't bother me, sometimes you want to play a more chill game.
Chaotic fun rougelike inventory management game requiring strategy and luck! Got it after watching Kirsche play on stream.

Found this game through Kirsche and I'm glad I did!

Very much an easy to learn, difficult to master kind of game that's awesome to spend an hour or two at a time playing. The experience can be described as soft, yet crunchy and a little chewy - just like a well made omelet!

Omelet You Cook is hands down one of the best cooking roguelikes I've ever played. The visuals and music are so cozy and chaotic at the same time, the roguelike mechanics and items are really fun, and just how could you not have fun making chaotic omeletes? 

https://youtu.be/EplFpt_9c-g

I am addicted to the luck be a landlord, Balatro style games. This is just another perfect example of playing with the formula.
This does a lot of interesting things by adding the conveyor belt and physics. it becomes a puzzle of trying to think on your feet on how to place everything quickly on the omelet for the most points. If the pressure is too much you can even turn off timers and conveyor belts and have all the time you need to think. 

 I foresee a lot more time put into this game and lots of sleepless nights and missed deadlines.

Decided to buy it after watching a fox lady play the game, very easy to get into and learn, great for trying to get someone who thinks most games of the genre are 'too hard' to get into. gameplay is so intuitive that you can get by just by trying to make things look good even

Fantastic game that's charming, fun, and engaging. Grows over time and never gets stale. Highly recommended!

Very unique roguelike with chaotically scaling gameplay and great premise, has alot of heart :)

Make sure you play realtime mode

I've been buying and installing roguelites/likes for months now, looking for something with the playability, replayability, aesthetic cohesion, and depth enough to hold on to my ever deteriorating attention span. With Omelet You Cook, I can confidently say I found it. This is a fantastic game that clearly has a ton of love and thought behind it. It's fun, funny, challenging, and replayable in a way that many, if not most, rogues are not. Most the time, i 'get' the gameplay loop and immediately lose interest. I'm just about 10 hours into the game and i still feel like a complete noob, but not in an alienating way, but in that i'm excited to understand the more advanced mechanics and tactics necessary to reach higher levels.

Similar to Luck Be a Landlord(though, with a lot more depth), Balatro(in it's complexity and challenge), and many other games I consider quite good, it's definitely going to be something I continue to pick up even after I complete a substantial amount of the challenges presented.

I hope the developers continue to add content in the form of new items, classes, or even gameplay modes. There's a lot here. I'll be on the lookout with these devs for future projects as well. good job y'all, thanks for the fun game.
I can't wait for Northernlion to play this game for 2 days, call it "what the Papa's game's should have been", and go back to Isaac once he sees the horrors of red apron+
Cook Serve Delicious and Balatro got together to make a ridiculous game, and I love it so much. It's great.
Kirsche played the game on stream have to give her credit for finding the game its relaxing and fun to play.

It only took me three hours and change to understand the title of this game is a pun for "Imma let you cook."

The games great, the closest thing to compare it to is Balatro, but instead of Poker its a point and tag based omelet puzzle; but just because that's the closest comparison doesn't mean the game is a clone of any sort. It's clearly inspired by the scoring system but definitely makes its own waves in design. I mean come on, have you ever heard of a point and tag based scoring puzzle?

The similarities between Omelet You Cook and Balatro remind me of how after Hades 1 became popular, there was a subgenre (that still persists to this day) of action roguelikes with the "Pick 1 of 3" progression system with select rerolls and RNG manipulation. Similar case with Vampire Survivors, but I'm getting off track. In another time we may have called these games Balatro-Likes, Hades-Likes, or Survivor-Likes; but I think its healthier that we recognize and appreciate the development and popularization of new mechanics to be taken as inspiration for a piece of another work. 

All this to say, Omelet You Cook is very unique, rewarding, enjoyable, and stylistically enthralling. Its worth your time, whether you plan to do one run or one thousand. 

If I could pick out two little things that I really liked:

1. One free removal per round, with further removals depending on your economic strategy. It adds a totally new layer of depth related to the amount of an ingredient given when you gain said ingredient; as a key part of progression is getting new ingredients to allow you to place more food, but also whittling down you pantry (deck) such as to give your builds the space to flourish. This little design choice here made it so clear to me just how much depth there was in the ingredient system.

2. The little animations on at least one of the helpers. I forget what its called, but its this helper that turns into two copies of a helper you fire (sell) and it plays this cute little animation when you do. I don't know why but this felt like such a flex from the developers, to take what's normally a static image possibly with effects displayed atop them and turn it into an animation. It made me feel like the developers paid extra attention to every little thing, so much so that even more astute players could take a very long time to notice and appreciate them.
Balatro but cooking, so basically perfect.
This game is amazing. Unique and fun. Every run even if it starts the same does not end that way unless you build it to. Artwork is fun and engaging. Well worth the time and money.
Very cute, and a very simple premise that can start to get really complicated the more ingredients you add. The strategising for which combination of ingredients work best together is really fun!

funnyand witty. endless content and combos. mechanically superb. awesome pixel art
next level roguelite.

really fun rogue-like. love the pixel art and funny character stories

Writing is good, art is great, sound design is solid. I have nothing but good things to say about this. Great work devs! Keep it up!

This is the BEST roguelite since Balatro! The puzzle aspect of rotating ingredients is so much fun! I also love the music and the games funny charming visuals!

Would be super cool if there were custom runs added :D

I'd say this is up there with Balatro as one of the best non-standard roguelikes on Steam. 10/10 would recommend.

Fantastic loop, incredibly fun and unique gameplay, engaging art style, and music that you'll be humming to yourself for years to come. I could go on forever, if you're on the fence, take it. Balatro with a conveyer belt. Fun to play, hope they update it more.
Balatro meets the kitchen... v good game and challenging a steal at this price!
Unassumingly fun!! Tons of ingredients, charming characters, and so many "just one more run" moments. Great roguelite!

This outrageously silly game is a wonderful treat. I'm not sure if I could consume most of the omelets I'll be making, but they do give me a lot of points!

All of the features are thoroughly fleshed out and well thought out. Even the simpler things make for a totally unique challenge. Some ingredients needing half a dozen others to gain points, or others wiggling around on and off the omelet, really change up the flow as well. Picking the right ingredients to mesh (or hopefully to mesh) make your choices actually mean something. I haven't had or seen a run where you "win" by the first shop. It doesn't seem likely that will happen very often, especially with how much more difficult the game gets with each apron.

I hope they implement being able to see others' omelets (or the winning omelet) on the leaderboards. I'm not sure if that's possible or not, but that is about the only feature missing that would add some enjoyment for me.

This game is a perfect blend of Balatro-esque gameplay, and it sometimes feels like Keep Talking and Nobody Explodes. The fact that some of the ingredients NEED to be monitored is wonderful. They're dangerous but can help you out tremendously. 

I HIGHLY recommend at least trying this game out or watching it be played to get a better feel for it. If you're a fan of rougelikes, it doesn't get much better than this. It's a relaxing game AND a stressful game, depending on how you choose to play. The replayability is absurd. The music is solid and the changes with it are great. The differences between runs feels perfect and I barely have unlocked any new ingredients.

I can honestly say this is one of my favorite games of recent memory, and I look forward to 100% it.

I'm only a few runs in, but I'm having a great time. The art style is unique, the soundtrack is phenomenal, the characters have fun quirks, and the roguelike gameplay loop is enjoyable time and again. 

I tend to compare each roguelike I play to Balatro. In my not-so-unpopular opinion, that's the upper echelon of titles in this genre. From what I can tell so far, this game isn't quite as complex. Runs of Omelet take 10-20 minutes on average until you hit Endless mode, whereas Balatro runs lock me in for 30+ minutes and have me over analyzing joker combinations. No, it's not Balatro, but Omelet clearly has 'it.'

This take on roguelike deck builders feels more 'hands on,' allowing you to design omelettes that score points based on the ingredients and modifiers in your 'deck.'

At its current sale price of $9.89 (and its current full price of $15) it's well worth it to support indie devs on a clear passion project. Fantastic on Steam Deck, too.

An absolutely wonderful nderdul roguelite with runs that respect your time. You can even change hats to remove real time events, which is greatly appreciated for when I'm a sad little baby with a pain flare up. Starting to move into different aprons and unlocking different ingredients now and it's exciting to see strategies unfold and the points rack up, the music ties in perfectly. I'm gonna be a wacky little omelet chef >:3
its an ok game, i just dont like how the ingredients move around if you bump into them . Ill give it some more time
An absolute gem of a game. Great gameplay loops, art, and music. It even has a chill casual mode if you just want to vibe.

genuinely fun & creative game!

gameplay strikes a perfect balance of addictive & relaxing.

GREAT soundtrack, I love how dynamic it is... different characters & situations will introduce new instruments & change the sound selection of the theme song. details like this add a lot to a game for me :)

cute art & nostalgic graphics throughout! lots of love went into this game and it's clear through every detail.
It's cute, it's fun, it's an original "roguelike" in a sea of derivatives, it's worth your time and it's a shame it isn't more popular.

-very cool roguelike with some unique gameplay

-big balatro-esque vibes

Balatro, but you're making omelets.

That sentence should be enough to sell this game to most players, but I can barely begin to describe how little it does the game justice. I've played for about 4 hours since I loaded it the first time, and I've been smiling non-stop, for the entire duration. From the word, "go," the game oozes passion and charm, and displays a level of polish preserved for only the most loved and focused of indie projects. Runs that go well feel like watching art come together, runs that go poorly leave you strategizing for the next play, and runs that suddenly become catastrophic are always a result of player error that can't help but leave you laughing as a squid shoves all your ingredients off your omelet, or your entire omelet is suddenly on fire. If you want something fun and fast, this game is it. If you want something slower and methodical, even cozy and comical, you can turn on turn-based mode and enjoy the game exactly the same.

If you enjoy Balatro-like rogue-likes, this is a must buy. If you have an appreciation for comedy and kitchen work, this is a must buy. If you appreciate impeccable design and polish in fun indie titles that you can easily sink hundreds of hours into, this is a must buy.

I've played two hours, but I'm in love with everything this game has in store. The art style, the gameplay, the different foods you can get, the different combinations you can make to get some absolutely crazy points from. It reminds me of the parts of Balatro that just keep you coming back, and keep you playing. I've literally only done the first starter deck and almost all the difficulties with it, and I can see myself getting all the achievements in this game just to see what's in store once you have all the different food items in the game. Check it out. 10/10

Super fun, great idea and well executed

But, small gripe, the 1.0 release broke touch screen support. On the demo I was able to easily drag and drop ingredients with a touch screen laptop. This is not possible on the full game, I have to first touch then drag which is more difficult at speed. 

Please fix

A fun addition to the deckbuilding roguelike genre with charmingly ridiculous pixel art. I love how completely chaotic some rounds can be and it's nice to have a turn-based option for when you just want to relax and create weird omelettes (although I think the realtime conveyor in the regular mode is what really sets the gameplay apart from others in this genre like Luck Be A Landlord, Ballionaire, Balatro etc...)

I've encountered a couple of very minor kinks running it on my SteamDeck at release but the developers are super responsive on Reddit/Discord and have promised to address them.

Wonderful game. As someone who has played  a myriad of roguelikes, I always love playing one that changes up the format, and this does just that. It being 1.0 as well just blows my mind for how much is already in the game. I highly recommend.
Very unique gameplay, truly charming. I had a great time. I can tell this game was made with love.

I almost want to say this is my first roguelike game... I don't play them because I feel too dumb, hahaha. I have to use my brain for this one which makes it pretty challenging, LOL. I love this game though. It is so cute and definitely a game meant for me!!!! I love the art, sounds, and music. 10/10 recommend! So glad I gave in and purchased it. Great trailer too (second video that's posted on the storefront page). Ha. =)

In short:

Solid roguelike
Feels like it was made with love and care by the 2 devs. Art and music is cute, gameplay is great.
Similar to Balatro, but requires some quick thinking and finesse.
Some mechanics can be unpredictable, and end things in funny or infuriating ways
Unlocks are critical to higher difficulty progress; it feels good to unlock and use them, but tracking them is a bit annoying

Try the demo, you'll know if it's a purchase after a few runs.
This is now my THE go-to cozy game to unwind in the afternoons. Superb gameplay loop, spot-on music and cutesy playful graphics. Hyped for the future updates

Apparently I haven't reviewed this yet. The game is super interesting, pretty much a balatrolike, but instead of poker hands you make an omelet. Looks very good, plays equally well. My favourite part is that this game has leaderboards, so even if you beat all the pantries, there's still plenty of challenge there. 

Only thing I don't really like is that late game it can be very unclear how much your omelet will score.

Strong recommend

Looks like a nice game so I leave a positive review, but it's one of these games that say "100% controller support" ego Steam Deck compatibility ; but you have to use the sticks as a mouse, which is something I hate, so I have to ask a refund

Date Played:  2/7/26
Time Played: 63 minutes
Rating: 9/10

Art Style:  Retro pixel art (SNES vibe)
Animation:  Very pleasing. and the art is fun to look at
Music: N/a (didnt listen to music)
Sound Design:  N/A (didnt listen to it)
UI / UX: Blended with gameplay really nicely. Makes you forget that it is a UI. 

Core Gameplay Loop:  Place ingredients on an Omelet with some handicaps on what to do and what not to do
Controls / Feel:  Drag and drop. It feels very nice.
Difficulty Curve: Mediocre. There is some difficulty but it's more strategy then anything

Progression Systems: Beat an Apron and unlock a new one. Use ingredients to unlock other ones.   
Risk vs Reward:  Depends. If you're a gambler you can get some very interesting ingredients that may cost you some points but they can pay off really really well.
RNG: Mostly RNG, you get your pick out of a few ingredients and you're hoping to get good ones. RNG works very well in this games favor.

Pacing: Fast Paced (I swear the conveyor works against me)

Friction Points:  Returns to start menu after each game. It's not a deal breaker at all, but I would've liked to just be put in the apron select screen. Sometimes the game ends too quickly and then I have to reclick everything.

Almost Quit Moments: None. Even when you lose you feel like you want to run it back.

Memorable Moments: The first time I lost. The giant chicken made me laugh. It had very entertaining dialogue and I had a lot of fun with it.
I really like this game. I like this game almost as much as I enjoy Weezer's Raditude album. I think other people should play this game.

My favorite little "Yeah, I've got fifteen minutes," game. There are so many ingredients, I love trying to keep track of them and their synergies. Each run feels very different than the last, and the leaderboards keep me engaged. 

While not a game for children, I would describe it as family-friendly, which is a positive if you like to game around your kids. 

I recommend playing with a mouse over a controller for more precision.

The Best cooking game on steam IMO. Lots of charm and good loop to keep coming back to. The humor, writing and art direction are so good, i can't deny it makes half of the game worth it already. I will keep coming back to this to keep making my signature Omelets, this is a solid Omelet/10
Absurd, delightful, and satisfying roguelite. If you like 'numbers go up' strategy games like Balatro or enjoy careful organization then this is a fun way to kill time.
Stands out among the "pick one of three" roguelike games crowd, and has skill expression as there is lots of mouse movement. (your ingrediants will run away) Very fun.

My friend got me into this (hello you know who you are #upcake) and I've been enjoying it so much. I love the art, the music, the balancing, the love and care gone into it, and especially the community involvement. Us at Team Great Auk would like to say a massive thank you for that alone. There's always new goals to reach and new things to try. What a unique, high quality game, 10/10
Delightful and sily game that fills all of its promises of a funny and lighthearted game about riddiculous omeletes. Come for absurd omelet requests and you wont be dissapointed.
Great game! Super fun to try out a bunch of combos and see what can score you the most. Would recommend!

Great game! Lots of fun to be had :) Each run feels unique, and the minute to minute gameplay is a good balance of strategy and light action.

Unlocks consist of new items to find in new runs so there is always a reason to keep playing! Does a great job not falling into the roguelike trap of requiring grinding background stat upgrades to win. You are just as likely to win your first run as any other.

(Obligatory thank you Northernlion for turning me onto this game)

Fantastic game! Simple setup that reminds me a lot of the Balatro scoring system, but with food instead of cards.  If you're a fan of these types of games give it a try, demo had me hooked in minutes!  There's a turned based mode to learn the basics, but the real fun is when the conveyor belt is constantly moving so you have to think on the fly!

I have played a variety of roguelikes and some of my favorites are: Slay The Spire, Peglin, Balatro, Cloverpit, Blue Prince, Wildfrost and now Omelet You Cook. This game is fast paced and easy to learn for beginners but hard to master in my opinion. With a wide arrange of different and unique ingredients, letting you create so many different combos. and a unique amount of customers to adapt to.

Community is very great and weekly challenges are also fun addition. The art is cute and charming with plenty of visual and sound cues to let you understand what is going on in all the chaos. The developers are also fast at responding to problems/bugs so shout-outs to them.

Very excited for the full release, this game has a lot of potential! 

Well Done Chefs!
Very fun game! got it after seeing a funny fox woman play it, looked fun.

Watched Kirsche Play and ended up purchasing.  Loving it, very enjoyable game loop and chill as hell soundtrack.  Excellent fun little puzzler roguelike.

is my goty after finishing one run. dopamine machine. devs please add a super hard mode and like 100 more ingredients I will pay for the dev time!!!!!!!!!

This game makes my brain hurt, in a very excellent way. Do you fancy yourself someone who can think out of the box? Here is your chance to prove it.

if you enjoy puzzles, i would definitely recommend this guy! I had a blast on it, did have some trouble a few times but got through it in the end :D i really enjoyed the storyline that was going on through it and all the different formats there were!

This game kinda rules!

my partner gifted me this game because they know that I love puzzle games. it kept me busy and it made me so happy!!! all the jigsaws were different and some were a little more challenging than others. at the end it was all fun and entertaining :3

A beautiful and whimsical puzzle game! A lot of puzzle games take a single mechanic and squeeze everything out of it, this game instead dashes through dozens of jigsaw variants with lots of quick ahas and clever ideas. There's a lot of reflection on the designer's personal relationship with puzzles which fits well in the context of the game. Would highly recommend, it's super original and fun!
Not long but very fun and interesting for puzzle lovers. Honestly worth the price

It is a very creative and charming puzzle game, and I thoroughly enjoyed myself. If you appreciate creativity and unique puzzles, then I think you will have a good time, too.
Short but sweet. Not too difficult and not too easy.
Strange Jigsaws was a positively delightful experience. Wasn't afraid to try weird stuff and go for it. Absolutely play it. I feel like I had a conversation with a friend.
A fun and unique puzzle game that keeps you engaged with both the story and the gameplay.
beautiful game, perfect use of adaptive design for deaf and colour blind
This game was such an experience. The puzzles are definitely big brained, but the journey was also captivating.

This game has been an incredible experience and comes across as a masterpiece of Jigsaw solving in its truest form whilst not taking itself too seriously.

If you're like me, and grew up on Flash games on Newgrounds, then you've already played this game. 

Not [i]literally[/i] this game, but the same skin, the same bones, the same kinds of twists and puzzles. A lot of games back then did this very strange thing, where they completely became a different game with the same characters and mechanics every so often, or each entry in a game's series winding up radically different because the game's creator got better and also got bored over time.

What I'm saying is that Strange Jigsaws is the Frog Fractions of digital jigsaw puzzles. And if that sentence made any sense to you -- first off, hey! You've got great taste in games. But also -- there's probably not much else I could say to pique your interest in the game.

Strange Jigsaws has a sort of delightful manic energy to it, a restlessness of always making sure you don't know what it's going to do next. Sometimes, that's annoying, but mostly, it's great. It's a drunk toddler, wandering around an Ikea, picking up objects, and making it your problem to deal with them: Seemingly insane, nonsensical, chaotic...but at the same time, hell, it's a roller coaster.

That metaphor is tortured in that, obviously the game's creator is no drunken toddler (or if they are, holy crap). It's a puzzle game, and the puzzles are sometimes extremely clever, demanding, and devious. So the toddler is insane, nonsensical, chaotic...but not at all random. There is a hidden madness to the Scandinavian-named furniture they hurtle at you.

And that's all you're getting for an explanation. If any or all of that ranting appealed to you, you can do so, so much worse for five bucks.
Incredible puzzles, fantastic world building, and an overall amazing experience for puzzle lovers everywhere!!!

Just like 20 small mazes, this is an excellent lateral thinking game with a very absurdist meta sense of humor. Excellent puzzles starting as basic jigsaws and progressing to time travel, auditory jigsaws, and the death of the universe. Would recommend.

For me, Crow Country is one of those rare games that goes beyond nostalgia, It re-captures a feeling rather than just an aesthetic. It’s like dusting off an old memory and finding it still feels alive. Uncanny, evocative, and rich with mystery. I am a huge fan of horror, mysteries and love the graphical styles of the PS1/N64 era and this really scratches both. I'm the type of person who lives for eerie atmosphere, curious lore, and that old survival horror pacing reminiscence of Silent Hill, Resident Evil, and yes, even Countdown Vampire.

You play as the lovely "Mara Forest", Who is a detective that seems drawn into the park known as "Crow Country" (haha get it? its the name of the game.) The theme park itself was abandoned after its owner had disappeared, but something is wrong with place, your objective is to uncover the mysteries of Crow Country, What is Mara after? Is she there to find the owner of said park? is she there to meet the lovely residents of the fine establishment? Is she there, to enjoy the fun and happy times of this great park? Who knows....Except you, when you buy the game and enjoy it, like i did.

The game itself is a short 4-5 hour playthrough, with the options of Tank (Heck you, Tank controls are the best.) or Modern controls (Peasant.) Enemies and puzzles are plentiful, but so are traps as well. The combat isn't flashy, its slow, feels clunky, but that's the charm. Now go, Buy the game, and uncover the secrets that is "Crow Country"

(you better get a S Rank, or ima be angry at you.)

This is one the best games I've played in a long time. Love the plot, the atmosphere, the sound design is phenominal. and the puzzle are hella enjoyable. Combat is tasty and well designed. 10/10 play this for yourself, you'll love it.
Such a cool little survival horror. I absolutely adored those puzzles! The visual were stunning, the music was captivating. So so so so much JOYYYYY!

Well this was quite the cozy little horror game! Words that sound contradictory, but it actually works really well for me! My girlfriend and I don't do great with horror games usually, so I played this while with her and we both quite enjoyed it! The story is quite intriguing, and the combat felt pretty good on controller! There is the option to play with tank controls, but I personally didn't find those enjoyable as someone who doesn't use them usually. 

The biggest downsides to the game was that near the middle of the game we were quite lost as to where to go and what to do, but the good news here is that there is a hint system! It only has a total of 10 hints, I never ran out as I only needed one (and did a test hint at the very beginning) but it is seemingly possible to run out of hints if you struggle with the puzzles or anything. The other downside, is jumpscares. Now, there were actually only a few of these, which was a pleasant surprise! There are a few minibosses who show up with varying intensities of jumpscares, but other than that, the game didn't try jumpscaring us!

Overall, I highly recommend the game! Very chill if you want it to be chill, we played on the medium difficulty, and never had to worry too much about healing or ammo! I am by no means a veteran at the survival horror genre, and this is a fantastic way to learn if you might enjoy the genre, or just want to experience the story without monsters!
A callback to old PS1 horror games and a great game in its own right.

It's good.  I only wish there was a Crow Country 2.  The base difficulty is almost a bit too easy, especially if you're used to these types of games, but hard mode definitely helps to actually force you to manage your resources a bit, especially if you're going for the S+ rank.

One thing I wish the game could've done differently is how the flamethrower works.  As a weapon it didn't seem that differentiated from the other weapons you have?  It has pretty terrible range, even once upgraded, and it eats through fuel fast so you're not likely to get much use out of it.  It kind of just felt like a worse shotgun, and I mostly ignored it on my playthroughs.  One way you could maybe make it more useful or unique is to have it actually set enemies on fire?  I've seen other games handle it this way, where the burning enemy will then take some damage over time as they burn.  I think something like that could've helped the flamethrower shine more as a weapon rather than just being another ammo pool you can use to take care of enemies.

One other minor thing is that although the game gives you a rank at the end of your playthrough (B, A, S, etc), the game doesn't seem to keep track of this?  Or at the very least doesn't display it anywhere in-game, which reduces the incentive to go for those ranks, especially on the harder difficulties.  It'd be nice to have some sort of "results" option from the main menu to see the best rank you've achieved on each difficulty or something like that.

But yeah I really enjoyed this game, looking forward to a sequel if there ever is one.

Crow Country is a survival horror game with light-to-medium puzzle adventure mixed in.

As I understand it, the controls and presentation are explicitly intended to evoke early Resident Evil (and other survival horror) games on Playstation. I didn't grow up playing those games, so the nostalgia-bait was lost on me. I found the controls to be very clunky and frustratingly hard to aim in close-quarters combat situations. The graphics were intentionally low-fidelity, which likewise did not evoke the intended Playstation nostalgia for me personally.

The puzzles had enough variety and challenge to be quite fun and engaging. The vibes and story evoked a true sense of dread and mystery. I especially enjoyed the unique setting (an abandoned amusement park) and the non-linear map layout. Crow Country is nice and short, too.
Fantastic experience even if a little short

Genuinely one of the best games I have ever played. The story was very interesting, the atmosphere was perfect as well for the setting. In a world full of PS1 slop graphics games this one is by far the best. It's just a great game, I would definitely recommend it to anyone who enjoys puzzles/survivor/shooter/horror games.

A very polished, compact and enjoyable tribute to survival horror and chunky polygonal art of the past. Tank controls for those who want them but the modern controls are very snappy and comfortable. The new hard mode is available from the start and is decently challenging and although it's short I was motivated enough to re-run the game a few times for better ranks. Granted, I've never been the type of player to criticize a game for choosing to be smaller and more polished, but I still think it's to its credit that it gives you reasons to replay (and its easier to replay shorter games, naturally).

Overall, great game for survival horror fans. I'm even tempted to say old and new fans since the difficulties are pretty inclusive.

Crow Country is survival horror with PS1 era visuals set in an abandoned crow theme park. What more could you want?

It is a very strong homage to the old era of survival horror. The atmosphere is tight, the setting is memorable, and the retro presentation works incredibly well.

It also respects your time. At around five hours, it delivers a focused experience from start to finish.

This is a review for people who have doubts about this game , even after the demo. I must admit, I tried the demo, thought it was meh, bought the game after seeing the reviews, played as far as the demo did and then got a refund. However, something kept drawing me back to it, all the positive review, had me wondering "What was I not seeing? Am I wrong?" After beating it, Now I get it. 

The Good:
[list]
[*] THE SOUND AND ATMOSPHERE IN THIS GAME IS SUPER GOOD! I thought it was just gonna be some "Nostalgia based game that made the player go "remember when games were low poly?" Nope. As someone who does not get scared from games,  There were legit moments when I jumped  , Moments where the tension had me nervous even when I was over equipped to fight, There are even moments that even had me doubting my next move in game. I can think of a specific moment that creeped me out so much literally made me say out loud "OH ♥♥♥♥ THAT!" To the art and audio team ♥♥♥♥♥♥ BRAVO, what a fun ride.

[*]Some of the puzzles were really enjoyable to figure out and took some logical thinking that once solved made you feel smarter and better for having solved them.

[*]Some may not personally like it, but I enjoyed the idea of only using handgun bullets to open jars and interact, which makes ever shot count when solving a puzzle or thinking "Do I really need that extra item or this bullet? What is coming up next I need to prepare for?"

[*]The game even on medium difficulty has a great balance of "Running out of ammo, but still enjoyable". However, it really rewards puzzle solving and exploration as items are usually hidden in the environment well. That way the more you explore and try to figure things out , the powerful you become with items and weapons. Very well Balanced, which Id did not expect. 
[/list]


The Bad: 
[list]
[*]Though the game is built on tension, I must admit I was not a big fan of the shooting style. Even though I eventually got used to it and starting hitting shots pretty consistently, there was still times when it felt like lining up the shot was taking forever, which is one of the first things that made me refund the game the first time. However, with the quality of the game, I now can say I would accept over looking it personally.

[*]Although 99% of the puzzles I genuinely enjoyed, I can say without a doubt, 2 of the puzzles had me ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ PISSED AT THE END. [spoiler] THE TRAIN PUZZLE AND THE CRYPT COFFIN PUZZLE. The Train puzzle I ended up kind of getting, but mostly brute forcing as at the end it made sense, just really very odd. But the Crypt Coffin Puzzle WAS SO ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ DUMB AND THEY ALLOW YOU TO INTERACT WITH CERTIAN THINGS EVEN THOUGH THEY DO NOT MATTER. [/spoiler] I am trying so hard not to spoil any puzzles, because this game deserves you play them, but I am sorry ♥♥♥♥ those 2 puzzles specifically. Oh and even though it was not as bad as those two , but still kinda eh ♥♥♥♥ the [spoiler] mushroom king puzzle as well, your solution was ♥♥♥♥♥♥ lame [/spoiler] 

[*] Although the story is original, I must admit it does lack a little bit and although the ending is ok and makes the end fight make sense, the ending feels lacking and the post game features don't really add much, making replay value a 4 out of 10 for me as I don't see a reason to try and get an S rank and spending another 9 hours on this game.
[/list]

TLDR: This game is fantastic, I completely understand why it is getting such amazing reviews even though it just seems like something to play with rose colored glasses. This game is something to not judge by its cover, and even though its a short game of about 9 hours, its a buy for me, and 100% a sale game without question, the experience was worth it for me. Buy this game and 2106

Damn it! this game is so good!
There’s really no better way to put it than this: it’s incredibly fun! Just buy it! I’m serious!!

I haven’t played a game this enjoyable in a long time. It has everything a survival horror game should have: limited resources, genuinely frightening enemies, beautiful music, and intense sound design. There are puzzles to solve and an intriguing story to uncover—yet the game remains highly accessible. The narrative is linear, sprinkled with light humor, and packed with secrets that are plentiful but never frustrating to find.

The controls are intuitive, the gameplay is easy to grasp, and the rotating isometric camera works wonderfully. Even though you can finish it in around 10 hours, it feels perfectly satisfying—not too long, not too short. Add in the sweet nostalgia of PS1-era aesthetics and classic Resident Evil vibes, and the experience feels just right.

Seriously. Buy it.
This is one of those rare hidden gems buried in the mud.
Really solid little game, very well made, great story, good price. 10/10

One of the most refreshing games I've played in a while. So many games are all the same now it's really nice to see something like this embrace the style its emulating, with a bizarre narrative, immersive controls, and a really rewarding exploration system. The art style is unlike anything I've seen before. It's like if games like Mario RPG and Donkey Kong Country were full 3d environments, rather then prerendered 3d assets. There wasn't a single moment during my playthrough where I wasn't in love with this game's art style. The sound design and music are frighteningly good, featuring surprisingly catchy tunes, and ambience that made my heart sink on more then one occasion. The story had my attention the whole way through, solving puzzles and mysteries all the way through pushing me closer to the answers I sought. Well worth the price!

I had never played any games intensely before, except for this one. If Resident Evil, Silent Hill, and Final Fantasy VII on the PlayStation 1 had a child, this gem would be born.

I had been eyeing this game for two years ever since the IGN YouTube channel released the official trailer. What makes it interesting are the CRT effects and the PlayStation 1–style polygonal visuals.

The characters and their respective dialogues are well staged, including the puzzles that need to be solved (they’re actually quite easy if you read everything carefully and listen to what the characters are saying).

The game modes are also fair with their descriptions, but you really can’t take it easy with the final boss—mutated Edward Crow—if you’re playing the Murder of Crows mode (unless you've a handful of ammo for your gold-plated magnum gun and some grenades).

The bonus items for the 17-year-old protagonist, Mara Forest, are really nice. You get a lot of them, such as the crowbar, crow launcher, Zapgirl costume (you can find the original poster at Ocean Kingdom’s arcade), the crow hat costume, and the crowsbow. They really paid off the time and effort I put into the game.

Even though you’re playing Exploration Mode, there’s still something like the Crowquest, where you've to find 42 crystal crows around the theme park’s surface in order to unlock the Zapgirl costume at a hidden corner of the lounge room. So, it’s really worth it!

This game has one of the eeriest backstories, from the founder of Crow Country’s theme park to the enemies (also known as Guests), who came from the year 2106—118 years after they began invading in 1988—as well as key characters like Tolman and Marvin Trumble, especially if you read their letters and notes.

Last but not least, I’m writing this review after retrying the Murder of Crows mode and getting a B+. Satisfied? Yes—especially since I got a C+ before this.

9.5/10

[h1]Artstyle[/h1]
Retro PSX like graphics which fit very nicely. Character designs are nice and same goes for the enemies!

Whether you prefer that less resource-intensive gameplay may decide whether or not this is for you, but if you enjoy the PS1 Resi games, this is definitely worth an afternoon playthrough, and if those old games are hard to revisit for you, this is a great survival horror game to jump into the genre with.

As someone who's never played any of the old resident evil games or even many horror games, I really enjoyed this game. The old PS1 aesthetic is a great homage to games of that time, the controls while needing to get used to, are intuitive once you get the hang of it. Having to stand still to aim and shoot just was not second nature to me at first. 

The story I found compelling because it feeds you little bits and pieces of what's going on and doesn't stop the gameplay flow to tell you something, instead they put clues around that you get to explore for yourself that makes it really fun to explore. 

The exploration I found to be really addicting, I found myself searching every room as thoroughly as possible just because there's always something that you can find in every room, its usually ammo pickups which is necessary if you want to have enough for combat, but there's also 15 secrets to find on the map and they either involve a puzzle, a secret code, or just finding it because its hidden. There's nothing like having that AH-HA! moment when find them. The general puzzles tied to progression are great for much of the same reason.

Crow Country is a very good retro styled horror game created by SFB Games, the same people that have made the detective gramore series, but let's get into the story. The year is 1990 a young woman named Mara Forest is heading to the abandoned theme park known as Crow Country, years ago Crow Country was a famous Atlanta amusement park created by none other than landowner Edward Crow. But after an incident Edward closed the park then he later disappeared.

Now two years later Mara goes there thinking it won't be too hard to find Edward as that was his last known location and the park is supposed to be abandoned, but soon this young girl will learn that despite its closure there are still guests in the park and she will have to fight her way through the horrors to get to Edward Crow and to find out the truth of the Park's closure.

Alright let’s talk about the likes, the story is pretty good you can definitely see SFB Games took some inspiration from Resident Evil and maybe a little bit from the original Final Fantasy 7, while still making their own original story. I also like how the story has a good sense of humor from the main character and the NPCs comments and the game poking fun at the ridiculousness of Resident Evil with its overly complicated puzzles and stuff. And while the story can be a bit confusing at first once you get it, it starts making a lot of sense. 

The characters are all very good each one is well written and has a nice designs, my favorite is obviously the main character Mara Forest she's adorable, a badass and has plenty of great snarky comments while also being sympathetic and human. The gameplay is very good it's a Resident Evil styled game, basically travel around the amusement park, fight monsters and solve intricate puzzles in order to progress through the game and find upgrades for your weapons. The puzzles were pretty good a bit tough, but fair with plenty of hints to help you figure it out. 

The graphics are absolutely beautiful it has this bubbly appearance that takes its inspiration from old games like the original Final Fantasy 7, it's quite good to look at with its bubbly colors and designs that are really eye-catching and just makes you want to stare at it all. The levels are fantastic, they really do a good job in making Crow Country feel like a real theme park,  with its rides, a gift shop, a restaurant and a haunted house. Just all the little things you could find in parks like this, I also like how this takes place in an amusement park as not a lot of horror games do that.

The enemies and bosses are all very good each one has its own creepy and disturbing look, it has a sort of uncanny valley kind of vibe to it and they're quite disturbing the way they shuffle about and make these creepy moans and cries. The soundtrack is very good, it really fits the game very well. The notes are quite good as you know I'm a guy who loves his notes and these really help with the story and make the park feel like a real place.

The atmosphere is good for the first time playing the game it does a good job keeping you on edge as you go through it dreading every sound and keeping your eyes sharp for enemies and traps. Okay then is this game scary, yes it's actually can be quite scary sometimes and even when you start getting use to the scares the game can still throw something at you to make you jump.

Also I like how this is the first real horror game that SFB Games has made, I mean they have made games with a little bit of horror  in it like with Haunt the House: Terrortown and Detective Grimoire secrets of the swamp, but this is their first real attempt at a full horror game and I think they succeeded.

Alright let’s talk about the dislikes, honestly there's nothing really to dislike about the game it's practically perfect, but I will say one thing I do wish there was an achievement for getting all the crystal crows, basically after beating the game for the first time you unlock a mode where you can shoot a bunch of Crystal crows and if you get all 42 you get to unlock a bonus costume, but there really should have been an achievement for that just to give extra incentive to want to go around the game and find all the crows.

Okay with done Crow Country is a very good retro styled horror game created by SFB Games, the same people that have made the detective gramore series; it has very good characters, very good gameplay and absolutely beautiful graphics. So I definitely recommend getting this game, if you're a fan of retro styled games or Resident Evil like games then you then you'll definitely like this one.

Seriously one of the best games I've played in some time. Felt like a breath of fresh air and broke me out of a gaming slump. Beautiful visuals, fun gameplay, and incredible music. Would recommend even if you're not a Hololive fan.

I've 100%ed this game, I have three things to praise:

1. The MUSIC. No exaggeration, my personal favorite aspect. The remixes are insane, and I always take a little damage at the start of boss fights because I take my hand off the keyboard to turn the volume up to max. Personal standouts are The Maze, The World tree, The Depths of Atlantis, Symphonic Gallery, Highway of Dreams, [spoiler] On The Case, Alter Hoshinova Castle, Truth of Despair, Alter KFP Stadium, My Decaying Heart, Daydreaming in Darkness [/spoiler], and BARE YOUR TEETH. OOOOOOOHH MY GOD.

2. The intuitiveness. The game subtly nudges you in the right direction constantly, but it's never intrusive. The coin equivalents have different forms to hint at what power you need for the area, which makes it so that you don't struggle for too long. And every mechanic introduced is done so in a way that naturally introduces its function to the player, like placing an enemy nearby or placing it in a convenient spot. Even the *boss arenas* are set up so that you don't even realize it's handing you the answer until you figure it out. It's hard to explain, but the sheer ease of use legitimately rivals Nintendo and SEGA levels of polish in a platformer. In a game where a new mechanic is introduced PRACTICALLY EVERY LEVEL (already an insane feat), the developers handle it in stride. Absolute 10/10 on the gameplay.

3. This is (to my knowledge) the only hololive fan game that heavily focuses on story. And it owns that. No spoilers, but the plot does NOT pull its punches the way you probably expect it to. I cried. There are a couple good gut-punch moments, especially near the end.

All in all, this game is well worth the money spent. It has all the quality and polish and artistic merit of a fully funded AAA platformer at a little under a third of the normal price. If you're not a hololive fan, you might be a little lost on the VAST array of chartacters, but there are codex entries you can read up on if you want to follow the story. You should catch everything important even without it, though. If you ARE a hololive fan, PLAY THIS GAME. It's an unforgettable experience, and I would be thrilled if we got more story-driven fan games like this in the future. Go show your support for this game, your oshi, and our Warden!! Go!!!

I honestly don't have anything to add, this is one of these fangames that crosses the line of being "Good fangame" to be an excellent video game in general, even you don't know ANYTHING about vTubers please give this game a try if you are into 2D platformers.

This game is so much fun. I love how the slow and fast time mechanic works both for both game assets and difficulty. You can do so much without using the time mechanic if you are skilled at platforming enough.

I bought this game entirely on the merit of Galaxytrail having made it. I came into it knowing absolutely nothing about the source material, setting, characters, etc. It turns out that my trust was well-placed.

The gameplay overall isn't quite as fast as something like Freedom Planet, but it certainly has its own sense of speed and flow. Once you've been through a level once or twice, you can really get through it fast, and it's really satisfying to play that way.

The first several levels are pretty easy, but even then it's fun to zip through them, and look around them for collectibles. I mean, on the normal difficulty, it took a very long time for me to take my first death. I got the "accumulate lots of score" achievement just by playing normally and trying to get all of the collectibles. But oh, once you get to the mid-late to actual-late game, it finally starts showing some teeth, and that's when it gets REALLY good. Various stage gimmicks and mechanics introduced earlier are combined in really cool ways, along with some new concepts mixed in. It's very satisfying to play through, struggle a bit, and eventually win.

The story and characters are fine; even if you have no prior familiarity, the game does a good enough job of introducing and explaining things. It's very goofy and casual up until about the point where some difficulty finally kicks in, and (probably intentionally) that's the point where some actual stakes and such finally arrive.

I highly recommend playing this if you like action-platformers, in general, at all. Especially if you've played a Freedom Planet before and want to experience something else Galaxy Trail has made.

This is genuinely one of the best platformers I’ve ever played.

From the very first minutes, the game feels good to control—movement is responsive, timing is consistent, and the mechanics are introduced in a way that makes you want to experiment rather than just “get through” a level. What impressed me most is how confidently the game keeps evolving. Instead of repeating the same ideas with slightly harder obstacles, it constantly builds on what you’ve learned and then twists it into something new. One inventive stage leads into the next, and the pacing stays exciting without feeling exhausting.

The presentation matches that quality. The visuals are charming and expressive, the soundtrack elevates the mood in exactly the right moments, and the story has real heart. You can tell there was a lot of care put into every part of it—not only a love for platformers as a genre, but also a clear respect for the characters and the world. And even if you don’t know anything about Hololive, the story still works on its own. You don’t need prior knowledge to enjoy it; it’s simply a well-told, emotionally engaging adventure.

What surprised me is how much it affected me. That “warm, excited feeling in my stomach”—that rare mix of wonder and motivation to keep playing—was something I hadn’t felt in a long time. The last time a game hit me like that was when I played Gravity Rush Remastered and Gravity Rush 2.

Chrono Gear: Warden of Time is absolutely worth your time. Even if you normally don’t like platformers, I’d still recommend giving it a real chance—because this isn’t just a good platformer, it’s a great game overall. If you’re looking for something special to play in 2026, I honestly think this is one of the best choices you can make.

Extremely well executed treasure-style platformer with a time-manipulate mechanic that gets a lot of use over the course of the game. Writing was surprisingly funny with some well done serious bits once you start approaching the finale.

It's a pretty fun 2d platformer, but I think, Kroni is just too strong in initial stages, should have went for progression system where we unlock new moves as we go. Initially I think she should be a bit more limited, like no double jumps, no dashes etc. Despite those minor flaws, I think it is a good game. It does get a little challenging later on, but not too much.

An exceptionally polished and gorgeous platformer that keeps you on your toes with a wide range of levels and mechanics. An incredible time, even if you're not that familiar with Hololive. Reminds me of a modern-day Touhou doujin pc title, with tons of love and passion for all of these strange girls.

I was expecting this game to be filled to the brim with Hololive fanservice,, and it was - there were so many moments that put a big goofy smile on my face as a Holo fan.

I was expecting the platforming to be good, and it was - tight controls, satisfying game feel, and level design that rewards mastery and speedrunning.

I was expecting banger music, and - I mean, just listen to the trailers. Suffice it to say, there's much more where that came from.

What I wasn't expecting was a story that hit me right in the feels on multiple occasions. Hololive fangames are usually hesitant to dive into the characters or lore too deeply, for fear of offending the real-life streamers or their fans. But this game gives Kronii, IRyS, and La+ proper character depth, with flaws to overcome, moments of weakness, and all the stuff that makes stories compelling.

This game is great. And not just "great by fangame standards", I mean actually great.

Just finished the game, and let me tell you its a 10/10. it was made purely out of love for both Hololive, and classic platformers. the controls were amazing, the music was top notch. and the story was amazing. thank you for making this. it truly was amazing.

Update: Having beaten it, I wasn't expecting that ending. Well done. Now again, I'm not overly familiar with these folks so I have no clue how much of this is in their lore as I know a lot of them like to have little stories and stuff for their avatars, but if this is really connected with them then bravo for their interconnections. The final zones were a bit painful, I have no idea how to avoid danger in a few areas, and eventually I just sort of gave up and powered through like a tank. Still, fun game. I won't go for the rest of the achievements I don't think, but pretty fun. 

Rest of the review still stands:

First thing first: I am not really a follower of vTubers. I saw Kronii playing this randomly on YouTube, one of the first videos I ever saw from her directly and it looked like fine. The platforming and moves looked good. In fact, they are. 

A lot of games lately in this style tend to have floaty controls, latency, weird hitboxes, and things like that where here this isn't really any. It's snappy, feels pretty precise, and moves are fun, the slowing and haste are really cool and powerful effects, the hit back is fun, and the story so far is .. disjointed, but I expect that to get better as I proceed. 

That said, I am VERY certain that I'd probably enjoy this more if I actually knew anything about the people showing up, who/what they are trying to play as characters, histories, and this stuff with the statues and council. I have no idea where the game starts and the fantasy they portray with their characters overlaps or splits. That could make it more fun, but it's not really needed. Just game-wise alone this is a recommendation. The music is also really good, and the little hints of voice acting are good. 

Actually going with the voice acting, the art is really good. Not just the in-game/running around art which is also good (granted I really like this style), but the portraits convey emotion pretty well. When our character is being smug and you get that little Huuhuu, sound effect along with her smug anime face it's kind of cute and ties in really well with the story. They really kind of nailed the aesthetics and emotions properly. Big props for that.


So all in all: I'd recommend. I don't really give arbitrary number scores so I'd just say that if you like action platformers then it's a fun game.

secound: i wish that the chrono gear did more than being a rarely used gimmick in bosses like yeah it makes your haste and stasis regen faster, and i don't remenber the game ever telling you it did that. but thats about it the only other reason you would use it is for a few places that require it to pass and to remove the time stop from laplus and gandr. other than that you forget that the chrono gear exists as an ability to use most of the time from my experience.

and lastly: i wish there was a harder mode than proud that wasn't using custom to make your atacks do 25% damage and make you take 300% damage. and make new patterns for the bosses, and in this mode for they could use things i said ealier as gimmicks for example or new things the team could come up with. of course i only say about the difficulty because i saw a missed oportunity with. but i still love how the game is and if the things i said weren't in their vision for the game that is fine, its not like its my game for me to decide how it should work.
