unfortunatley, if I want to 100% the game (and I do), I have to do so on a new save as when [spoiler]holoX reunites[/spoiler] they disapear from the hub worlds, and to [spoiler]fix roboco[/spoiler] the game asks you to talk to [spoiler]koyori[/spoiler], and as far as I can tell, there is no way to talk to her beyond that story threshold

I also managed to get softlocked twice

Seriously one of the best games I've played in some time. Felt like a breath of fresh air and broke me out of a gaming slump. Beautiful visuals, fun gameplay, and incredible music. Would recommend even if you're not a Hololive fan.

I've 100%ed this game, I have three things to praise:

1. The MUSIC. No exaggeration, my personal favorite aspect. The remixes are insane, and I always take a little damage at the start of boss fights because I take my hand off the keyboard to turn the volume up to max. Personal standouts are The Maze, The World tree, The Depths of Atlantis, Symphonic Gallery, Highway of Dreams, [spoiler] On The Case, Alter Hoshinova Castle, Truth of Despair, Alter KFP Stadium, My Decaying Heart, Daydreaming in Darkness [/spoiler], and BARE YOUR TEETH. OOOOOOOHH MY GOD.

2. The intuitiveness. The game subtly nudges you in the right direction constantly, but it's never intrusive. The coin equivalents have different forms to hint at what power you need for the area, which makes it so that you don't struggle for too long. And every mechanic introduced is done so in a way that naturally introduces its function to the player, like placing an enemy nearby or placing it in a convenient spot. Even the *boss arenas* are set up so that you don't even realize it's handing you the answer until you figure it out. It's hard to explain, but the sheer ease of use legitimately rivals Nintendo and SEGA levels of polish in a platformer. In a game where a new mechanic is introduced PRACTICALLY EVERY LEVEL (already an insane feat), the developers handle it in stride. Absolute 10/10 on the gameplay.

3. This is (to my knowledge) the only hololive fan game that heavily focuses on story. And it owns that. No spoilers, but the plot does NOT pull its punches the way you probably expect it to. I cried. There are a couple good gut-punch moments, especially near the end.

All in all, this game is well worth the money spent. It has all the quality and polish and artistic merit of a fully funded AAA platformer at a little under a third of the normal price. If you're not a hololive fan, you might be a little lost on the VAST array of chartacters, but there are codex entries you can read up on if you want to follow the story. You should catch everything important even without it, though. If you ARE a hololive fan, PLAY THIS GAME. It's an unforgettable experience, and I would be thrilled if we got more story-driven fan games like this in the future. Go show your support for this game, your oshi, and our Warden!! Go!!!

jut wow, still not sure of get all achivements, well played on max difficulty so at least have that. Now I really don't know what to say apart of that each time I think in play silksong my mind was thinking in finish this game. The combat is great, the plataforming fullfilling and yup cliche story but at least more interesting that others that never really know what is happening.
What a blockbuster, ♥♥♥♥♥♥. Just an indulgent feast of amazing art and music and cute fun movement, and then the story and climax actually land really really hard. Awesome, awesome work y'all.

Overall the game is good. story is okay, it controls great, art is awesome, and it's awesome to see members that you enjoy be part of the story

my problem is the last part of the game. The levels feel annoying to deal with instead of fun. even the last boss you feel "ffs man let this end already". 

it's not a game I'll replay but for the price you are getting good stuff.
Great graphics, nice gameplay, a fun story, and lots of attention to detail too. Only negative is some of the levels are either kinda whatever or can get a little annoying.

Being made by the creators of Freedom Planet 1 and 2, it comes as no surprise that Chrono Gear has similarly excellent gameplay, music, and wit. The boss fights are remarkable, especially the final one, and the story is so much more dramatic and ambitious than you'd expect.

As others have already said this truly is a love-letter to Hololive and especially Council/Promise.

TLDR: This game single-handedly threw me back into the rabbit hole and gave me a nostalgia-filled gut punch about the last few years and HoloENs early months.

The gameplay might not be as challenging as other titles in this or similar genres though in my mind it still feels great and is a lot of fun for a few casual afternoons. After the first few levels getting used to the controls it started becoming continuously more enjoyable and the later levels getting ever more complex and increasing the difficulty really prevented a monotonous gameplay cycle.
The story, while non-canon and nothing too spectacular, is well thought-out and fits the characters well. As someone very familiar with Hololive English, the story and writing do grip you and hit quite hard,
though I don't think this translates as well to someone without prior knowledge of Hololive.


In conclusion: I truly think this is a great game and everyone can and should enjoy it, though of course the target audience are fans of Hololive. I definitely want to 100% the game as it seems very much doable and the game doesn't overstay its welcome. Depending on your playstyle you can definitely get your money's worth of gametime out of Chrono Gear, though I do think that every Euro I spent on this game was well worth it regardless.

Absolute cinema game, peaks story, peak gameplay, peak music. I dont even like these kinds of games but this one will forever have a place in my heart. Its genuinely incredible and it well exceeded my expectations. I whole heartedly think this game is worth the money

For a fanwork, this game is incredibly high quality. Even if it wasn't based on pre-existing characters, the gameplay and story are all very good. The artstyle is beautiful and the music is amazing!
If you're a Hololive fan, there's even more in here for you to enjoy. References, character moments, voices, remixes of your favorite songs, it's great!
The gameplay loop is fun and fast, the bosses are very fun and exciting. The game doesn't overstay its welcome. You may beat it in a few hours, but those hours are worth it.

I loved it!  I got a little nervous when I heard this compared to old-school Sonic games, as I've never really clicked with those, but I think this game is slowed down quite a bit by comparison, and as a result, a lot more enjoyable!  The controls felt really good, and once I'd somewhat 'mastered' movement, I was blasting through levels on replays!  

As a fan of Hololive, the highlight for me was definitely the story, plus everything involving the characters and their lore.  Most vtubers in this game represent their actual counterparts pretty damn well!  You can tell the people who made this game are extremely familiar with the subject matter.  And the writing and some of the references were hilarious!  The whole 'arc' with Kobo's gun was pretty damn funny, and as a KFP, I loved EVERYTHING having to do with the Kiara-centric level.  It wouldn't surprise me if the devs had a KFP on their staff!

Anyway, this game was an absolute blast.  I've only played and watched a few Holo Indie games so far, but this is the only one that I've seen that offers a complete narrative experience.  If this team gets together again and makes a new Hololive game, count me in!

If you're already a fan of Hololive, this is an absolute no-brainer. Believe me when I say that the farther you go, the more you'll fall in love with the amount of respect and knowledge the devs have for all of the girls and their respective lore featured in the game. The plot ended up being better than I could have imagined, and the last part of the game had me getting unexpectedly emotional. 

If you're not already a fan, I would STILL say this game is worth it as a solid Sonic-style platformer, with levels that are begging you to go back and clear in record time after you've finished scouring them for the secret items off the beaten path. But then it ends by making you feel like you're playing one of the best love-letters to not only the genre, but the fandom that it's based on. 

I cannot praise the team enough for this game. When the boss fights started adding vocal tracks, when the levels started making me unironically sit up in my chair to lock in feeling like I was playing Silksong at times, I knew this was going be one of my favorite modern side-scrollers.

I never expected a Hololive fangame to become one of my favorite games of recent times.

An excellent combination of Sonic/Megaman X gameplay by the developers Freedom Planet, with some amazing pixel art, music, level design and fan service for those hololive fans out there.

I honestly don't have anything to add, this is one of these fangames that crosses the line of being "Good fangame" to be an excellent video game in general, even you don't know ANYTHING about vTubers please give this game a try if you are into 2D platformers.

I first went into this game simply because I just like Hololive, but this is legitimately one of the best 2D platforms I've ever played! The story is incredible, but more importantly, the movement is so fun and fluid. I've never really felt the urge to speedrun a game as much as I have with this one, and I've only played for 20 hours so far. I highly recommend this game to anyone who likes Hololive or anyone who just likes good 2D platformers.

Game is absolute peak, i don't even like platformers and this is sooo much fun.
Every single level is more creative than previous, bright cast of characters, funny writing, great music and smooth movement.

PS. At least surface level knowledge of hololive is recommended to understand "lore" and jokes.

As a Kronie this warms my heart, seeing my oshi having her own game.
As a gamer I'm having a great time, this brings me back to the fun I had when I was a kid, playing video games. The retro CRT shader is such a great touch, brings a certain magic to the game in addition to the great gameplay.

This game is so much fun. I love how the slow and fast time mechanic works both for both game assets and difficulty. You can do so much without using the time mechanic if you are skilled at platforming enough.

Great game, creative levels with time powers that influence not just yourself but the speed of surrounding level objects. Play if you're a Hololive fan, or if you like Freedom Planet or other Treasure-esque 16-bit era platformers.

Also has genuinely one of the most in depth CRT filters I've seen, with lots of options to tinker with, and actually does what CRT filters are meant to do and blurs pixels for a smoother image, puts Capcom's Megaman Legacy Collections to shame.

I guess I never left a review on this huh...

Chrono Gear is a fantastic game, similar to certain actions games of the past with which I am less familiar (Megaman X comes to mind) You can take your time to look for secrets, or run thru like a maniac and slash thru your foes, either works, both are fun.

Movement is fluid and fast, animations are crisp and many, soundtrack and UI are both very evocative of the aesthetic they are going for.  I would say pretty replayable, and a fantastic story that takes the Hololive lore, and plays it straight.

I would gladly pay for a DLC or sequel, but if we never get it, I will still be following these devs next project regardless.

yeah that was pretty good

i know next to nothing about vtubers, aside from knowing that the shark one was apparently at an official baseball game of all things to sing a song

that being said, this game is fantastic 
if you're at all familiar with galaxytrail's work this will feel quite familiar while straying far enough away from freedom planet to be its own thing

the pixel art is pretty great all around, though i do believe it is built to be seen with the included "retro shader" which gives a crt filter to the screen which makes everything look nicer if you ask me

music is super good, especially towards the end of the game, a ton of amazing stuff in there. gonna listen to a lot of it in my spare time to give it a proper listen 

level design is also really fun, though there are some parts near the end where it would feel near impossible to avoid damage, but do know that i was mostly running in the speed gear mode as the momentum you can get from that is super fun to mess around with. speaking of the speed gear, the opposing slow gear has some cool little puzzle solving things that i kinda wish there was more of, as throughout a lot of the middle of the game it mostly goes away which is a shame. though to be fair im not sure what else they could do with it without repeating themselves.

the game starts off pretty alright and remains that for a while, but the final third of the game kicks everything up a notch and vastly improves which shot my opinion of the game up in real time

i cant really comment on the story as again i know nothing about any of these characters so a lot of my experience was spent going "mhm, uh huh" but i did chuckle at quite a few moments in here, the writing is pretty sharp.

i highly recommend checking out this game especially if you're a fan of galaxytrail or i assume hololive, i imagine you would love this even more if you were into that stuff. imo it isn't as strong as either freedom planet game but on its own it is extremely good, and this is coming from someone who's favourite game ever is freedom planet 2, with the 1st game being up there in the top 10.

9/10

Just taking the game as is and ignoring the fact that it's a Hololive fan game, this game is 10/10.

The mechanics of the game and incredibly simple, and the mission for most levels is equally so. But the level designs are all thematically unique, to the point where I just wanted to keep playing because I wanted to see what the next level looked like.

Almost every level also comes with some new gimmick or mechanic that makes the level unique and keeps the gameplay fresh. It's honestly pretty impressive how new mechanics are added into every level leading all the way to the credits. 

Music is solid, especially if you're a fan of the Hololive talents that appear in the game.

But in my opinion the story is by far the highlight of this game. I hope the writers for this game got a raise or something because the writing for the story, the characters, the flavor, all of it was top tier and had me hooked from start to finish. The writing clearly showed a love for the talents and a desire to share a heartwarming story about regret and self acceptance. Absolutely stellar, the story can't be praised enough.

The only con I can think of is the Haste mechanic isn't really useful outside of just DPSing some enemies. The game already moves pretty quick so I rarely had any desire to make it go even faster. It feels as though this mechanic was made with speedrunning in mind, which is definitely not for me.

If you're a fan of games like Sonic or Megaman, or a fan of Hololive in general, this game will surprise you in ways you won't expect.

Played through Chrono Gear twice now, watched multiple playthroughs, and I can't get over how much I love this game. The art is just lovely! The character sprites and animations are crazy, and not just for Kronii. So many characters, so many expressions, all the character art feels so alive. The visuals have so much variety across every level, the backgrounds are wonderful, and the level design! Every level is lovingly crafted, the themes, the challenges, the way you move through the level, all thoughtfully done. The music. THE MUSIC. So many tracks in the OST make me think "oh, this is also my favorite." The music moves through the different emotions and vibes throughout the game so well. Action, safety, suspense, mystery, wonder, adventure, grief. The dialog is so wonderful. Pulling me through moments of comedy, character building, confidence, uncertainty, friendship, and so so much more. 

And the story!! The story pulls me back in every time. I laugh and get hyped and cry and cry some more at all the right times. I'm absorbed and invested and eagerly awaiting the next development because it keeps getting better right to the finish! I love the movement, to learn the mechanics, to realize what you can use to better overcome the challenges each level presents. I love how it feels to push yourself too fast to the point of recklessness, and to slowly improve your speed and control. To face some of the harder levels and bosses, get wrecked, and come back with a better approach, intentional improvements to your gameplay. How it feels to no longer have to think about certain movements, you just do them. I love the small details and interactions. NPCs, chairs, mascots, falling, cartwheels, small changes in expressions and body language that are so easy to miss, the extra dialog, and more and more. 

By the end of the game, I've gone through so many emotions with the rise and fall of the music, flowing tears as the story unfolds, the environments, the conflicts, and then such a deeply satisfying finale. To SpaceyBat and the entire Chrono Gear team, thank you for pouring in the hours and love and care to create something so beautiful. Your game is a work of art and joy to experience.

If you love well made 2D Platformers, play it.

If you love Hololive, regardless if its Holo EN Gen 2 or not. Play it.

If you like neither of these things, try it anyway!

Such an amazing love letter to not only EN Gen 2 but a multitude of Holo members, with such a fantastic OST as a cherry on top with multiple talented composers.

Easily one of the best games I've played in a long time. Each new mechanic/gimmick was a pleasure to experience. Art style is amazing and the animations are fluid. Gameplay feels very smooth and very fun to play. The music is PHENOMENAL and a few songs honestly get me emotional every time I listen to them. The music always fits the situation. The story is incredible and I loved piecing everything together and wondering what might happen. I feel like everyone was characterized very well too. I loved catching as many references as I could. I genuinely had so much fun the entire time and I love every single aspect of this game. It was so good that on my first session I decided it was one of those games where I reserve some time to continue it to treat myself. 
This game means a lot to me and is genuinely my personal GOTY, and I wish it could be for more people too. You definitely don't need to be a Hololive fan to have fun with this game.
Video games shouldn't be allowed to be this good, but hey, rules are meant to be broken, right?
10/10

I bought this game entirely on the merit of Galaxytrail having made it. I came into it knowing absolutely nothing about the source material, setting, characters, etc. It turns out that my trust was well-placed.

The gameplay overall isn't quite as fast as something like Freedom Planet, but it certainly has its own sense of speed and flow. Once you've been through a level once or twice, you can really get through it fast, and it's really satisfying to play that way.

The first several levels are pretty easy, but even then it's fun to zip through them, and look around them for collectibles. I mean, on the normal difficulty, it took a very long time for me to take my first death. I got the "accumulate lots of score" achievement just by playing normally and trying to get all of the collectibles. But oh, once you get to the mid-late to actual-late game, it finally starts showing some teeth, and that's when it gets REALLY good. Various stage gimmicks and mechanics introduced earlier are combined in really cool ways, along with some new concepts mixed in. It's very satisfying to play through, struggle a bit, and eventually win.

The story and characters are fine; even if you have no prior familiarity, the game does a good enough job of introducing and explaining things. It's very goofy and casual up until about the point where some difficulty finally kicks in, and (probably intentionally) that's the point where some actual stakes and such finally arrive.

I highly recommend playing this if you like action-platformers, in general, at all. Especially if you've played a Freedom Planet before and want to experience something else Galaxy Trail has made.

This is genuinely one of the best platformers I’ve ever played.

From the very first minutes, the game feels good to control—movement is responsive, timing is consistent, and the mechanics are introduced in a way that makes you want to experiment rather than just “get through” a level. What impressed me most is how confidently the game keeps evolving. Instead of repeating the same ideas with slightly harder obstacles, it constantly builds on what you’ve learned and then twists it into something new. One inventive stage leads into the next, and the pacing stays exciting without feeling exhausting.

The presentation matches that quality. The visuals are charming and expressive, the soundtrack elevates the mood in exactly the right moments, and the story has real heart. You can tell there was a lot of care put into every part of it—not only a love for platformers as a genre, but also a clear respect for the characters and the world. And even if you don’t know anything about Hololive, the story still works on its own. You don’t need prior knowledge to enjoy it; it’s simply a well-told, emotionally engaging adventure.

What surprised me is how much it affected me. That “warm, excited feeling in my stomach”—that rare mix of wonder and motivation to keep playing—was something I hadn’t felt in a long time. The last time a game hit me like that was when I played Gravity Rush Remastered and Gravity Rush 2.

Chrono Gear: Warden of Time is absolutely worth your time. Even if you normally don’t like platformers, I’d still recommend giving it a real chance—because this isn’t just a good platformer, it’s a great game overall. If you’re looking for something special to play in 2026, I honestly think this is one of the best choices you can make.

Extremely well executed treasure-style platformer with a time-manipulate mechanic that gets a lot of use over the course of the game. Writing was surprisingly funny with some well done serious bits once you start approaching the finale.

It's a pretty fun 2d platformer, but I think, Kroni is just too strong in initial stages, should have went for progression system where we unlock new moves as we go. Initially I think she should be a bit more limited, like no double jumps, no dashes etc. Despite those minor flaws, I think it is a good game. It does get a little challenging later on, but not too much.

An exceptionally polished and gorgeous platformer that keeps you on your toes with a wide range of levels and mechanics. An incredible time, even if you're not that familiar with Hololive. Reminds me of a modern-day Touhou doujin pc title, with tons of love and passion for all of these strange girls.

I was expecting this game to be filled to the brim with Hololive fanservice,, and it was - there were so many moments that put a big goofy smile on my face as a Holo fan.

I was expecting the platforming to be good, and it was - tight controls, satisfying game feel, and level design that rewards mastery and speedrunning.

I was expecting banger music, and - I mean, just listen to the trailers. Suffice it to say, there's much more where that came from.

What I wasn't expecting was a story that hit me right in the feels on multiple occasions. Hololive fangames are usually hesitant to dive into the characters or lore too deeply, for fear of offending the real-life streamers or their fans. But this game gives Kronii, IRyS, and La+ proper character depth, with flaws to overcome, moments of weakness, and all the stuff that makes stories compelling.

This game is great. And not just "great by fangame standards", I mean actually great.

Just finished the game, and let me tell you its a 10/10. it was made purely out of love for both Hololive, and classic platformers. the controls were amazing, the music was top notch. and the story was amazing. thank you for making this. it truly was amazing.

A well-made and entertaining little platformer. The gameplay is difficult enough to be challenging without being obnoxious. The art style is very pretty. And the story, in addition to funny moments and (non-irritating) hololive references and memes, has much more depth and emotion than I had expected. What more is there to say, really?

Great game overall.
Speaking as a fan of Hololive, it was fun seeing all the character interactions that were written in the game. I think they were all very fitting with the talents personalities.

Game mechanics:
This game reminds me a lot of older sonic titles, basically a 2D plataformer with enphasis on Speed, with speed being one of the things that affect your score on each stage. It felt great to play, and the addition of the Haste and Time Slow mechanics made traversing the maps a lot more fun. At first it feels a bit weird, but as soon as i got used to using both, it became like second nature.

The game is divided into multiple worlds with different themes, and most stages of each world have some specific mechanics that you have to interact with to clear the stage. Many of these are very fun and intuitive. It was not hard to learn each of them and they make playing the game never few tiring or samey.

Story (No spoilers):
The story was very good, emotional and fitting to the characters lore.
Even for non fans, who do not know the characters personalities and their quirks, the codex does a good job of explaining the knowledge needed to understand the story and the characters.

Even though I don't really play action platformers, this one was exceptional. 
It has a stellar soundtrack, fun boss fights and mechanics, great art.
The story and level design get REALLY good, especially towards the later sections of the game. 
There is quite a bit of replayability as well, for those who enjoy speedrunning stages for faster clear times or chasing higher scores and hunting down all the collectables.

I got this game as a Hololive fan primarily so I'm probably a bit biased, but it's pretty clear this is much more than just a fan game. Chrono Gear is incredibly well-polished and achieves things with its design I've not seen in other games before. From start to finish you can tell this was made by people with genuine passion.

And now I'm hoping for a sequel or some other project with Justice vs. Advent.

Wow, I had a great time with this game. I thought it looked like fun, and I was sure I'd get some enjoyment out of it as someone who is familiar with Hololive. But this game surprised me with how fun and well put together it is. I think everyone did a great job on this; anyone who likes 2D platformers should give this a shot. And obviously that goes for Hololive fans too.

There sure is a lot to work with when you're making something based off of Hololive. So many virtual streamers with unique designs, original songs, character lore, and in-jokes. As someone who's familiar with Hololive, recognizing everything and seeing it all put together is its own kind of delight. It's a great foundation to build upon, but also I don't think that's the only appeal here.

I was impressed by the variety in the levels. I thought the level design was well-done throughout with unique gimmicks. There's also the variety in the art design and the sort of moods that the levels invoke. Some are rather chaotic, and others are more calm. It felt like a good balancing act.

I did have a slight issue, but I'm willing to say it was more on me. Beyond simply reaching the end of a level, there are also hidden collectibles to find. Familiar territory for platformer players. I was motivated to find them all, but I found myself really slowing down and checking every corner so I didn't miss anything. And that really harms the pacing. I did eventually notice that the game will alert you if there's something nearby, which is very helpful. Later on in the game, I managed to change my playstyle and stop searching so excessively, and that worked out for me.

I'll say that the collectible placement is pretty good. Although the icons are not 100% reliable. What I mean is that if you found collectible #1 and #3, you are inclined to believe that #2 is somewhere between them. That's not always the case. This is a little nitpicky, but it feels like something worth mentioning.

I really like the core mechanics of slowing down time and speeding it up. I felt like they were useful abilities to have in any given moment, but also the levels would have specific sections that demanded their use. Slowing things down helped with platforming and taking down enemies safely, and speeding things up helped you with moving quickly and taking down enemies quickly. It helped make the combat feel satisfying too.

I quite liked the story and writing in this. There's good jokes and fun exchanges between characters. And I enjoyed the main plot and how it resolved. For whatever reason, I had these doubts that this game would let me down in some way. Simple pessimism I suppose. But no, I was pleasantly surprised by how much I enjoyed this all the way through. One could say the "finale" is a bit long— I was certainly surprised by the length of it. But I like it. Very climactic and filled with hype and aura moments.

I really liked the music in this. All of the remixes are great. The highlight for me personally is the outer space level based on Sana. I'm a big fan of the song Astrogirl, and that level felt like the song brought to life. It's hard not to smile while playing. The art in the game overall is wonderful. Great sprite work.

One of my favorite small things is Kronii's sprite portrait where she's looking down. It's used to great effect comedically; it got multiple laughs out of me.

I'm gonna stop myself here; I think I've written enough. Again, I loved the game. The team did a great job.

Just finished the game, I loved it. It captured the feel of each character perfectly, and the gameplay was challenging yet fair. The time slow/speed up abilities are really well done, the puzzles in each level are challenging and fair and don't overstay their welcome. The way the game teaches you by doing instead of with a written guide to the mechanics is superb. Level design is amazing and the music and art are so well done. Highly recommend even if you don't know what Hololive or who Kronii is.

Chrono Gear is a platformer with a lot of heart, it handles its source material well and even provides a solid and decently interesting story to boot. The gameplay is smooth, fun and a challenge at times. I honestly had a pretty good experience outside of one issue I just found strange. In some cutscenes (not all of them thankfully but most of them) the video would just hitch and the audio would continue for a moment a couple times resulting in it going out of sync until the cutscene was over. As far as I know it seems to be a me issue but I figure I would mention it.

It’s a good game, it’s not expensive and while I lost interest in Hololive itself a while back this reminded me that in the end it's all goofs and GWAK.

(please remember to boop Boro’s nose folks)
Platforming elements work well with the combat mechanics along with difficulty not being outrageous.

10/10 game that made me have smile the entire time I was playing it. Not very big into Vtubers, but as a passing interest, I was still happy to see all the interactions. With great stages, AMAZING music, the story is ok. It's good, kept my interest, and now I'm going to go back for 100%.

Highly recommend.

Really great experience, coming from a person not too familiar with Hololive. Each level always had something fun or interesting that surprised me! And the boss fights were cool too, especially towards the end.
Excellent music, thoughtful level design and amazing art, just an outstanding platformer and enjoyable playthrough

I'm not much a VTuber guy, I've only looked at a couple clips, I only really know about the bigger VTubers and the only times when I really pay attention to the VTubing community is when there's an explosive drama (What can I say, I enjoy celebrity gossip), though I do have a few games published by HoloLive in my Steam library after hearing some good things about the games or due to being somewhat interested in a game after seeing it. I heard about this game from someone in a game modding discord server, looked at the page for this game and saw that this was made by Galaxy Trail. I enjoy both Freedom Planet games, so I'd thought I'd pick this up and I'll say I had a fun time. This game actually plays quite a bit like the Freedom Planet games, Kronii plays well, though I feel she's a bit slow when not using Haste, and the combat is about as fun. The levels are quite fun too, many have neat gimmicks that I'd like to see in expanded upon in either a sequel to this or in a Freedom Planet 3, special mention goes to the mirror gimmick levels, rail grinding, and the bubbles of frozen time. I'd say, of the handful of games I've played under the HoloIndie label, this has been my personal favorite, I'd definitely like to see more from this game, be it a sequel or DLC that adds more levels, and maybe with a different protagonist to see how Galaxy Trail adapts another of the streamers into a platforming moveset, I personally think Ina with her Eldritch god powers seems like she'd make for a good platforming protagonist.

A game truly deserving of its overwhelmingly positive all-time rating of 98% and a pristine 100% of recent reviews at the time of writing.

Chrono Gear is a typical 2D action platformer that utilizes basic time related powers.

It is dedicated to Hololive, a by now global VTuber group. Specifically an English member named Ouro Kronii. Who is also the main character of this game.
The game is stuffed to the brim with references and other stuff related to Hololive's VTubers. 

Now if you aren't in the VTuber game at all, don't worry. The game does a decent job at exposition and explaining things. So it isn't the type of game where you are required to have prerequisite knowledge of things to understand them.


Alright. Now to the game itself.

Visually, the game follows the typical pixel art style. And it follows it well. The characters and enemies are expressive and nice to look at. It doesn't even go into hornybait territory, which is a plus in my book.

Each stage is visually distinct. While each world obviously follows a theme, each individual stage is very individual and they all USUALLY use a to their stage unique gimmick. Though some gimmicks are used in other stages later on of course. The same goes for enemies. Most stages have their own, unique enemies. But they reoccur sometimes in other stages. 

There are also 20 border arts made by different artists to decorate your left and right side of the screen. Though I myself turned them off, I do respect the work that went into them! 

Tl;dr. If you like pixel art, this game offers it and a lot of variety at that! Alongside some other art as well!

MUSIC! Music is very varied in this game as well. Each stage has its own track and you can unlock every single one of these tracks to play in your jukebox. Which is a nice thing. There are a whooping 
[b] 87 [/b]songs you can unlock. 

Gameplay? It sure ain't a Darksouls of 2D platformers, I tell you that. It is in fact quite casual. I personally do not play a lot of 2D platformers but had no problem playing so far at hard (Proud) difficulty. Though you can always switch the difficulty at ANY TIME. Find a stage too hard? Bump it down. Want it harder? Bump it up.

Now in terms of mechanics. From where I am at, you get 2 major powers. One that temporarily increases your attack and movement speed by a lot. And another that adds an stasis aura around you that slows the environment and enemies down. While this stasis is active, you also get 1 free hit. In exchange for exhausting your stasis power instantly. 

That is it from what I found out. You get some fancy movement techniques but nothing groundbreaking. It is a fairly simple game in terms of gameplay. But fun.


Another thing I do like to praise is that the game credits every song and border art you can aquire. 

Is Chrono Gear worth buying?
Yes. If you are a 2D platformer fan? Double yes. If you are a Hololive fan on top of that? Triple yes. 

Go forth, become a time warden as well. 

- Azzy

What a fool I was. Like some of the others who came to this game after watching the developer's video I thought, "No way is this game as good as the goat Pizza Tower." And then I played it. And the dev was right and I was shocked. Now onto the actual praise and not just "it as good as other game doh."

First off this game is incredibly charming, little details everywhere. Your main character (I played as Pint-goat) has so many quirky little expressions for jumping, walking, spitting, and swimming. The craftsmanship cannot be understated.  All your little buddies throughout the game are also very fun and goofy. But what really shines in this game is the level design.

The levels are very VERY out there, like there's one where you get run over by cars and another where gravity flips and you have to hit dog blocks to stop the floor from disappearing. Think of every level gimmick mario has ever thought of and add 30, now you have the diversity of Garbanzo Quest. Now the enemy diversity isn't as great as the level diversity but who cares when the fifth yellow sun is chasing you down while you steal coins from bees. Now don't be fooled this isn't random nonsense, it's organized chaos. It's like going to a buffet filled with exclusively good but strange food. like sardines or quail eggs.

But above all I owe Zagawee an apology, not because I actually said or commented anything, but because I doubted him. Here I was doubting the chef but when he served me his dish it was all clear, I was out of my depth.  And the fact that it was made by only one guy is beyond impressive, there's like 5 games that exist that are up to this quality with only one guy behind the scene. Very well done, 10/10 best ten bucks I ever spent, would've happily paid 30. 

I cannot wait till his next game hits kickstarter so that I can buy my name into the credits so I can be apart of some truly great gaming history.

tldr; If you like platformers this game is worth 20 times what you'll pay for it. If you hate platformers pound sand and buy it anyway.

Garbanzo Quest isn't just good, it's really good. It's very inspired by Super Mario World, even if it plays completely differently. It does share a lot of modern Mario DNA in being a platformer that continually builds stages around a specific concept, introduced at the beginning of the stage and escalating the difficulty of it as the stage unfolds.

Difficulty is higher (on standard settings) than Super Mario World and it does reuse some gimmicks a little too much, like many stages has some kind of "stress factor" similar to the sun in Super Mario Bros 3, but instead of limiting usage to specific worlds or sections, it keeps doing them over and over again.

Not that that is enough to bring my enthusiasm down. There just so many great levels and so many neat little touches - it even has a dvd bouncing ball screen saver with the main character, if you leave the game alone - it's another one of those, why aren't people talking about this?

If you like 2D platformers it comes highly recommended. There is a very minor bug when you close it on Steam Deck, where the screen goes blank, but you can just hit the home/xbox button and go to library, so it's not really an issue.

This game is excellent. It's cute, it sounds nice, and it mashes its incredibly large number of gimmicks together in a variety of interesting ways. I have two gripes:

1. Shooting enemies sometimes feels a bit bad because you will not-infrequently whiff your shot even though it visibly touches your target. It feels like enemies, projectiles, and the player only have one hitbox instead of multiple for different interactions, and while this mostly feels fine, it can result in stuff like seeing my character very visibly touch a teleporter, only to fall past it and into the pit below.
2. Chaser-type enemies (especially the yellow sun guy) are pretty blatantly used as a difficulty crutch for *way* too many of these levels (like i stg the majority of the stages have a chaser in them), robbing some stages of their identity and causing what would otherwise feel like briskly-paced levels to devolve into the exact same motions you will always do when trying to avoid a chasing enemy. Genuinely I would cut the number of invincible chasers in this game in half, they're a very "empty calorie" type of difficulty past a certain point.

I do still overwhelmingly recommend this game, it's still a pretty reasonable level of difficulty for those experienced with the genre, so unless you're going for specific challenges it's unlikely to frustrate too much.

I've been keeping an eye on this game since release and finally thought it was time to give it a go and see if it was more my idea of a city builder.

I've tried a lot of "city builder", and adjacent games, but this one is far more my preference than a lot of what I've tried recently. It feels much more laid back and enjoyable, a strong focus on design and layout but still requiring some level of strategy to keep your citizens happy. 

The game itself is beautiful, taking a more simple style but still at any angle my city looks stunning. I think a large part of this is how light functions, and it just is truly satisfying.

My hopes for the game aka my wish list would include:
1) Definitely optimizations for late game. I have a decent PC but after maybe 600 citizens I start to feel the lag, and 800+ becomes very difficult to run if I even click on a warehouse. 
2) Preset houses & buildings! Once you get after you first 30 odd houses, I don't want to sit and place lights on each one and then the next, and the next. It'd be great if we could save a layout of lights and decorations for placing structures.
3) An undo button.

I'm really excited to see what comes next! <3

Is generally a chill city building game with lots of opportunities to decorate and focus on the design of the city. It seems to run quite smoothly and be a nice change of pace from other city builders. 

I've had some issues with closing out of the game (and have had to use task manager) and it would be nice to have an autosave feature - though I haven't had issues saving my progress.

I must say, as far a city builders go, that this is one of the better. Now I cannot speak for all game players, but playing a city builder as relaxing as this is a breath of fresh air in comparison to other ones. This game is a minimal easy to play and non-demanding masterpiece. There are no limits to your creativity, and the campaign; despite not being fully complete still manages to keep me occupied. The maps are large and unique, and every building, path and decoration can be placed wherever you want and in any fashion. the graphics on this game are stunning, being a contributing factor to it's appeal. However, I must warn that the more you progress, the more your PC may struggle to handle the higher graphics, in which case, I think the medium setting looks just fine. The residents of your town only ask for one thing: happiness, which can be provided by having things like food, apparel, leisure and public services. The further you get in this game, the more that becomes available to use. the only real challenge to this game is maintaining happiness, and making sure every shop and house can be supplied. as long as your creativity is ready, this game has no bounds. 9.5/10 I would most certainly recommend this to all city builders who need a different option.

First couple hours in and really enjoying myself. There are city requirements that need to be met but it doesn't feel like you are stressing to stay afloat while you build your city. There is a lot of customization and the low stress feel allows you to put more time and thought into decorating your city/town rather than fighting to maintain resource management. Feels a lot more alive than other city builders I have played.

I would describe this game as utterly charming. It's a cosy, relaxing experience that encourages you to slow down and enjoy the process. This also makes it, for me, insanely addictive. Did I take a day of annual leave so I could work on creating a botanic garden outside my city hall?? Yes, I did, and it was great. 

The game is richly detailed and I've discovered many things that have made me and my partner call each other over to point out little details. From the immersive sound design, with tinkling fountains and owls hooting in the night, to detailed animations where swans wag their tails and toddlers hopscotch across playgrounds. I am never bored while playing. There's always something to do, whether it's figuring out the best placement for a restaurant to max out my residents' happiness, or discovering a row of houses in dire need of extra flowers on their balconies. 

The design features are adorable, fun and creative. I love that decorations transform naturally when moved across different surfaces. Discovering how many items can be put onto the side of buildings has created lots of little moments of joy, and I genuinely squealed in delight when I discovered how to place lily pads onto water. I love that the game prompts you to think about how to design and decorate your city in campaign mode, too, so if you're not a natural builder/decorator in games, there's some good prompts to get you thinking about how to fill space. And it's easy to make spaces beautiful with so many decor items and candy coloured buildings. I love that the building tools encourage you away from a strict gridded system and towards a more wandering, organic style of building up a city (although designing a grid is also very easy to execute, if that's your preference). 

100/10. I would be content if this was the finished version of the game, so I'm so pleased that it's in Early Access and we have more cities, more buildings, and more decorations still to come!

I love this game. There is so much to unlock and do to expand your city. I love being able to chillax and decorate and also think strategically about design and the happiness of my people. It looks amazing too.

this is not a good game, this is a good therapy. 
this is a literal good therapy for those who those who are looking for a deep rest from games thats are competitive gameplay, overly stimulating(usually action games) gameplay and using pop cultures for its world building and marketing because... it's totally have none of that and... It is so relaxing. I Love It. 1000/10 from me.

There is not one bad thing I can say about this game because it is that great! I love how it's very free flow to where you can even put housing decorations on each house. its challenging enough to where you don't feel overwhelmed.  Some games like this just throw too much at you at once this has a nice and steady progression to it. The small details they put in this game is amazing. Example ducks that swim in water... 

Amazing game and really do love it!
Cute, cosy settlement builder with plenty of ornamentation options, and depth too. No pressure play, and I am looking forward to further features such as (more of) campaign quests.
A very low stress fun game where you get to make things pretty. Thoroughly enjoying this game! Life is challenging enough right now. This is a lovely refreshing break.

The game is adorably in that comfortable cozy zone of gaming where all you need to do is grab a cuppa of tea and relax into building your communities.  I appreciate the simplicity of the UI and gameplay. No scary world shattering events or big bads to smash your hard work.   You can focus on maximum macro efficiency or you can spend time on the micro level, decorating and building your houses and parks. 

The art style is very cute and well defined with a cohesive theme and the game flow just feels "good."  Give it a try!

I am looking forward to upgrades and future content expansion as the development progresses.

I don't typically write reviews for games but omg this game is so good! I love this game so much and it's (almost) everything I want in a cozy game! The developers put so much thought and detail into this game and it shows. It's such a good game now so I can't even fathom how great it will be once it is out of early access. 

First of all, I was pleasantly surprised by the amount of sounds in this game. I'm talking birds chirping, wind rustling the trees, people conversing with one another, the different noises coming from the shops and objects. It makes it all just feel that much more lively. I also love how when you look into the windows of the different buildings you can see little rooms! It adds to the immersion of the game. Speaking of immersion efforts, there's a FREEWALK MODE?! You're telling me I can make my cute little town AND THEN WALK AROUND IN IT? I'm obsessed. I just wish that there were building accurate rooms to see; when you look into the windows.

Secondly, there is a myriad of decorations and they are all not only cute but have purpose as well. I love that the decorations make a difference to your resident's moods. I feel like in other games that I've played, the decorations have been cute but left me feeling a little bit jaded at the end of my efforts. Like "Well it looks nice but what was the real point of all that effort?" This game solves that problem for me. Also, even though there is a lot you can do in this game, the devs did great with piece mealing the added info; so nothing gets overwhelming. It just feels like they have thought of everything.

This is the best cozy game I have ever played, and I'm so excited to see what more amazing things the devs have in store for this game!

Town to City is a calm and fun city management game. As the name implies, it's all about growing your settlement and unlocking technologies along the way.

I think the game is easy to pick up and play. It has no lose condition, so you're really given free reign to design your towns however you'd like. There's a plethora of decorations to use, so you can really express yourself with it.

I liked the gradual introduction of mechanics and systems into the game, which really gives it a 'comfy' feel. I also like the mayor request feature, which brings a lot of lighthearted comedy to the game.
I think the artstyle is unique and looks great, and the first-person mode is absolutely awesome. Getting to explore your town as an NPC is great as there are so many details, and you find yourself strangely familiar with the town you made!

This game definitely has problems though. I don't think it does a good job of explaining how many structures supply the same need, and yet that variety itself plays a major role in citizen satisfaction.
I also think that the paused state behaves weirdly - you can get some achievements during that state and then just reset them. In general, I think it's quite common to enter a 'redesign' phase in any playthrough, which the game doesn't make very easy to do.

In short - it's a city management game that learned from lessons of the past to be approachable and pleasing to the eyes. If you're looking for a chill time or want to enjoy making the city of your dreams, this is definitely the game to play. If you're looking for a challenge, or don't like waiting, then this game is probably not for you.

The vibes in this game are immaculate. The artistic styling is adorable, and the detailing of simple animations at shops is well thought. The game is in early access, however there is so much content available and I'm sure so much more to come. The potential this game holds is endless, I could see this becoming a cozy contender to cities. I love it so much and I highly recommend (as a gift or for yourself) <3

Honestly I really struggle with "relaxation" games- even Stardew & Coral Island I turn into a hyper fueled money grind. Despite this tendency, something about this game really hooks me in. I genuinely take my time, think about how to decorate, and relax. Maybe it's the entire lack of any genuine time crunch. Either way, gorgeous, simple, rewarding, and something I can genuinely throw on to relax.

If you are looking for a city builder with beautiful graphics and well balanced city management, this is the game for you!
I have played a lot of city builders/management games but a game that's early access and feeling as if nothing is missing is amazing.

[spoiler] This game is a 100% worth your time and money!!!! [/spoiler]

(The music is also really well done! this game is easy on the mind and ears)

This is a really fun, stress free city builder. The progression has kept me interested to keep playing. Lots of variations to the different objects and scenery, providing what feels like an unlimited ability to design your town to your preference. Given that this is still in the development stage, I have high hopes for this being a game that I will continue to come back to for some time.
Love this game very much, the story mode is a great guide on how the game works. Very easy to spend hours decorating and designing.

this igame is goated for sure, but its missing a copule things. Espiecily optimisation i got a mid-high-tier  pc and yet with only 80+ lamps and 100+ trees with 100+ houses that only covers like 50% of the land at med graphics it runs really slow under 40fps but if i start a new save on max graphics its 144 at all times. So devs, consider adding optimisation to your game please
i loved the soundtrack and the style of the game. Its so fun and personaly its my favorite game.
Cozy European-esc city builder. It gives direction with tasks while not being overwhelming. I'm excited to see this game grow with development!

This is a super chill city builder. I love how much you can decorate! There are some bugs and not super user friendly aspects, but not enough to take away from the gameplay. Can't wait to see what more's to come.

Very cute and charming game. I like how you can decorate every part of your town not just place buildings down. You can decorate those buildings as you wish with lights and flowers. It really sets the game apart from other city builders. My only complaint is the game is very easy. Even without trying you can have 80% happiness its very hard to make your citizens unhappy. There is no skill to the game it really is more of a design game not management. Maybe that's the charm though as its not stressful at all. Very relaxing just building and decorating. I'm very excited to see the development of the game. Only thing Id like to have added is ability to edit terrain like let us put lakes and mountains in ourselves or ability to get rid of them. Maybe we can I've only unlocked the tool to remove rocks so far.
What a fun and relaxing experience! Great quest system, but I wish there were many more similar quests - especially in sandbox mode. Perhaps that will come later in development.
great city builder has some complex parts, but not to hard to get a hang of fast. love how cozy and fun the decorations are.

If you're looking for a city builder that is more creative and forgiving, I cannot recommend this game highly enough. It is amazing. You can just about customize EVERYTHING in your city. Yeah, there are needs that have to be met for the citizens but its much more forgiving than a lot of other city building games. Less numbers, more design. There isn't a terraforming mechanic in this game, which is a bold choice, BUT I personally feel like it doesn't need it. I quite like the challenge of working with the built in landscape and fitting my idea organically into the natural skyline. 
There are some "Quests" you can complete after you acquire your Mayor House, as you progress your citizens will send you requests for things they would like to have in the town- these quests are fun because they add an element of improv to the design you were already forming, causing you to add actual elements requested by your people.
I was instantly hooked by this game.
The only possible thing I would add would be more masonry options for roads- like red brick or something, as well as an option when upgrading the roads to leave the texture that they originally had instead of going to a white concrete.
A calming game, where one can just relax and build.  No pressures, just wholesome fun.
Its become my casual, comfort game. Visuals are beautful and the gameplay at least for me is actually enticing compared to other city builders!
I love this game, its already a complete game and they keep adding such awesome stuff to it, highly recommend.
Blew my expectations out of the water, absolutly one of the best city builders i've played and by far the most detailed one as well.

This is a very relaxed and pleasant experience and, despite being a very-much simplieid city management game, has just enough grist for a satisfying gameplay loop. Hand-placing hundreds of decorations can be enjoyable and meditative though I wouldn't mind seeing some options for automating this process. That being said, the game is forgiving enough that aggressive optimisation isn't required (nor is it the point).
I love this game. It had me locked in during the demo and when it finally released in early access I could not stop playing. I 1000000000000% recommend if you love a beautiful cozy city builder.
This is by far one of my favorite games on Steam. It is so simple to play, the graphics are outstanding, and it is very relaxing after a day of work. I highly recommend this!

If you love micro managing things and putting cute decorations on houses and just a liiiiiitle bit of farming this is worth having in your library. They'll be adding more over the course of 2026.

From watching the little people, to discovering new ways that lanterns can be decorated on buildings, there's always a cute little surprise with every new item unlock and upgrade. I look forward to more of what this game has to offer in the future.

Perhaps the best city builder I've played.

Cosy and forgiving enough to muck around in, but complex enough that there's actual decisions to be thought about.

It takes the decorating ability of Tinyglade and makes an actual game out of it.

The only real thing I'd like at this point are some more maps (or perhaps some way to make/customise your own maps) and for lakes etc. to serve any kinds of purpose.
I really like cozy management games and this is just the game i am looking for.
Cute, cozy little game. Currently it is about 13 hours worth of entertainment for me. I am hoping for more content in the future! It's adorable, though, and really did hold my interest for a while.

This game is a great way to relax and just enjoy spending time in a cozy cute world. The only thing I have problems with is the extreme resource hog of late game. Once I got to the City chapter, the graphics started struggling and I run on a 3070 Ti. Hopefully this will be fixed before full release. Until then, I'm just lowereing all settings. The game is still beautiful and well worth the money.

The game is fun and shows potential, however it's dropping from 120 to 19-25 frames if you have a large tavern with many customers late game, this needs to be adressed sooner than later before the devs expand the game's content. This is coming from an AMD Ryzen 9 7950X3D with 64GB of DDR5 and a RTX 4070. 

Secondly, I think it could benefit from mod support and larger taverns, so you can actually fit everything you want and customize it with decorations accordingly. It's really hard to get a functioning and good looking tavern at the same time, even with custom presets which makes the game very easy.
Very relaxing game, was looking for a city builder to relax too. This was the perfect game. Menus are easy and Music is great. Also FP mode is so cool.

Good game. Very cosy city builder.

LOTS of potential for expansion though. Looking forward to more content + enhanced building tools. Fundamentals are great it just has a lot of room for depth.
very cute game. Not a hard city builder, its very chill. Your main goal is to beautify your city and you get a bunch of stuff you can use to do so. 8.5/10

Absolutely love this game. I just keep decorating everything, very addictive. 

It’s way more relaxing than any other city builder.

I love the ability to create unlimited street and building layouts. and the way that the towns people interact with your builds.
Great game. Not super far into it, but It's very fun. I can see a lot of replay potential!

I do recommend it! I have been in the deepest gaming slump of my life for the last year and this game brought me out of it. My only complaint is I have to play it through a Cloud PC on my mac to play but it's not an inconvenience other than the cost. I just wish there was a mac version otherwise I wouldn't have ANY complaints at all. This game is fantastic and I'd buy it again and I'd buy expansions. I wholeheartedly recommend this to anyone.

It's... perfect. You can see a lot of love from the creators — it can be felt in every detail. I was looking for a game that I could “feel” — and here it is. The level of coziness is indescribable.
It's been a long time since I felt such bliss playing a video game. Warm tea, a blanket and snow outside the window. And this game. A perfect evening, night, infinity. 
I fell in love.
Thank you, creators, for this title.

(My only "complaint" about early access is that I simply cannot wait for the patches; there is definitely a lot to add — bridges, stairs, more decoration options etc. Waiting is my weak point, but I know it's worth it.)
Absolutely ADORE this game!! A perfectly cozy combination of managing a city, decorating the details, and creating your own utopia! Highly recommend Town To City to everybody :)
Super cozy and lovely art style! Does exactly what it says on the box.

For me, this game is perfection. (Still in early access? Exciting.) I don't think I've ever lost track of time (and life) like I do when I sit down to play this game. I've put nearly 70 hours into it and still have so much to do - but I'm excited to keep going. 

I love the music. I will, usually, mute game music to watch a video or listen to a book while I play, but I enjoy it so much this is an exception. 

I love the art style, I love how the screen blurs depending on where you're focused, the people and pets are so cute. The way the foliage/lighting changes depending on where you try to put the items is amazing. Having that variety makes adding all the tiny decorations to the houses and surrounding areas fun, not tedious. 

I love the tasks. Being prompted to create little spaces based on your villagers wants and interests? Love the idea. My favorite is the pet rock quest line. I was really proud of what I made from that and it's something I never would have put together otherwise.

I can pet the animals. Have you seen the sheep? Precious little pixel babies.

I think my only asks for this game would be an undo button, to be able to delete smaller sections from a wall/fence, and to be able to add lighting to fences.

Highly recommend. Amazing, stress reducing, creative outlet. Just don't forget to eat!
Perfect mix between cozy and still having to use your brain a bit. The graphics are adorable - I can't wait for the full game release!
What a fun little game! Relaxing and still gives you something to do throughout your time playing. Cant wait to see where this game goes and to complete every last bit of it!

This game is the most relaxing decorative city builder ive ever played.  theres enough managment to keep you busy, but if you want to ignore it and just decorate to your hearts content, you really can.  theres so much room to be creative.  love it .  very worth the price
Lovely game. It takes my favorite part of city building games, designing the parks a public spaces, and makes it the focus for succeeding.
Good relaxing game, I dont usually play these sort of games but this ones not got a complicated building system and you have free reign over building your town. I recommend.

There is one issue, every so often the game won't load my save file. Fatal error appears. All I do is open it back up and my save is right there and it works.

I JUST WANT MORE!

 It could use some better optimization overtime but also what city builder doesn't eventually bog your PC down. However, it is 2026, my PC is 2k USD, and even then it chugs at full city size. This has the perfect building blocks for the best city builder ever. It is impossible to not make something beautiful and it is so F****ing satisfying. This is game is my zen, I love it so much. Please buy it, do yourself a favor.

If you love city-building games, I would one hundred percent recommend this one.

incredibly detailed and lots of content considering this is early access!! was pleasantly surprised by all the features, it's very evident that the developers put a lot of thought into every aspect of the game. 

i haven't gone through all the content of the game yet, but it seems quite substantial already, and while it could possibly get repetitive at times, the requests system helps break up the monotony and helps you plan for expansions in a less repetitive and more exciting way! 

excited to see how this game develops further :)

This game is 10/10, and the devs are adding more content!!. If you're looking for a city builder that's not overly complex and chill, this is your game. I love all the details. I recommend it if it remotely interests you; I think you'll like it.

AMAZING!! city skylines but more aesthetic and cozy. also not stressful at all but still has enough going on to be very interesting and fun. this is a perfect game to me, just play it! i cant wait to see what they add next, its well worth the $ even without being a finished product :) <3

It is the most amazing little game I've played. It's my first city builder of this slow paced kind. I love the litttle townsfolk using the stuff I placed. The general vibe of the game, and oh my god, the mayor's walk arond the town is my favourite.

addicted to making the prettiest city ever, the vibes of this game are immaculate especially paired with such a gorgeous soundtrack. this really scratches that city builder itch. early access already feels substantial, excited to see what a 1.0 will look like for the game!

the world machine edition has more content than the original game, giving you the names of the characters you meet and a bit about them inside the game, as well as wallpapers of certain locations and characters to customize the self contained "operating system". there's changes to the actual game inside, too, such as diagonal movement (since wme was built with consoles in mind), and a map in the fast travel menu rather than having to memorize where locations take you. if you plan on bringing OneShot on the go or playing on a Linux operating system, i highly recommend buying this version of the game. if you're planning on playing OneShot for the first time, i definitely still direct you to the world machine edition before the original steam release of the game.

what? review for the actual oneshot? well the story, characters, art, and music are all great. i don't think i can say much else. play the game and you can see for yourself.
dear god don't look a single thing up about this game. play the oiriginal if ur on pc, get this version for linux/console. Thats all u need to know.

I was initially gifted the original version of oneshot by a friend a while back, started playing it but never properly finished it. When I came back to finish it I realised that it doesn't run properly on linux mint (absolute skill issue on my part for not completing it prior to switching over). I liked the gameplay that I had seen previously and decided to get the world machine edition to properly complete the game. 

I think this game is an absolute delight, very casual and very enjoyable. Sure, its pretty light on the gameplay, but the world that is built is pretty cool and always had me interested in seeing the next part at any given moment. I also quite enjoyed the puzzles with minimal/no hand-holding, leaving everything up to you to figure out.

If you have already played the original version and were a big fan of the visuals, music, and characters, I would STRONGLY RECOMMEND getting this version. If you have not played the original version, this version has all the same story as the original while being compatible with more systems and includes additional bonus content for those who want more from the game.

OneShot is a short but very impactful story based game with puzzle elements. The characters are captivating, the world is begging to be explored, and the puzzles are very engaging and are the perfect mix of not too difficult but not too easy. The game looks beautiful, both the pixel art and the additional art in the given cutscenes, it is very pretty and a joy to ogle at. I am not someone who typically cries to stories, but when I played this game all the way back in 2017 I definitely cried then, and I got very close when I was playing through this version. If you don't know anything at all about the story or the gameplay of this game, keep it that way and play it for yourself, this is one of those games where I wish I could play through it for the first time again.

If you want to know what the difference between this version and the original is, the quick explanation is the original works on windows only (and 32 bit MacOS for any mac over 7 years old), and this version has been redone to functionally work on all operating systems and consoles, while including additional superfluous content which will be enjoyed by anyone who is a fan of this game.



If you have not played the original, there are some minor game spoilers in the following paragraph. Not story spoilers, moreso gameplay and gimmick spoilers, but I would still recommend not reading if you do not want to be spoiled. 
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
The big difference between this version and the prior version is the "World Machine" aspect, where instead of the game directly affecting the windows/files on your computer to progress in the game, the game now runs in its own sandboxed operating system and affects the files directly there. I think this approach makes the most sense from a compatability sense, both for porting the game to 64 bit MacOS and making it functional on Linux systems, but for making console versions as well, and the implementation done here is very smooth and sublime. The major upside is that the operating system is able to be influenced or modified by the game easier and with more freedom than the original. On top of that the game has lots of little goodies baked in that fans of the original will love, and that newcomers will be able to enjoy as well. I won't spoil exactly what can be found in this new operating system, but I can assue you that if you are a big fan of the original you will LOVE the added features of this version.
This a beautiful game, with some of the most adorable and well-crafted story telling put to video-gaming. An excellent game, and well worth it.

To be honest this game is one of my ultimate favorite games (One of my top 5), the story was very good and the music, the characters and the sprite-work is just wonderful. I have played this game previously on a different system and i have 100% the games both times and both times of me playing this game it had me in tears at the end (tears of sadness + Happiness).

From the perspective of someone who never played the original game, this rules. 
This game is a perfect combination of casual yet very difficult. It's one of those games I can pick up and play a couple levels whenever I want (without a big time dedication), but I can also do a full run all the way through each level in a night. This is a difficult to master game. There are lots of frustrating moments but it makes it even more rewarding when you complete an achievement. I have only completed the game on normal so far but I plan to train up to complete nasty mode for that sweet, sweet achievement. 
That said, the graphics are amazing. The team at Claymatic painstakingly modeled these sprites from real clay and the dedication to that shines through. There's something to be said about these clay games. They give such a fun and unique vibe that is missing from today's gaming landscape. 
I would certainly recommend this game to fans of the original and new fans alike.

This game definitely has a major nostalgia factor but it also has new contents that makes it stand out from 2002 original as well. With its upgraded visuals and new (sometimes silly) contents, I really love this remake of a good ol' classic shooter game.

My favourite new addition in particular is the weapons, both regulars and sillies.
[list]
    [*]Fireball makes a great melee and piercing damage with its charge-up.
    [*]Lightning (I'm not sure if it returned from Platypus II or it was always there in Platypus 2002) grants the power of Zeus in your ship, especially when paired with weapon pods.
    [*]Homing missiles and Bombs gave me a feeling of multitasking, Bombs lean more into niche usage since it recommends flying on higher altitude, while Homing missiles are Rocket's jack-of-all-trades brethren.
    [*]Tail gun is also another niche weapon meant to counter rear enemies while attacking the front.
    [*]Silly weapons are good giggle factor playing this game since they're just as powerful as regular weapons, some of them even are counterparts of regular weapons too. Just be sure you don't get too hungry when you use them.
[/list]

What I'm interested in the most is mod add-on support, I'm interested to see what the community can cook in their Mungolan kitchens.

Platypus Reclayed is the very definition of a passion project. You can feel love and care put into every facet of its design, the creators really care about making it the best possible version it can be so it reaches an audience that cares about it. And I think it deserves a second chance in the modern era for people like myself who never played the original.

+++Pros+++
+The hand-made clay art style is simply phenomenal. Every ship, power up shot types, environment, boss and even the game's FONT is made and filmed for the purpose of this little horizontal shooter. There are hundreds of art assets in here, and not only do they sell the visual design of a SHMUP, they forge a strong identity for the game that is wholly unique to itself.
+The funny weapons are just awesome. They feel great to acquire, I love hearing the announcer call them out, and they give this game a strong Parodius vibe, at times.
+There are multiple difficulty levels and ship types for the people who need some hand-holding and the people who are real sickos. I appreciate having the option to customize the game's difficulty a bit, even if it skews into "too tough" during the final stretch on Regular difficulty and above.
+The music is fairly good, doubling as both nostalgia for 80's synthy jams and late90's / early2000's computer games.
+You get a large amount of content for the price, considering the levels are fairly lengthy for a SHMUP, and there are additional ones added for free by the developer. 
+It's generally very impressive that a clay artist and this team have strong ideas of good SHMUP design. The power-ups make you feel strong without lasting forever, the extra score opportunities make you really work for it, and you can earn plenty of lives even if you play safe.

---Cons---
-When I say the levels are lengthy, I think they might actually be a bit too long. A playthrough of this whole game is an hour long, when most SHMUPs will take you maybe 25-30 mins on average, And that length doesn't quite feel justified when most of it comes from bullet sponge enemies. If you don't have any powerups and you're fighting a boss with just the peashooter, it feels really bad and takes forever. Luckily, the devs added some power-ups mid-fight, but again, you have to work to acquire them, and the screen can get VERY busy with bullets to the point where you will most likely lose more lives trying to get them. It's a difficult balancing act of what feels like a fun challenge vs what feels a little hacky, and I think this game gets it right about 7 out of 10 times.
-You seemingly can't re-bind controls, either on PC or on a controller. A little odd, seeing as I would rather use a controller face button to shoot than hold down right trigger for most of the game, personally.


If you're into Horizontal shooters, or even if you're brand new to this kind of game, I think Platypus Reclayed has enough new things and strong fundamentals to keep you covered!  Highly recommend this game, 8.5/10.

This is an alright shmup.  The main draw is the claymation - which looks neat.  

The game lets you continue at the last stage you reached, like [b]Tyrian[/b] or [b]Drainus[/b], but you don't get to upgrade your ship as you go so it doesn't really feel similar to those games.  Instead you have the retro and less enjoyable 'random powerups' that frequently appear throughout the stages which change your weapon.  The powerups themselves are pretty cool.  When you see a powerup drop, you can shoot it to change which powerup it is. 
 While that gives some flexibility, I find that to be a kinda awkward distraction if anything and would prefer if I couldn't do that (I "mostly" just ran into the power up with guns blazing, not caring which one I got).

Bigger enemy ships explode into fruit, which you can collect for the sole sake of getting "points", but it's a little awkward.  They fall from the sky pretty quickly in an arc, usually surrounded by bullets and enemies that you can crash into.  Something that would be a little less troublesome if they just floated in place like the powerups.  At some point I just started ignoring the fruit because I found that annoying, but that's just me.

This is an alright shmup - and a fairly accessable one.  Although I didn't love it, its competent and enjoyable enough and gets the nod from me.
