Its a cute and cozy game. It has elements of city building, customizing and cute vibes in the form of the hidden tanuki in each level and cats when you unlock them :3

Right, so Minami Lane is this little Japanese street management thing where you slap down buildings and pretend you're some sort of urban planning genius. Groundbreaking stuff. You've got five whole levels -I know, try to contain your excitement- and your job is to build shops, keep everyone happy, and click on raccoons like some sort of demented whack-a-mole game. Oh, and cats. Because of course there are cats. It's the internet, innit?

The thing is, there's absolutely nothing at stake here. No pressure whatsoever. You could literally fall asleep on your keyboard and your little digital street would probably be fine. Even the achievements are piss-easy. they practically throw themselves at you. "Congratulations, you clicked on three things! Here's a trophy!" Brilliant.

it's actually quite lovely. The art's adorable -all these tiny people milling about your street like a Wes Anderson film directed by someone who's had a few too many herbal teas. The music's all tinkly and pleasant, the sort of thing your mum would have on during a Sunday roast. And the whole vibe is so relentlessly wholesome you'll feel like you've been force-fed puppies and rainbows.

Best bit? You can automate all the tedious rubbish -trash collection, surveys, all that admin bollocks- so you can focus on what really matters: petting digital cats. Because that's what gaming's come to, hasn't it? We've gone from saving princesses to stroking pixelated felines. What a time to be alive.

Bottom line: it's cheap, it's short, and it's about as threatening as a marshmallow. If you need something to decompress after repeatedly dying in whatever masochistic nightmare you're currently playing, Minami Lane's your game. Just don't expect to be challenged. At all. Ever.

All the adjectives you would expect to describe this game are true. Cute, cozy, comfy, and pleasant. What a joy and I can't wait to 100% this! I wish it was longer, but that is only because I love it so much and I am very happy to have played this game and grateful for the sandbox mode. Love it, pick it up, relax and enjoy.

the super cute art style really suits the simple game play loop and low-stakes natures of the game overall. the structure of the chapters, where each introduces new mechanics/shops/decor flows very well. while on the one hand i do think having two kinds of human residents works, it would've been nice to see more complexity in that area (maybe a middle-aged demographic, or particular groups like parents, students, etc) as well as making the shops themselves more robust so that several groups can be satisfied with products/recipes from a single establishment instead of having to create separate duplicate establishments for each demographic. the cat mechanic as a whole is actually very well implemented, both because all the cats are pettable and that to attract cats, your street has to have decor.

very few cons for me. one is that it takes so long to unlock auto-trash pick up and auto gathering reviews from shop patrons. for the trash in particular, it can sometimes be partially hidden/clips through some of the surrounding building or decor, or it spawns way across the lane and you don't have enough time to manually click on them before the day ends. another minor bug is that sometimes people following the same randomized path glitch and clip through each other.

however there's an entire separate sandbox mode that allows for custom challenge rounds, which helps the overall game feel well worth its price.

overall plays well on linux but there's a weird "wrong focus" bug that hits me every once in a while that softlocks the game (thankfully the game saves between levels), but switching from "fullscreen" to "windowed" helps; i've only encountered the bug once in windowed mode (and many times in fullscreen).  i would have ragequit without the save functionality, but it's a testament to how good the game is that i put up with a bunch of those softlocks before i figured out the trick.

Ya know? It's the kind of fun where a good run will give you a God complex, and a bad run will have you trying new stuff and finding out you have a "thing" for acid traps. Each run is fun, cartoonish, and satisfying! Wholeheartedly recommend trying it out, it's worth the price tag!

Emberward is a tower defense roguelike. Runs take about an half an hour, give or take, and there is plenty to unlock for meta progression.

The roguelike aspect is fun, there is a lot of cool synergies and towers to build around, so the game works well as a roguelike. There is also a good variety of enemies and things like status effects to play with. But I am burying the lede here, because while all the regular tower defense aspects are all good, the creativity and extra mechanics they add are fantastic. You maze the enemies with a hand of tetris like blocks, forcing you to do some creative placement and you can even directly interact with the hand of blocks for some extra tiricks. Many levels have really fun mechanics, like big flashy traps that you can activate or areas where enemies are buffed to avoid. And the boss fights are unique and memorable. Also, the towers themselves come in 1x1, 2x2, 3x3 configurations and even some wonkier combinations, like a row of cannons that are 1x5 with a large minimum range. There are a lot of cool toys to play with and they all come with their own restrictions and work arounds.

At the end of the day, this is probably the best tower defense game I have played. It combines well known tropes of the genre like status effects and mazing, but does it in unique and fun ways. Any tower defense fan needs to play this, and most people can probably find something here to enjoy.

The has to be among the best roguelike games of all time, possibly even being better that the likes of Risk of Rain 2. 

This game has four layers of strategic diversity in this game. 

The first layer is towers. Each and every tower is completely unique alongside 5 elements which serve different purposes. 
Normal: does nothing. Usually focused purely on damage 
Fire: can apply burn, some towers apply it more often at the cost of damage. Burn makes enemies take a percent of their health as damage every second, though this effect is reduced on boss enemies. 
Ice: most towers apply chill. Chill builds up on enemies on hit from certain ice towers, with it  slowing down enemies the higher the stack but decaying over time. Some towers or relics consume chill for extra effects or damage. 
Poison: the damage these towers apply occurs over time, with the accumulated poison damage that is yet to trigger being shown as a green portion on the enemy’s health bar. Some relics let it tick faster.
Electric: when hitting an enemy these towers accumulate charge based on the percent of hp the tower took away from the enemy in hit. Once the yellow bar gets full in the enemy the enemy becomes stunned. Some towers will apply more charge than others. Some relics allow you to stun them faster or deal more damage to stunned enemies.
Arcane: they do guaranteed critical hits for 50% more damage to enemies above 80% hp and some make enemies more likely to receive critical damage. 
No tower will feel like “this is just x tower but has y element”. As well as the tower diversity, each tower has two upgrades that can vastly change their function, even changing their element. 

The second layer is the path building. At the start of each run and after some stages you get Tetris blocks which you draw at random at the start of each round or by using coins. You can place them anywhere as long as they aren’t overlapping other Tetris blocks/obstacles or blocking the path to your flame. Unlike other tower defense games where you need to revolve the defense around the path, you revolve the path around your defense. It doesn’t end there, the blocks you get at the end of stages have runes on them, which buff the towers placed on them. 

The third layer are relics, which provide passive bonuses which can often change the function or interaction of elements. Some even allow for different elements to interact with eachother. Besides that, they can have a whole variety of effects and they will usually have some sort of benefit to you. There’s no “worst relic” since they all have their use, but some are more specific and niche than others. 

There’s last layer are characters, unlike other games where they may have slightly different stats, in this game each and every character has a unique set of passives and even starts out with certain relics. Here’s some good examples:
Tiny: he can only use blocks made of 3 or less tiles, but every block has a rune on it. He also has a relic that lets him draw an extra card after placing a block for three blocks every turn at the first of them immediately being locked in place, which sorta alleviates the problem of him not being able to build big mazes. So he can’t build the biggest maze but he can build lots of potent towers. 
Chunk: he starts with several blocks with Mega runes, which makes them twice as big. He’d be very annoying to use if he didn’t start with the relic, the Blessed Chisel, which lets makes his blocks shave themselves down to fit wherever they’re placed. This also lets him place blocks with runes close together with ease. This together makes chunk a very solid choice for when you need to build bigger mazes and or use longer ranged towers. 
Last but not least that I wanna mention is Tara the Witch: sometimes enemies drop chests that give you power up cards which act as a temporary boost to a single tower. Tara’s special ability makes it so these cards get drawn naturally alongside block cards and they work permanently (they can be removed by using an ability or overwritten) though all towers and their upgrades are more expensive. This may not seem that good until you realize certain interactions with certain towers and abilities result in even small 1x1 sized towers overshadowing the damage of bigger towers.

Due to all of the layers of strategy in this game it results in not only every run being unique but it also being stupidly satisfying when you get a setup that absolutely demolishes enemies or finding a good way to use a niche tower. 

A common trap of roguelikes is lacking enough layers to create strategic diversity or each layer itself not being diverse. A good example is Rogue Tower, another tower defense roguelike that has unique mechanics and towers but each upgrade essentially is just “tower does more to health/armor/shield” or “tower is able to apply x status effect” and results in every run just being another flavor of the same damage and each tier just being another way of applying damage. EmberWard manages to avoid this by having many layers and a lot of diversity between these layers. RoR2 even manages to fall into this trap with the builds that support different status effects or playstyles often being drowned out by other damage items.

EmberWard is the pinnacle of roguelike design and should serve as a role model for any developer trying to make an interesting and deep roguelike.

[b]Leaf it Alone[/b] is a really great indie entry in the casual cleanup genre. It was very zen and easy to [i]pick up,[/i] and a while into playing I actually found myself realizing that I hadn't been listening to anything else in the background (no YouTube videos or other distractions). I just relaxed and focused on picking up leaves and performing a few other related tasks, which was made especially easy by the ambient soundtrack and very pleasant visuals. The game also runs great. I shudder to think about how many leaves I piled up at some points; but, regardless, the experience remained buttery smooth. Before too long, I did manage to 100% this game - [spoiler]pro tip: you can go make a manual backup of your save if you want to go for different, conflicting achievements.[/spoiler] I could absolutely see myself revisiting this another time, especially if they add further content - not that they need to. It's simple, relaxing fun, and the game feels complete in my book. Still, if they do add more stuff, I'll be happy to revisit for that.

Ultimately, I highly recommend picking this one up, especially via one of the bundles as those games also deserve some love and its a great way to maximize savings. I had a great time with this title, and I think you will too. A great purchase in my book, and well worth it as such a great price!

Yes, this game is easy enough for kids. But that doesn't mean it's only for kids. It's also for very tired adults who don't want to fight anymore. The reward is the satisfaction of leading a lost bee home.

Snufkin: Melody of Moominvalley is a musical story game about wrecking parks.  Beat it in 4.4 hours using a keyboard and mouse (has full keyboard and game controller support, too).  Difficulty is easy.  Have not played dlc as of this review.  Good game for kids.

Story - You play as Snufkin, a musician who helps restore nature back to its rightful place.

Gameplay - Most of the mechanics are simple fetch quests and moving rocks/logs.  You also play an instrument to various animals to get them to do things for you.

Accessibility - The settings have an accessibility setting making text bigger, diff text for dyslexic, and more.

Kids - The game's steered towards kids.  And why not?  It's Moomin.  If you're an adult who's played games your entire life and have the reflex abilities of a super sonic jet in flight, then you might be underwhelmed.  As for kids, I recommend ages 7ish+ where they're past the bigger hurdle of learning right from wrong - just based on Snufkin wrecking other people's gardens and being praised for it.

Kid triggers - The game has spiders, snakes, lightning, and one mildly tense chase scene.  None of it struck me as too scary, what with everything being cartoony, but now you know.  

Music - It's ok.  Pretty chill background music.  

Lite Motion Sickness Warning - This is the lowest score of motionsickness, I wouldn't even call it 'motion sickness', but something about the background slightly blurring everything but Snufkin when you move around (especially when running) gave me a mild headache.  Focusing on Snufkin instead of the background helped a little.  Playing with the lights on in the room and taking breaks also helped alleviate it.  No ginger needed.



Do I recommend Snufkin: Melody of Moominvalley?  Sure.  If you know the Moomin series, you'll enjoy the crap out of this game.  If you don't and you're a kid, you'll still enjoy this game.  There's nothing complex here and everything (outside of maybe the butterflies, which made me check the community forum) is a fun playthrough.  :D

This game is nothing short of a masterpiece. A common complaint is that the game is too short, but I actually think it’s the perfect length. 

I am a longtime lover of everything Moomin and have a pretty extensive collection of Moomin collectables and books, so I feel qualified in saying that this game is an amazing preservation and retelling of Tove Jansson’s original stories in a way that is unique, but also maintains the original art style of the books, and that is something truly special. I really do believe Tove herself, if she were still alive today, would be proud of what Hyper Games has created.

The gameplay is calming and cozy, and it was the perfect game to play before bed on my Steam Deck during my Christmas Holiday. Really, this is just a great game if you love Moomin/know someone who loves Moomin and likes those types of games. Even if you know *nothing* about Moomin, I highly recommend this. It’s not very long, and takes less than 5 hours to complete the base game. Plus, Sigur Rós is on the soundtrack, so you really can’t go wrong. Very few games have ever made me cry at their endings, and this one made me bawl. That alone makes it my favorite game I’ve completed this year.

Thank you, Hyper Games. Here’s to the next Moomin game! I will be there Day 1 <3

Me and my gf (both adults) played this together, she controlled Snufkins movement and I did stuff like jump, play music and interact with objects.

It's obviously a game made for small children, there's almost next to no challenge at all. But that didn't matter to us: the art style is just too amazing. We marvelled at some of the places. 

My only complaint about this game is the music and the audio:
1. There are no voices. Huge disappointment, I feel like this game would become so much better if they had voices for dialogue.
2. The music during gameplay is extremely repetitive and lacks melody. Me and my gf actually often shut the music off and listen to 90's Moomin anime music instead that actually fit the game way better. The intro and outro-music feels like standard bittersweet Sigur Rós-music that by itself is beautiful but does not match the cozy atmosphere of Moomin.

[h1]Snufkin: Melody of Moominvalley[/h1] to nostalgiczna podróż do doliny Muminków. W grze wcielamy się w postać Włóczykija, który jak to na niego przystało wraca wraz z końcem zimy do doliny Muminków by raczyć mieszkańców doliny swoimi opowieściami oraz pomocą.  

Niestety po powrocie zastaje zdewastowaną dolinę, w której głównym problemem jest brak wody, co za tym idzie życie w dolinie zaczyna zamierać. Włóczykij nie daje za wygraną i wraz z przyjaciółmi postanawia przywrócić dawny blask doliny Muminków.    

Gra łączy w sobie elemeny eksploracji, skradania oraz rozwiązywania nieskomplikowanych zagadek logicznych. Głównymi narzędziami którymi w trakcie eksploracji posługuje się nasz bohater są jego instrumenty: harmonijka, flet oraz bębenek. Każdy z nich inaczej wpływa na otoczenie. 

Dużym atutem gry jest oprawa audiowizualna, która mocno nawiązuje do tej z serialu. Ręcznie rysowane, pastelowe grafiki są niezwykle klimatyczne co w połączeniu ze świetną ścieżką dźwiękową sprawia, że można się kompletnie zanurzyć w tym wspaniałym świecie.  

Sam świat gry nie jest duży, poruszamy się po małej mapie, na której znajdują się kluczowe miejsca. Cała gra to raptem kilka godzin zabawy. Szczerze mówiąc spodziewałem się nieco dłuższej zabawy.  

 Podsumowując, jest to świetna gra dla dzieci jak i starszych którzy poczują nostalgiczną nutkę z dzieciństwa. Sama gra mogła być nieco dłuższa. Zabrakło też tutaj tego budowania napięcia, grozy, które w swoich odcinkach tworzył serial. Szczególnie gdy pojawiała się Buka czy Czarownica, tych postaci też tutaj jak na lekarstwo.  Tak czy owak polecam ;)

the visuals are so beautiful especially hattifattener island... I ALSO REALLY ENJOYED THE GROKE CAMEO!!! I MISSED HER SO MUCH The games mechanics are very easy to understand and before i even opened it i bought most the dlc, I dont usually enjoy RPG games but this one is just REALLY nice. (and i grew up with moominvalley so theres also that!)  I CANT WAIT FOR WINTERS WARMTH!!!!!!
Great visuals. What I like about it is that it's not ridiculously hard, but still requires some thinking to navigate. Pretty rewarding once you get all the achievements too.

I loved it. Very cute and relaxing. The only slightly annoying thing was having to travel places through the whole forest multiple times (the map isn't super accurate). That's what makes the majority of playtime for me because I have 0 sense of direction. But would definitely play again and I'll be getting the next game that comes out.

I didn't think it was a short game but neither did it overstay it's welcome. There were a couple of things I missed but I still got a solid 4 hours playtime out of it.
 I was a little sceptical as to how much I would enjoy it because at times you did some puzzles and I was almost assuming I would get stuck at some point but I didn't get stuck for long.
Easy but doesn't feel cheap. Wonderful animation and every character feels real to the world. A great play!

A cute game, not very long, only about 3-4 hours, but cute and generally relaxing. The art is lovely and plot is gentle. A good pickup on sale if you like basic puzzle games. Would be excellent for children in the 8-13 range.

If you are a fan, this is a must play. If you don't know Moomins about this Finnish IP and love a cozy game, this is a delightful introduction. It's not complex and the game is not long, but the art and music create a cozy vibe focused on nature, community and whimsy. Nauti!
Absolutely adored this. It was so cutesy and the art style was so pretty. I never watched Moomin before, but I might after playing this bc I really liked Snufkin as a character.
beautiful znd so cozy, saving the forest and helping every creature is so wholesome happy and calming, easy going, chill, interesting and fun!
tbh this was a suprised but its fun plays well and the graphics are beautiful

Really kind of fun to have cute anime girls wandering around your desktop if you're gonna be writing or coding or scrolling, anything but actually playing a game where they'd get in the way. Otherwise, just a fun desktop accessory. Works really well, they stay put and their heads follow your mouse cursor. You can click on them and sometimes they do things, or say a greeting. I wasn't able to figure out how to get more than a point or two from clicking, so unsure how you'd ever purchase additional girl unlocks or clothes in the shop but those feature do exist if you're interesting in clicking 900+ times! Would be cool to see even more interactivity with the characters, I'll keep my eye on this game! Definitely try it if you've got a lot of monotonous work you do on your pc!

Edit: I think the more they like you the more they will slowly begin to spin around so that they're facing away.. ahaha, I can't wait to unlock the wooden mannequin and raise its affection till it turns around and shows off that wooden booty!!! :P

Very early access review, may update review later. This is my first physics-based automation game (need to try Hydroneer eventually...). Having (mostly) come off of a Satisfactory binge, I'll be using that game as a comparison.

Having made it about halfway through the research tree, this is a very enjoyable automation game so far. This is very much like a simpler, smaller scale Satisfactory. Where Satisfactory can get pretty complicated between the amount of combining objects to make new objects and using math to make sure your machines are using the resources available to them equally to ensure production doesn't stop due to over/underutilization, this is much simpler in that regard. There is much less combining in this game so far (only had any with steel production) so math is not nearly as important. While this sounds like it's missing that crucial ingredient that makes Satisfactory so satisfying, it makes up for it with being able to create multiple items from the same item and giving you quests towards different items (occasionally prioritizing an item you've already made much earlier), so you need to switch production occasionally. This leaves you with the option to just make 'everything' at once, but it's more time-efficient to switch production to what is necessary with branching paths and gates.

(I'll point out I'm playing the 'classic' map, there is also a bigger map) Having binged Satisfactory for about 100hrs, I appreciate how much smaller this map is. Don't get me wrong, it's great to have a huge map to explore... but after a while, the map size can get pretty tedious when you're spending 1-3 hrs bringing a new resource back to your base, whether it's by conveyor, car or railroad (I STILL need to unlock drones...). And heck, even with blueprints I'll typically need to spend AT LEAST 30 minutes setting up a new production line. The MUCH smaller building size in this game (the biggest building I've unlocked so far is about 9 conveyor belts in a square) and much smaller scale makes setting up a new line MUCH quicker. It also makes it much easier to tear down and rebuild if you want to try something different.

These two factors, combined with the item physics, makes for a satisfying loop of creating lines, combining lines, sorting items, and making gates so you can change production without rearranging lines. So even though you lose  a little of that 'smart guy' feeling when you perfectly balance production like in Satisfactory, It's still really satisfying seeing how much you can fit on a conveyor belt before it overflows and adjusting your lines when they do. Heck, it's honestly pretty satisfying when everything breaks and there's crap everywhere. It's fun to look at and super easy to clean up thanks to the magnet tool. 

In conclusion, if you love Satisfactory but hate how much time it eats up, this may be the perfect game for you. Same goes for if you want something a bit simpler, or if physics-based automation just sounds cool. I can easily recommend this game to automation aficionados, even in as early access as this is. It's clearly still a work in progress (with some items literally labeled with WIP), but the bones here are strong. I can't wait to see what's in store for this game.
I have been pretty busy lately and lately my attention span for games has only lasted an hour at best. This game is the first in a while that had me hooked and losing track of time.

Factorio and Satisfactory had a baby and shes STUNNING. I’m genuinely obsessed with every part of this game -from the collision physics to uncovering clever little hidden paths, you can feel the passion poured into every detail. Nothing feels lazy, nothing feels accidental. The only thing I can nitpick so far is that conveyor belts can be a bit awkward to place at certain angles, but that’s honestly minor compared to how good everything else feels. This game is incredible, and I can’t wait to see how far it goes.

On the face of it MineMogul is just another conveyor belt game, and the conveyors themselves...they're fairly basic. There isn't really any new ground tread here.

So why should you buy this game over other factory games?

Space/setting - its confined, and very little of the games factory elements help you out with the cramped conditions. Usually in a massive open factory game such as satisfactory you occassionally box yourself in and need to create an exceptionally creative spagetti conveyor system in order to keep production going. This part of any factory game is arguably the most satisfying and human part of it. And Minemogul forces you into these problem solving situations right from the beginning.

The ore/materials - the materials on the conveyors arnt just representations of products - they exist. They interact with the world. You can't ignore a problem like in other games because a bottleneck literally results in a slowly increasing pile of stuff. And its a race against time if you break a section of a factory up to make it better, as the materials will be piling up where you broke the factory. Once you've resolved a situation you then need to safely add the excess materials back into the supply chain without clogging up the factory again.

Solutions to problems - I think the accidental genius of this game is in the fact that because the game relies on the random movements of objects on the conveyor belts, the fact that factory components dont perfectly mathmatically line up, the limited space, the constant game updates which change solutions up and the exponential nature of the cause and effect of the choices you make; solutions that would work perfectly in other games dont work here, there are no perfect solutions to your problems. Simply said: YouTubers will struggle to ruin this game by posting perfect setups. A good example of this would be that I've been trying to find a reliable way of balancing belts, (the splitters are mostly random, the supply of ore comes randomly so bottlenecks can appear sporadically), there are no tutorials on Youtube for this and once I started experimenting I saw why... its incredibly hard to balance belts... and I'm happy about that, it means there's currently no foolproof solution. And the few videos I've seen of any attempts to make a sort of blueprint dont fit into my factory, and thats a great thing, because if I wanted to try to incorporate someones idea i'd have to reverse engineer it and change it to fit my space and factory.

One of the best factory games on the market, I just hope the updates keep what makes this game special alive.
This game has ruined me for any other mine/conveyor game. Absolute masterclass in optimisation. Can't recommend enough

I have played a lot of these factory games

This is a great one, probably one of the best I've played

HOWEVER

it does have a lot of things holding it back.

Conveyers are very inconsistent size wise and can lead to frustrations in placement and upgrading existing lines.

even with the biggest cave size, there is very little room to do much of anything.

Progression feels very unbalanced 

It becomes very bothersome to fix routing issues and redesign large areas of the factory late game

A pleasant mix of hydroneer and techtonica gameplay wise. From a developer's perspective, I would have to recommend several sites each with exclusive unlocks to give a start fresh feel each time. Most players get overwhelmed by the complexity of their own creation and stop playing, I've felt that starting over but not starting anew is a good fix for that, while dedicated players will make nightmare fuel of each stage as per normal.
I hate to compare this to something else, but this game is everything Hydroneer wanted to be and more. For folks who like a solid progression and rewarding gameplay loop, this is it. Cheers.

This game has the exact core mechanics of any automation + mining game that I have awalys wanted. I can not wait for the new update and am really excited to see where this game goes. I also love the compact space that you have to work around in, it makes the production lines feel messy but also rewarding when solved.

For €14.79, I haven’t had this much fun with a game in a long time. Even though it doesn’t have tons of features yet, it’s already super addictive. My first run took about 58 hours, and unlocking new objects while constantly optimizing production chains feels really rewarding. I’m definitely looking forward to future updates!

Small suggestions:

One-click replacement of conveyors like in Satisfactory would be great (e.g., Conveyor → Roller/Walled).

A 1x1 Roller Conveyor Corner is missing.

Sorter Conveyors / Roller Sorters sometimes clog because some items don’t pass through correctly.

Overall, I’m very happy with the game and wish the dev team continued success!
I so love the building aspect. The new update has made it so much bigger for more builds and it lags less now. It's just a really fun game overall

Even in this early in development, the physics is some of the best I've ever seen. Games like Factorio, Satisfactory, even the Contraptions DLC of Fallout 4 have been fun, but this game is a whole different level of satisfying both to play and just to watch it go. I've longed for a game like this for YEARS.

I've just completed my first run through and it's great! Very enjoyable factory/automation game but with the confined space of a cave makes this game very rewarding to just stand and watch all your products drop into the selling bin. the only reason I'm not starting a new game is because of the imminent update that's about to drop which is stated to provide double the gameplay.

I highly recommend this game. especially if you like games like satisfactory. This feels a little more accessable and fun with a surprisingly complex mechanic but wrapped up in a very easy to learn way.

The game has potential but right now there is not much worth doing. I have basically done all the game really needs to have done within 4 hours to consider it done at this stage. Considering this is an early access game, it has a good framework that they can expand so I am leaving a positive review but I don't think its worth buying at this time.

Very fun game. The only downside I had is when everything was running fill force my FPS would drop from 260 to 25. The more pieces on belts, the worse the FPS would get. I was running 3 FPS trying to produce enough product for the last quest.

No, I don't want to make 1 thing at a time. I WANNA MAKE IT ALL AT ONCE! :)

Physic Based production lines come with frustrations but sure is fun, i like the map style and and whether into maths, sheer volume of just relaxed entertainment this is a very good production style game

Yes!

Was looking for a game like Parcel Simulator. Chill, fun automation, not as tedious or boring as Satisfactory. This really hits the spot!


Bravo developer! Keep those updates coming!

What I like:
The controls are intuitive and simple.
It's easy to tear down your build to make a more efficient one.
The game saves your ore in your sorting conveyor after you save and quit.
The grid for placing buildings and conveyors is typically very smooth.
I LOVE the Demo feature. If there weren't a Demo for me to play, I wouldn't have known this game was for me and wouldn't have bought it.
                  What I think could use improvement:
I would love for the Merging Conveyor to replace the T-Junction spot in the Research Tree. It just seems to me like the T-Junction is useless.
I would love to see an 'advanced sorter' which allowed you to set filters via a screen, not by dropping ores into the basket - Depending on how the sorter is set up, it can be a PITA to actually get the ore / gem to put into the basket.
And also phillip

This game literally got me out of a depressive low. I bought a ton of games in the past months only to open them once for half an hour and close them but this is different. I sat down yesterday and actually focused and enjoyed myself for 2 hours. I’m actually looking forward to playing it today too!!!! It doesn’t sound like much but it’s a massive compliment to the game developers for being able to keep my attention for that long! Also the sound design scratches my brain in all the right ways. I hope they add new maps and stuff and possibly a rail system for long distance transport of ore.
Pretty fun. Needs multiplayer and hopefully more content in the future. Hope the dev(s) don't drop this project, has a lot of potential.
Really fun game, it makes you think and you have to constantly be on your toes cause if not, you have a mess of ores on your hands. I highly reccomend it!

I don't agree with not recommending a game because it has a low amount of content. This game is extremely fun and I would say worth your money, but it is however a little undercooked.

After 9 hours I had practically finished the game, automated all the finished products and all that was left is some insanely grindy acheivements to complete. The game is increadibly satisfying though, slowly creating your empire of production in an underground mine. Money could be balanced a lot better as it is practically non-exsistent and I was never able to not buy whatever I wanted after only an hour of playtime.

Still an extremely fun game for the 10-ish hours you will get out of it and the price point of the game is perfect.
I'm in love with the game, super excited for the future
this the most addicted i've been to a mining game in years. i spent 7 hours straight in one day just so i can figure out how to make my shaft as effecient as possible. 10/10 for me
Still early access, short but a lot of fun. Loved it and I can't wait for the future updates

This game is brimming with potential and is already tons of fun in early access. Dealing with a physical jam in the production line is a blast. 

Wishlist for things to come:
1) Increasingly complex building chains and more resources. If I were a gambling man I think steel would be next on the menu.

2) Better inventory management and ability to store/delete building items. There are a ton of items to build with but the inventory is getting clogged up. 

3) A bigger 'sandbox' map would be awesome. The current map is really well thought out though - feels like a puzzle trying to get all the production lines to fit. 

4) I question a few minor things like polishing persisting through multiple processing steps and the redundant expendable mining bits. Some aspects could be streamlined

Not sure why people are saying this is better than hydroneer. At the current state, it's fun and has satisfying automation. Has a great inventory system by comparison. However, the lack of real purpose is where it falls in comparison to hydroneer. If you're only doing the quests, once you have your automation set up, there really isnt much else to do. The quests will eventually complete themselves. That said, well worth the price and has the makings of a top quality game. Hydroneer offers more things to do that require your input with the order system.

This game definitely has the makings of something great, and since it is in early access, I'm sure more content will follow. It's worth picking up if you are a fan of these automation games.

The game is like Satisfactory where you build factories for different materials and sort them, I try to make everything like in Satisfactory it takes time and the game is still in the works. The game as lots of protentional.

The game looks promising for the future, but I wish all the features shown in the trailer were already in the game. I also hope for more challenges, as beta testers have already completed them all. I played for eight hours straight without stopping and thoroughly enjoyed building my small factory. I really like that the game is without music, and I hope no music is added in the future. The mining atmosphere is great with its sounds alone. Finally, I think the new elevator in the copper-rich area should be opened; there is one, but it's locked.

Completely unique factory game. I love the genre and this is a whole different way to enjoy it. I'll admit it took me a little while with the demo to get the hang of it and realize how cool it is, but I'm so glad I gave it a chance. Instead of neatly arranged sprites representing ore on conveyor belts, you have actual rocks that you have to manage. (I love the sound they make when you drop a bunch.) The physics part is FUN and super challenging to say the least. I really have no complaints except that I'm almost done and I don't want to finish! I believe they're still in early access, and they've been hyping a bigger map. Can't wait! If you love factory games and resource management and want something different, I highly recommend you check out the demo. If you like that, you'll love the game, because it's even better.

Not really a fan of Factorio style games, but this one hits different.
its has minecraft like vibes but also its has 100% its own style that i just enjoy.
Started with the Demo, played, lost track of time untill the wife said good morning. 
Bought the game the next day.

Effectively completed all game content in 8 hours; would like to see more content added but I can see what the developer is cooking and the physics-based tycoon stuff is fun. 

Play this if you're wanting to goof around for a few days, it's enjoyable, but it'll leave you longing for more content.

Oh this game is good. The physics is insane!
Highly recommend!!

Simple to understand and learn, even for players who don't usually mess with infrastructure management games. The tech tree is simple enough, and there isn't much left to the imagination, save for how you want your output to be processed through.

Some objects prefer transfer via conveyor, and some are fine on the belts, but the belts can basically get you through most, if not all of the game. It's still early access, though, so be aware that you're not buying a finished game.

for an automation and base building game, i really appreciate its use of full physics for objects no matter where they are in the world, helps sell you the immersion of being in a mine and tending to an always-working machine (though that sometimes can be an annoyance, obviously). obviously its in its rough stages for now but i have faith in the team that it will improve with time and await the full 1.0 release!

I CANNOT RECOMMEND IT ENOUGH!!!!! THIS GAME IS SO MUCH FUN

I really wanna see how the devs continue to build this game, i cant put into words how much i wanna see this game grow into a massive factory simulator

I was pleasantly surprised by this game. It's a relaxing factory game that challenges your ability to spaghettify conveyor belts in inventive ways. Looking forward to future updates. Maybe a set of collectibles spread across the world. Interactive NPCs. More maps with unique challenges.

Fun & Addictive. Very Addictive...

Started over 5 times on a short period just to get better. And you will get better once you get the hang of it and progress in the research tree. Almost finished the game, can't wait for more expansion and extra stuff. Worth every cent.
Amusing game, cant wait for updates and expansions.  Worth the first playthrough.
I use a gaming laptop for this game, and it doesnt lag that much even if there is over 500 objects at once! this is why i love this game, Im looking up to new updates for this MINEtastic game
Never really got into Satisfactory, but I like this one. A nice and relaxing game

Great game, I did every quest upto the last 8 mogul quests and stopped there because it would have been a long waiting game to get the thousands of items needed only beacause I didn't want to fully dismantle and rebuild my factory for the third time lol.

Make sure to unlock sorter lids asap I ruined my production line multiple times using the magnet tool on the wrong settings and always keep an eye on the entity counter on the pause menu so you don't have to cleanup 2000 items when the game shuts your miners down to prevent lag.

I recommend playing on the big cave for your first run because there isn't much room to work with on the regular map. There wasn't alot of room to make larger scale production lines and I wished I had chosen the larger map for more freedom. All the items work great even the wip items only thing it needs is 1x1 roller corners as the roller convayors and normal convayers dont work well together, which is to be expected.

Can't wait for future updates.
very good game, but because its still in early development u need to have a beefy computer to actually enjoy many machines

Genuinely a great factory game. Originally tried out the demo with some enjoyement, but had many issues with the game. The full game fixed every issue I had with the demo and offered a substantial amount of new content. Props to the devs for that, especially with their consistent, good updates (they listen to the community). 

Obviously early access, so some aspects of the game need improvements. Such as the conveyors. My biggest problem with them is the diversity, there is 3 types of conveyors that aren't compatible with each other, alongside buildings that may not use the same conveyor inputs/outputs, forcing you to use a certain type. This overall causes annoying work arounds and glitches. A good fix is to maybe blend each conveyor type when connected to eachother? (I also may be missing something since I originally thought building was limited until I learnt I could press Q to scroll through multiple wall/floor types)

Another thing I personally hate in regards to conveyors, is how you insert items into buildings. Most buildings require you to insert resources at the top and output it at the bottom. This is annoying because you're having to contantly find a way up with the conveyors, which limits your room a ton. A nice feature may be a 1x1 vertical conveyor/elevator, that way transporting items up doesn't require making a long ass squiggly slope. 

Last complaint is the inventory, holy ♥♥♥♥ why can't I delete items? I'm stuck trash piling a ♥♥♥♥ load of items to make room for more. Let me atleast delete or store it (seems to be in the works though) 

Overall I found it to be both fun and challenging, which I like. Buy it if u like factory games and are too impatient to wait for full release.

A fun enjoyable game that keeps you going. The final trophys are a bit of a grind but its fun to work it all out and try to find an optimal setup. In the end I just left my computer runing overnight to get the last of the cash.

A relaxed, grind-focused game that lets you create the most ridiculous things you can imagine. Nonetheless, the time spent was well worth it and thoroughly enjoyable. 
I’m really looking forward to future updates and new features so I can come back and expand my mine even further!

Game has been fantastic. Tickles that itch to build and control flows of materials. 
 Things I'd like to see : 
1: Magnet / Pull device set for specific resources or items.
2: Maybe an aerial drone to place or locate conveyors etc? (end game most likely)

Keep up the great work Devs! Love it!
Fun little game, but honestly you progress through it very fast.

Excellent baseline for a fantastic game.

I'm really picky about factory games, but this one does it right.

Looking forward to how it looks in a few years!

♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ do I love this game the game still has a couple of quality of life changes it couple make, like the snap lock for converter is a little jank, But it never makes the game any less fun. I will keep playing this game for as long as there are updates.

i love this game. i need a brightness option though as my monitors brightness is not enough.
Played the demo for 10 mins and immediately bought the game. Love it and can't wait to see where it goes in the future.

by the way 100% reccomended!
Really enjoying this game, it works incredibly well. Had no bugs, glitches or anything so far. Very impressed highly recommend!

After completing the game (getting the last trophy available) with just over 99 hours into the game I will say. It was worth the 15 bucks and I can not wait until the next update. Thanks for the great game!

This is an addictive mining game! The only thing you have to worry about is building up your factory the way you want it to make money and complete the quests. Then you can rearrange, delete machines, and do whatever you see fit. The physics mechanic is lovely and makes the game more satisfying. I feel anyone that like the mining genre can get deep into this game easy! Dev also are working on this game because of early access but they are doing updates and fixes often! Thanks for the great game!

Spaghetti heaven.

I really enjoy the vibe of Mine Mogul. It's definitely casual and makes for some great spaghetti.

Take it easy, slowly increment and plan your factory to make more money. Overcome the challenge of _too many resources_ at times.

If you like factory games, definitely check it out.

Overall, this has been a great game to jump into, but then let it run in the background. There can be grindy parts toward the end, but I've almost completed the game twice over as it is. And honestly the addition of an entirely different map (Age of Steel Update), was a happy surprise and I've been enjoying exploring it as I did the previous one (which is still available).

I Prefer This .

There's just enough construction to be satisfying, enough transportation to go idle with, and enough discovery to keep things new when you go spelunking. 

I know people won't like the reference, but its almost Satisfactory Lite or Introductory. and I haven't stopped yet.

100 hours in the game.

The good:
From someone who has 3000+ hours in satisfactory, this game is really good. The environemnt is cool, belting, machinery, puzzle/building solving... it's all pretty good.
The physicallity of it offers a fresh air to the "staticness" of games like Satisfactory or Dyson Sphere Program. I really liked it.

The bad:
2000 items limit. I hope devs can get past this tech limit.

Grab system. This "fish hook and line" grab system is bad... Looks cool... but it's bad. I spent 10 min laying the trophies on a table. Something that I could've done in 30 seconds in a game like Skyrim.
I wanted to organize all the boxes (belts, machines, buildings, etc) in a neat way, but I can't with this system... it's all just laying on the floor. Please devs, remove this system and replace it with a "static" grab, with rotational options.

The inventory. It has it's ups... but mostly downs. There are so many items to use, and they keep changing places when I move them. Please devs, move to a more static inventory. At least this way I know that my walled belt is always in that exact slot... and I don't have to spend 30 seconds looking all over the slots to find it. (NOTE: Satisfactory offers alt quick inventories, as in, hold ALT + mouse wheel, and you can have up to 10 quick inventory bars... you should use that exact same mechanic... too many items... not enough slots for all of them)

End-game. I mean... 100 hours in the game. On this new map, I spent probably about 60. 30 for building... 30 just watching it play while I do something else.
It's inconsistent. I spent 30-40 hours building and tweaking the factory, Only for the last quests to tell me that I need only half the machines I built, and basically leaves me with eihter the option of re-doing everything I spent the last 40 hours building, or leave it, and wait 30 hours of nothing but watching to get the last quest lines done.
Please devs... RETHINK your end game.
It should be about expanding and scaling your factory... not destroying or re-doing everything over and over.

Other than that... it's a pretty good game. If you like factory games, you should give this one a try.

I have 45 hours in this game (~10 of it AFK) with even more in the demo. I'm a certified MineMogul for 0.2. I love the gameplay loop of designing a complex ore development loop, then getting it to the product elevator. It is engaging and fun. HOWEVER, this game is clearly in early access and it certainly shows. That along with the $15 price tag leads me to just slightly recommend it, if only barely. This is in the hope that the game improves with further updates.

Recommendations for newbies:
- Money is rarely an issue. Just get stuff into the product elevator.
- Use the research tokens and prioritize: all conveyer upgrades, hoppers, rapid auto-miners
- Look ahead on the quests and save products that are required for later quests.
- The slanted conveyers can go downwards using Q. You DON'T need to just have stuff fall through the air. I didn't figure this out until ~20 hours in.
- Casting furnace ONLY needs coal if making steel. It works just fine for all other ingots/gears.
- Prioritize unlocking new areas (big map). This allows for more auto-miners, more space, new ores, and nice shortcuts back to the product elevator.

Recommendations for the Devs:
- Sometimes player gravity is massive, bringing me to ground level almost instantly. I notice this when I have a buildable selected from the hotbar but it could be something else.
- Keep the small inconsistencies between rollers and conveyers. It's part of the game to figure out which splitter/curve/slope is best and have physics object flying through the air and piling up when you don't get it right.
- Implement a "trash" button/tool (possibly an upgrade to the magnet). When clearing out a blockage at one of the filters, it'd be way easier to just delete the objects than babysit the junction with the filter. It often isn't an issue of sorter throughput not being enough, it's an issue of the items just not getting to the "mouth" of the sorter (see below).
- Implement a conveyer sorter with a wider mouth. Plates and pipes almost always got stuck in the conveyer sorter and the rollers were too finicky with ores. This upgrade should be quite late game, but would be a god-send.
- Implement a "Grab Physics" range extender tool/upgrade. If I'm standing on a walled conveyer, I should be able to grab what is rolling by me without having to lay down on the conveyer belt.

Very much a Satisfactory but with physics.

I spent 16~ hours playing this game, both sessions taking me to 7:30am wanting more.

The physics work well and things definitely have unexpected reactions sometimes.

Unfortunately the current issue is the long term game play isnt in the game yet. it definitely needs more time to cook and more added in, but with the consistent updates im excited to wait for them.

My biggest complaint about this game is there isnt enough and i basically beat it solo in 20~ (working on the "Make 400 of this item" quests). second biggest is a way to fly or grappling hook, i like verticality

I would recommend this game to anyone who likes satisfactory or factorio
Very good game love it very fun in progression but does take awhile to progress and I enjoy the expanding your factory

As-is is a fine automation game with plenty to do, will only be better with more content. It's an interesting twist compared to most automation games. In most other games you shove everything down the line and not worry about it, where in MineMogul you need to manage your consumption and throughput of goods to prevent jams. Some of the videos make the game look chaotic, but the physics is smooth and predictable, making it easy to solve problems.
What can I say? It's a fun game. The dev is active. It's exactly what it looks like. There's a free demo.

I'll need to go back to review Kyle Thompson's previous games: Sheepo and Islets. The trend I noticed going to Sheepo was increased scope, more expansive world, and a refinement of the arts Tyler. In other words, nothing but positives. Crypt Custodian continues down the same path.

The perspective shifts in CC to an isometric view. Originally, I was skeptical, but upon completion I was truly enjoying viewing the world from that perspective. It helps that the environments are varied and well detailed. I've been a sucker for Kyle's art style since Sheepo so that's no surprise. The combat is fun! It's nothing too complex compared to a titans of the industry like Hollow Knight or Silksong, but nevertheless it's enjoyable. There's a good amount of progression both in terms of new abilities after defeating bosses and increasing the number of slots you have available for this game's version of charms. There's also a solid amount of options for your special attack that I found myself changing every so often. Speaking of the bosses, they were often well designed and challenging enough to need a couple of attempts. Nothing too crazy, you won't be spending dozens of attempts to beat any of the bosses.

I didn't play much of the post-game content due to getting distracted by another game so I can't speak to that. The core game and story are very good and this would be a solid choice for any fan of metroidvanias to pick up and play over a weekend. Very excited to check out Well Dweller when that releases!

The protag reminds me of my old black cat who met the same fate. This game almost gives me some sort of closure for her, it makes me happy to think that maybe she's out there having a good time killing pests in the afterlife.

Crypt Custodian went straight into my Top 10 Metroidvanias of all time. The combat feels so snappy, the exploration is super rewarding, and honestly, all the mechanics are just top-notch and polished.

I know some people might say it’s not hard enough if they’re looking for a challenge, but I actually really loved the overall pace of the game. It’s just a great time from start to finish without being frustrating. If you’re a fan of the genre, you definitely shouldn’t sleep on this one!

Played this after being disappointed with Prime 4. I was pleasantly surprised with this game, its pretty fun. The combat is a bit simple, but it makes up for it by having a ton of different abilities and upgrades you can equip for passive and some active effects.

This game is also about normal metroidvania length, ~8 hours. There seems to be a postgame, but I haven't had time to do it yet, I assume it involves 100%ing the game though.

All in all, great game, glad I took the time to play it after finding it on a whim
The story was very well done, and I really enjoyed the backstory that each side character had. Overall, this game was very fun to play!
After a long time i've come across a really enjoyable metroidvania game. I really enjoyed the story.

A kyle thompson classic, what more is there to say?

I don't love the curse stones, and there's something about the map that rubs me the wrong way that I can't quite put my finger on, but it's a good game.
fun and simple, boss rush is a bit annoying for the 100% just because you have to do all the difficulties

A fun Metroidvania with a top down view, with a simple but moving story about a cat who went to the afterlife. Gameplay is decently fast-paced, pretty graphics, a good variety of enemies, bosses and some "puzzles" as well as upgrades, skills and movement abilities. Overall the game is quite easy, which is probably what it was going for. 

This is a game that was made with love, and you can tell. It might not be the greatest game of all time, but it's definitely an enjoyable one.

Absolutely LOVED this game, was the perfect balence of difficulty and story telling
It never felt too difficult to progress while keeping it interesting and challenging . The puzzles were fun and the characters stories were so charming and deep
The map seemed daunting at the start but was very easy to naviagte and explore without feeling overwhelmed, the upgrades were useful and let the player really build their play style
Would love a sequel or DLC for this game, 100% completed the main game in 12 hours
Great game, Kyle Thompson makes bangers

I'd say this is a great Metroidvania and a pretty simple one. It's easy to understand, and easy to pick up and play. No tedious mechanics, just good gameplay and some interesting mechanics and abilities. Liking my playthrough so far for sure. Would recommend if it looks appealing, just don't go in with Nine Sols expectations.
Cute chill game with a satisfying map exploration and money collecting system, a little short but there is replayablity and speed run potential. 8.5/10

One of the most pleasant metroidvanias out there. It really works well on every level, from exploration, to environmental puzzles, to platforming, to bullet hell boss combat. Plus it's got the developer's signature wit and atmosphere to boot. And do I detect a bit of tunic influence there at the end?

if I was to give one criticism I'd say movement speed is a tad slow. I spent most of my time spamming dash. Islets has this great momentum that made traversal feel like a speedrun. this game could benefit from some of that.
One of my favorite indie developers is back with this one. Great vibes, fun as hell bullet hell combat, and great exploration.

What can I say? Crypt Custodian, like its predecessor, Islets, is a cozy metroidvania with a cute art style that never gets old. The first game I didn't enjoy as much, as it had pretty lame boss mechanics and doesn't really click until the mid game onwards. Crypt Custodian however grabs you from the get go, and doesn't drop a single beat until you're done with the game.

[b]Story[/b]

Crypt Custodian is a story about dead animals and a large part of the lore behind it is finding out how each companion died, and how they were when they were still alive. There's really not much to it; you collect photos, each with a single line of caption, and each companion has 3 photos. That said, every companion has a ton of personality that wouldn't be out of place in any children's book. Heck, playing the game feels like being read a bedtime story, really.

[b]Progression & abilities[/b]

Honestly, as a metroidvania, there isn't much depth to it. Abilities you unlock throughout the game are functional, but not mind-blowing. There's nothing revolutionary here, and I'm fine with it. There's only 1 or 2 of them that will be used constantly in combat; the rest are purely for traversal. All in all, nothing much to see here, it's mediocre in a good way. Like a shy kid that you can rely on as a friend.

[b]Combat & bosses[/b]

Bosses are much more interesting this time around. They still rely on being bullet-hell bosses that tests your pattern recognition more than reflexes, but that's just how the dev likes it. Don't expect Megaman or Hollow Knight levels of brawling and positioning here; most fights are purely about evading bullets while finding openings to strike at the boss.

That said, if you're a bullet-hell veteran, this game will be EASY as heck. For the rest of us, it's a decent amount of challenge. I have to give props to the dev for a few fights that are actually quite creative, like the biome that swaps tangibility on dashing, or the boss on a roller coaster track.

[b]Exploration[/b]

The game world is fun to explore. Each biome is distinct and actually memorable, although some of them are too large for their own good. Backtracking is done quite well; not only you have free fast travel from the get go, most of the time you unlock plenty of shortcuts that makes traversing biomes to hunt down collectibles a joy. This is often an area in metroidvanias that amateur devs drop the ball in, so it's good to see this dev (who is a veteran in this genre) nail it so well.

[b]Conclusion[/b]

I love it. This will be the game I recommend to people if they're looking for some light hearted, moderately challenging metroidvania that isn't pretentious nor self-indulgent. The game respects your time, and keeps things light and fun.

Very fun game.  

The exploration is great, there are lots of different routes you can explore the world in and get your upgrades in different orders which is definitely a highlight, a lot of other metroidvanias give the appearance of being open but are pretty limited in the order so I definitely appreciate this game being so open.  Looked like there was even a randomizer mode unlock after beating the game but I didn't try that out so can't say how well it works.

Combat was pretty fun, it's not super difficult even on hard mode however some bosses can provide a bit of a challenge, was fun enough that I went for the boss challenge achievements (limited gear, can't get hit, on a timer).  Game isn't super punishing, dying just sends you back to a checkpoint but you don't lose any progress which I appreciate.

Music is pretty good.  Art looks good.  Dialogue is fun and cute, story is limited but decent (mix of funny and depressing).

Overall very enjoyable experience, easy recommendation.

Crypt custodian is a hidden gem, graphics are great, game design is flawless to me, music is peak, controls are smooth.
Only drawbacks to me are the noise that makes npcs but that can be opted-out in the menu which is amazing AND the difficulty a bit too easy even set to hard.
The fact that Kyle did everything himself except the music blow my mind away.
Waiting to discover Well Dweller.
Although short and repetitive, as an ambience and atmospheric game glazer, i find this game to be enjoyable, relaxing with a hint of sweetness from the friendships made.

Fantastically Fun game, purrfect for those fond of our feline friends. Difficultly isn't bad on easy or even normal but hard is indeed hard. Boss Rush mode isn't bad either minus the achievement for getting S+ on all bosses on all difficulties
Loved this game! So cute, gameplay was smooth, bosses/enemies were fair in difficulty. Def recommend.

this game rocks! so many nice optional features for a non gamer such as myself, easy mode, minimap, and more. The game really feels like it wants you to play it, like it is meant for a fun hour or two after work or with your kids. It has its harder moments, the boss fights are fun and intuitive. For me a pro seems that its relatively short, like a 20hr experience, it says I am 40% complete as of right now. 
This is a great game, I will be checking out the Devs other stuff
 It is super cute, fun, and I would recommend it completely to anyone who likes the look of the main character.

Cute, unserious and at the same time a little tragic metroidvania about animals. (I almost cried at the credits because it resonated with me way too much). Had a lot of fun. The abilities were also a tiny bit more unique.

Having reached 100% game completion on Hard, the game was relatively challenging, but nothing crazy difficult. In the post-credits there is a section where you can find out how many times you died. I apparently did 117 times. Most of my deaths were due to silly mistakes and just doing some of the boss rushes. At first it might seem hard because there is no way to heal yourself outside of using save points, but it's actually pretty easy to dodge enemies and often times you can combo them before they do anything.

Now, onto some minor issues:
-It seems the author made some changes to the world after creating a map, so a few locations look very different on minimap in comparison to what they are really like.
-The last achievements requires you to complete Boss Rush in every difficulty, which is completely unnecessary considering it already requires you to do the hardest one.
-After completeing 3/4 curse challenges, they just get tedious and boring, nothing more.
-For some reason (MINOR SPOILER) Ice Witch boss auto positions you right in front of her face making you stand in the projectiles... Not sure who thought that's a good idea, but it's making me rage so much.

Absolutely awesome game !! I love the style ! Pleaase keep doing games such this one. The story is quite impressive too: sad, sweet and funny. It's a perfect fit. I can definitely recommend this game !
Creative and fun game, doesn't feel stale and respects players by not making us cross long distances to backtrack

A fun 3D platformer that feels like a combination of Hollow Knight and the older overhead Legend of Zelda titles. The writing, music, and level layouts are super well done. The puzzles + boss battles are just challenging enough that they inspire you to improve rather than frustrate you. Plus, you're playing as a black cat. That's always a good thing. 

For $20, you're getting roughly 12-20 hours worth of gameplay from beginning to end. There are quite a few secrets and hidden items to hunt for, hence the large range. At least a few areas/bosses will likely require multiple attempts, too. Speaking of bosses, there is also a boss rush mode with additional challenges and rewards. 

Recommended.

Absolutely stunning, and the fact that (for the most part), it's created by a single dev* only adds to the charm. Highly recommended if you're into metroidvanias!

* also, props to his brother for the music, it's perfect, and just as important as everything else!

This game really exceeded my expectations by quite a bit. I really didnt expect this game to reel me in as much as it did, and I am super glad that it did. It has a bunch of mechanics that feel smooth and simple to learn, while not being too easy or boring. Art is super well done, with the visuals being super attractive, and also the music and sound design really solidifying everything. There are some things I wouldve liked to see more of, like some abilities that are only really used a few times, or some certain charms that feel very situational, but it isnt too much of an issue to me. I feel this game has a lot to share to people, especially those looking for a more calmer and simplified metroidvania, without being too similar to most games out there of the genre. And the story itself, especially the ending, is very bittersweet and can be somewhat emotionally impactful to people who have experienced death before, as it approaches the topic very gently, making it easy for those to digest the game. I love this game very much, it hit close to home for me, and I really hope this game gets more recognition for the amazing game that it is. PLEASE BUY THIS GAME!!!!!

The whole game was very calming and a little bit sad, but also very satisfying. Thank you for the accessibility options - they’re very player-friendly.

The only thing in the game that made me completely mad was the extremely annoying fly-catching mechanic in the final part of the endgame. I needed to get the last photo of Kendra, and I had to try catching those ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ flies over and over again. There’s no way to restart - only to leave the area. The event should reset if the player fails even once.

I got this as a break from Silksong but it really makes me appreciate a lot of things like not dropping my stuff when I die and not having to run all the way to the boss room. Crypt Custodian can be quite challenging still as there are no health upgrades or healing mechanics. The world is very expansive. Every time I feel like I've seen it all, there's a whole new area to explore.

I'll need to go back to review Kyle Thompson's previous games: Sheepo and Islets. The trend I noticed going to Sheepo was increased scope, more expansive world, and a refinement of the arts Tyler. In other words, nothing but positives. Crypt Custodian continues down the same path.

The perspective shifts in CC to an isometric view. Originally, I was skeptical, but upon completion I was truly enjoying viewing the world from that perspective. It helps that the environments are varied and well detailed. I've been a sucker for Kyle's art style since Sheepo so that's no surprise. The combat is fun! It's nothing too complex compared to a titans of the industry like Hollow Knight or Silksong, but nevertheless it's enjoyable. There's a good amount of progression both in terms of new abilities after defeating bosses and increasing the number of slots you have available for this game's version of charms. There's also a solid amount of options for your special attack that I found myself changing every so often. Speaking of the bosses, they were often well designed and challenging enough to need a couple of attempts. Nothing too crazy, you won't be spending dozens of attempts to beat any of the bosses.

I didn't play much of the post-game content due to getting distracted by another game so I can't speak to that. The core game and story are very good and this would be a solid choice for any fan of metroidvanias to pick up and play over a weekend. Very excited to check out Well Dweller when that releases!

I am not usually a big fan of Metroidvanias. I know that's an unpopular opinion, but I've never been able to quite resonate with them as I would often get lost in what I was doing or the atmosphere wouldn't quite click. This game however, ended up being on my top games I have played on Steam. Despite it being a genre I do not usually enjoy, it exceeded my expectations. The art is beautiful, the level design is challenging but not impossible, the story while straight forward, hits incredibly hard. I could not recommend this game enough, even if its not your favorite genre, I guarantee you will find something to love in it.

I've been a big fan of Kyle Thompson's work ever since playing Islets, which is still easily among the best metroidvania's out there, and Sheepo is probably THE best microvania. I had thus bought Crypt Custodian day one to help Kyle with the steam algorythm, but only have gotten the time to really sit down with this game now. Also due to my weird tendency to want to play the best games of my backlog last, which was hard since new MV's keep coming out every other day nowadays. Regardless i'm so glad that i finally got to play this game, and i'm gonna be honest it's not only Kyle's best MV to date it's also among the absolute top of the genre being part of my top 10 for now. The game is exceptional in every regard and makes me all the more excited for Well Dweller which will also be a day one purchase. But we're not here to talk upcoming games, rather disect exactly why Crypt Custodian is so incredibly good and why you should play it.

The most important thing in a metroidvania are the controls and level design which are flawless in Custodian. Despite being a top down game, Crypt Custodian involves a lot of movement tech for everything. Luckily our adorable feline protagonist Pluto controls exceptionally well which makes every other aspect more enjoyable especially platforming and combat. The platforming in Custodian is insanely good for a top down game in no small part due to the exceptional level design. There's a perfect mix of fun platforming challenges, great puzzles, storytelling and combat rooms. Throw in biome specific mechanics, a great difficulty curve and an expansive interconnected map and you've got a recipe for succes to make exploration feel incredibly rewarding. There's lots of things to do and find so wherever you go there's something to enjoy. The map is also incredibly usefull and the game gives great tools to 100%, which i highly recommend going for.

The combat in Custodian is also incredible, lots of enemy variety with unique and well telegraphed attacks. Each and every single one was a joy to fight and the difficulty on normal is perfect, not too hard but not free either. You've gotta be focused but if you are locked in you will breeze through the levels which isn't a bad thing as it keeps progression going. The whole game is excellently paced but other than the platforming/exploration the combat will keep you hooked especially during bosses. The bosses up the difficulty a bit but are once again perfectly balanced and designed. These fights are incredibly fun and there's a good amount of them too! Kyle knows how to make great bosses as shown in Islets but these were even better. If you can't get enough of them don't fret as every boss you beat is immediately unlocked in the boss rush which has a few achievements tied to it for people who really wanna grind away at the game.

Regardless of to what completion you beat the game one thing will be obvious; the game is gorgeous. While backgrounds can feel a bit samey the actual paltforms you walk on are beautifully decorated, especially in the more verdant areas. The first biome reminded me of Hyper Light Drifter's eastern zone in all the best possible ways, but there's lots of visual variety between the numerous biomes. The art is still in Kyle's signature style but even more polished and detailed. The characters themselves are also super iconic and memorable with Crouton being my personal top pick. The voice lines are also quirky and fun, especially of the feline characters as they consist of remixed meows, purs and growls which i adore as a cat lover. They are also all written with his signature charm and sense of comedy. I audible chuckled multiple times over many of the different lines which were well written and delivered an at times suprisingly touching story.

Also speaking of audio the soundtrack once again fit the game perfectly. While this genre of music isn't generally my thing the brothers' works are inseperable as they just combine to make a perfectly cosy gaming experience. Nothing will break your feeling of comfort playing this, not even bugs or technicalities as the game runs flawlessly. It loads fast and it runs fast without any crashes or similar things, the only minor issue is that the game freezes ever so shortly if you take a screenshot but that's on me for wanting to capture the game's beauty. In general Crypt Custodian is another incredible addition to Kyle's repertoire and pretty much an easy recommendation for every gamer not just metroidvanieurs. Wether you're skilled at games or not, wether you have a beefy pc or not, wether you like a touching or lighthearted story, this game has everything. I'm so glad i finally got to play it and it makes me even more excited for Well Dweller <3

The protag reminds me of my old black cat who met the same fate. This game almost gives me some sort of closure for her, it makes me happy to think that maybe she's out there having a good time killing pests in the afterlife.
Super fun, short, well made game. Tight controls, good difficulty settings, cute art and story, definitely worth it if you like metroidvanias!

Crypt Custodian went straight into my Top 10 Metroidvanias of all time. The combat feels so snappy, the exploration is super rewarding, and honestly, all the mechanics are just top-notch and polished.

I know some people might say it’s not hard enough if they’re looking for a challenge, but I actually really loved the overall pace of the game. It’s just a great time from start to finish without being frustrating. If you’re a fan of the genre, you definitely shouldn’t sleep on this one!
its a good game that has great gameplay, interesting characters and story. If you enjoy cats then this is for you

I love Kyle Thompson’s games. He always brings a certain charm and quality to them. If you haven’t played any, you can expect a cartoonish Tim Burton aesthetic, floaty mechanics, whimsical music (shout out to his brother), and a contained, low-stakes adventure. Even as someone who prefers more “difficult” platformers, the quality of the games never prevent me from enjoying my time with them. Crypt Custodian is no exception either: a breezy experience well in line with his other game entries Sheepo, and Islets.

Highly recommended.
