What can I say? Crypt Custodian, like its predecessor, Islets, is a cozy metroidvania with a cute art style that never gets old. The first game I didn't enjoy as much, as it had pretty lame boss mechanics and doesn't really click until the mid game onwards. Crypt Custodian however grabs you from the get go, and doesn't drop a single beat until you're done with the game.

[b]Story[/b]

Crypt Custodian is a story about dead animals and a large part of the lore behind it is finding out how each companion died, and how they were when they were still alive. There's really not much to it; you collect photos, each with a single line of caption, and each companion has 3 photos. That said, every companion has a ton of personality that wouldn't be out of place in any children's book. Heck, playing the game feels like being read a bedtime story, really.

[b]Progression & abilities[/b]

Honestly, as a metroidvania, there isn't much depth to it. Abilities you unlock throughout the game are functional, but not mind-blowing. There's nothing revolutionary here, and I'm fine with it. There's only 1 or 2 of them that will be used constantly in combat; the rest are purely for traversal. All in all, nothing much to see here, it's mediocre in a good way. Like a shy kid that you can rely on as a friend.

[b]Combat & bosses[/b]

Bosses are much more interesting this time around. They still rely on being bullet-hell bosses that tests your pattern recognition more than reflexes, but that's just how the dev likes it. Don't expect Megaman or Hollow Knight levels of brawling and positioning here; most fights are purely about evading bullets while finding openings to strike at the boss.

That said, if you're a bullet-hell veteran, this game will be EASY as heck. For the rest of us, it's a decent amount of challenge. I have to give props to the dev for a few fights that are actually quite creative, like the biome that swaps tangibility on dashing, or the boss on a roller coaster track.

[b]Exploration[/b]

The game world is fun to explore. Each biome is distinct and actually memorable, although some of them are too large for their own good. Backtracking is done quite well; not only you have free fast travel from the get go, most of the time you unlock plenty of shortcuts that makes traversing biomes to hunt down collectibles a joy. This is often an area in metroidvanias that amateur devs drop the ball in, so it's good to see this dev (who is a veteran in this genre) nail it so well.

[b]Conclusion[/b]

I love it. This will be the game I recommend to people if they're looking for some light hearted, moderately challenging metroidvania that isn't pretentious nor self-indulgent. The game respects your time, and keeps things light and fun.

Very fun game.  

The exploration is great, there are lots of different routes you can explore the world in and get your upgrades in different orders which is definitely a highlight, a lot of other metroidvanias give the appearance of being open but are pretty limited in the order so I definitely appreciate this game being so open.  Looked like there was even a randomizer mode unlock after beating the game but I didn't try that out so can't say how well it works.

Combat was pretty fun, it's not super difficult even on hard mode however some bosses can provide a bit of a challenge, was fun enough that I went for the boss challenge achievements (limited gear, can't get hit, on a timer).  Game isn't super punishing, dying just sends you back to a checkpoint but you don't lose any progress which I appreciate.

Music is pretty good.  Art looks good.  Dialogue is fun and cute, story is limited but decent (mix of funny and depressing).

Overall very enjoyable experience, easy recommendation.

game_name: Duck Detective: The Ghost of Glamping
review_text:
Well, it didn’t take long for me to play and finish the sequel. I couldn’t stay away from the Duck Detective for too long! This game was just as short and comfy as the first one; I am very delighted to play these games! It’s such a delight to hear two of the main characters’ voice actors returning and reprising their role as their respective characters. And to my delight — this game does focus on their relationship a lot more!

There’s a lot more animated cut scenes this time around and, in general, a much bigger map to explore. However, it’s not so terribly big that I felt overwhelmed; I will say that it felt more progressive the more games being made for this series and it felt as though not only the devs felt more comfortable in their ability but also to keep the player’s attention. The mechanics are the same, more or less, but there’s no need to fix what’s not broken.

At least this time, the puzzles are streamlined in the way it doesn’t feel like it “came out of nowhere” but through sleuthing and putting it together a bit more natural than guessing and hoping you’re correct. On the other hand, at least for me, it did make me feel as though the game went faster than the first one did. Perhaps it’s more to the point there’s more to interact with and more character moments rather than simply getting to know the world.

Speaking of the story, I thoroughly enjoyed the story this time around as well! However, if you’re the type of person who wants to explore this world within the two-hour period you’re in, I admit, it’s a bit more lacking. There’s a lot more character development between Freddy and Eugene, which is fun for me, but it did create a way for me to see the story’s flaws a little easier. I do wish certain aspects were utilized more in the story to create either more of a mystery or at the very least, an even spookier atmosphere. I do like the creepiness of it and the humor still made me laugh.

However, because of this, I did feel that mystery itself was a little lacking. It does take its time to develop it, to be sure, but it didn’t feel as nearly scandalous as it did in the first game. I mean, it’s still there, but because of the laser focus between Eugene and Freddy, it’s less oomph when it’s revealed. It didn’t detract, though, from the gameplay or the story terribly but I wanted to bring it up if anyone cared about that.

I did love the art direction; since it’s spooky, there’s a lot of night shots and a lot of mood lighting. Because of this decision, it made it a bit hard to tell if the prompts needed to be pressed or not. Maybe in the future, these prompts could be blended in better and perhaps even enhance the scene more. Still, this was a very enjoyable game! I really hope there’s a third one in the works, especially in that cliffhanger of an ending!

game_name: Duck Detective: The Ghost of Glamping
review_text: Another amazing game from the Duck Detective, wonderful cast of charaters and an engaging mystery. Nothing new about the game play mechanics but it's just as fun as the first game. There is a slightly larger area to play with and there seemed to be a lot more side stories this time around. It's not a long game but it's a fun time.

game_name: Duck Detective: The Ghost of Glamping
review_text: Short, fun, funny, quack button

game_name: Duck Detective: The Ghost of Glamping
review_text: Would play 10 more of these after playing both games in the series. Very cozy game that holds your hand just enough to get you to the explosive twist ending.

game_name: Duck Detective: The Ghost of Glamping
review_text: This game is super cute & fun to play. Getting 100% is very easy & the story is only about an hour long depending on how well you are at mystery games. Worth it if you buy the bundle or buy the game on sale. For the amount of gameplay though I wouldn't tell people to buy it at full price.

game_name: Duck Detective: The Ghost of Glamping
review_text:
Generally good fun, but this is largely just a direct extension of the first game. There are no new mechanics to be seen, and everything plays in the same pattern. I found I didn't quite enjoy this as much as Secret Salami, often felt like you were still effectively guessing at the solution even once all clues were found; as opposed to the original where you likely already had a handle on the solution at 80/90% investigated.

Still worth a playthrough, still super charming and with fantastic voice acting and music, just expected a bit more. Hopefully for the next game they'll push the boat out more.

game_name: Duck Detective: The Ghost of Glamping
review_text:
Somewhat easier than the first game, however with a slighly larger area. 

I hope this series continues, the voice acting and writing are both very entertaining.

game_name: Duck Detective: The Ghost of Glamping
review_text: Super fun deduction game with real Golden Idol style deductions that present a real challenge at times.

game_name: Duck Detective: The Ghost of Glamping
review_text: This game was so wonderfully fun and quirky. I thoroughly enjoyed the exposition, jokes, and the scenes that were in this. The best deducktions and doing it all for the bread.

game_name: Duck Detective: The Ghost of Glamping
review_text:
[h3] positives [/h3] 

- just like the previous game, the art and character designs are all so cute
- the voice acting was once again great 
- the puzzles and mysteries were equally very fun in this one
- you can quack which i somehow missed in the first one. i made up for this in here by quacking incessantly while investigating
- freddy frederson

I had both of these on my wishlist and my girlfriend gifted them to me for Valentine's. We played this in one take after beating the first one and found the mysteries in here to be equally as compelling and hilarious as the first game. They're building the foundations for a very solid series and we literally can't wait to play the third one (provided a third one is made).

game_name: Duck Detective: The Ghost of Glamping
review_text: I got this a gift for Christmas and after playing the first game, I quickly played this one. Charming like the first one, characters are fun and story was interesting but not overall confusing. I can't wait to see what the next installment for this series brings.

game_name: Duck Detective: The Ghost of Glamping
review_text:
Fun, cute, well voice acted, and very short. For the price it's just fine, but I did find myself surprised when it suddenly ended.

And just like the first game, there were several times where the "deducktion" the game expects you to make simply doesn't make sense given the available information. 

Even after solving a particular mystery, I sometimes couldn't figure out what clues where supposed to have led me there. It's weirdly sloppy for a game this short.

But I did like it - the addition of Freddy as a companion was welcome, and endearing.

game_name: Duck Detective: The Ghost of Glamping
review_text:
An excellent follow up to a game that I already enjoyed.
This one plays much like the first, and with a story that has just as many twists and turns.
Like the first game a simple crime at the beginning leads to the exploration of a much bigger web of conspiracy and crime. This story does a great job of expanding on the setting and even has a sad and spooky ghost tale to add on to it.
Once again a solid recommend.

game_name: Duck Detective: The Ghost of Glamping
review_text:
The loading screens in the Duck Detective games have “Duck Facts.” When I saw the fact, “Ducks were named after Sir John Duckworth, who invented the duck in 1756,” I so failed to stifle my laughter that Mr. Act made me read the duck fact to him. He chuckled and then went, “Yup, that’s 100% your sense of humor.” So that this game is pandering to me specifically is probably something to keep in mind when I say: This game was hilarious.

Honestly I loved everything about these games. The art was wonderful, the environments were bright and colorful, the mysteries were simultaneously both low-stakes and huge conspiracies, and the gameplay was excellent. I thought the UI was one of the best of any of these games, and this is the first time I thought the mad-libs style solving system worked like a dream. Bless this game for the innovation of a separate character page so you don’t have to keep re-entering names. And the vibe was just unbeatable. The idea that ducks get trashed on white bread and DD is just stuffing wads of bread into his coat is incredible.

I do think the characters were stronger in the first game. I thought all the officepeople were such great hammy but real people. Sophie posting to AITA was one of the funniest gags I’ve ever seen. I do have to say I really like how they handled Theodore in the second game, without giving away too much the 180 in my opinion of him was A+ guiding by the narrative, and I also liked Toby and Amelia, though I found Madeline and Arnaud to be a bit one-dimensional.

One thing I did end up liking about the second game was Freddie and DD’s relationship, I thought the swing from Freddie being overbearing to DD just being so irresponsible it loops back to being unkind was really effective. Poor Freddie! He has so much pathos, lol. I also really liked the sequel hook, both because it says DD is going to have to actually deal with his issues as well as because the idea of Freddie having to save DD tickles me. I really appreciate the game playing with the detective convention but also being willing to go, “Actually someone like this this is probably not in a great place,” and picking it apart a little.

My only real complaint about these games is that I have to wait for the next one to come out! I wish there were 15 and I could just go through them all.

It’s worth buying (maybe when it’s on sale) and playing.

game_name: Duck Detective: The Ghost of Glamping
review_text:
Duck Detective: The Ghost of Glamping is an investigation adventure game and the sequel to The Secret Salami. The Duck Detective is on vacation, but his trip takes a turn for the mysterious when strange things start to happen at the campground (glampground?). 

Much like in the previous game, to investigate the mystery you explore the campground, talk to suspects, and inspect objects to gather clue words.  You plug these clue words in to statements, and finding the correct statements advances the story. There are two different difficulty modes, but even the more difficult of the two is very forgiving. In Sleuth mode, the game doesn't tell you which words you've filled in wrong, but still gives you an idea of how many words in your statement are incorrect. As long as you're following the story, it should be pretty easy to fill in the right statements once you have all the clues. The game was a little easier than the first game. There were fewer places where I ended up feeling like I needed to guess.

I liked the story just as much as I did in the first game. The mystery was very engaging and had some twists and turns that I wasn't expecting. It was usually pretty easy to follow, although there was one spot where I wasn't quite understand what the game was trying to get at, and I ultimately had to make some guesses and let the game explain it to me. The characters are still really cute and endearing, and the dynamic between the Duck Detective and his roomate/assistant is pretty fun. Like in the first game, you have a choice of which character(s) to arrest at the end, and making the right choice left me a little conflicted. 

Overall, The Ghost of Glamping was an interesting mystery and just as funny and entertaining as its predecessor. The deductions are usually easy and logical, the mystery is intriguing, and the voice acting is really good. I'm eagerly looking forward to the next entry in the series. 8/10

game_name: Duck Detective: The Ghost of Glamping
review_text:
Well, it didn’t take long for me to play and finish the sequel. I couldn’t stay away from the Duck Detective for too long! This game was just as short and comfy as the first one; I am very delighted to play these games! It’s such a delight to hear two of the main characters’ voice actors returning and reprising their role as their respective characters. And to my delight — this game does focus on their relationship a lot more!

There’s a lot more animated cut scenes this time around and, in general, a much bigger map to explore. However, it’s not so terribly big that I felt overwhelmed; I will say that it felt more progressive the more games being made for this series and it felt as though not only the devs felt more comfortable in their ability but also to keep the player’s attention. The mechanics are the same, more or less, but there’s no need to fix what’s not broken.

At least this time, the puzzles are streamlined in the way it doesn’t feel like it “came out of nowhere” but through sleuthing and putting it together a bit more natural than guessing and hoping you’re correct. On the other hand, at least for me, it did make me feel as though the game went faster than the first one did. Perhaps it’s more to the point there’s more to interact with and more character moments rather than simply getting to know the world.

Speaking of the story, I thoroughly enjoyed the story this time around as well! However, if you’re the type of person who wants to explore this world within the two-hour period you’re in, I admit, it’s a bit more lacking. There’s a lot more character development between Freddy and Eugene, which is fun for me, but it did create a way for me to see the story’s flaws a little easier. I do wish certain aspects were utilized more in the story to create either more of a mystery or at the very least, an even spookier atmosphere. I do like the creepiness of it and the humor still made me laugh.

However, because of this, I did feel that mystery itself was a little lacking. It does take its time to develop it, to be sure, but it didn’t feel as nearly scandalous as it did in the first game. I mean, it’s still there, but because of the laser focus between Eugene and Freddy, it’s less oomph when it’s revealed. It didn’t detract, though, from the gameplay or the story terribly but I wanted to bring it up if anyone cared about that.

I did love the art direction; since it’s spooky, there’s a lot of night shots and a lot of mood lighting. Because of this decision, it made it a bit hard to tell if the prompts needed to be pressed or not. Maybe in the future, these prompts could be blended in better and perhaps even enhance the scene more. Still, this was a very enjoyable game! I really hope there’s a third one in the works, especially in that cliffhanger of an ending!

game_name: Duck Detective: The Ghost of Glamping
review_text: Just like the first game, it's got a great detective story, solid puzzles, and some fun humor. Perfect to play through on a chill Saturday night - highly recommend. And if you haven't touched the first one yet, definitely give it a go too. You can crack that case in just a couple of hours as well.

game_name: Duck Detective: The Ghost of Glamping
review_text:
Much like the first one, this is a short, cute, fun and family-friendly game about a duck being a detective for a case.

Comparing it to the first one, the gameplay is pretty similar, though there's nuances that make it better. I like that it both a) doesn't hold your hand a whole lot like the first one does, and b) that it also doesn't get to a point where you're better off bruteforcing the answers of deductions because the clues aren't clear enough (even though this time around, the answers are somewhat obvious and I could finish it all without a guide). Game felt somewhat shorter than the first one, as it took me just a bit over 2h to finish, but I guess that was bound to happen with those changes. Humour is still cheesy, a bit more subtle than the first one was. Art style and voice acting are, once again, top notch, whether or not that makes it worth the price depends on how much you value those two factors. For me, it was worth it, but it's understandable if you see it as a bit steep outside of a sale.

For a game rated E, I can see someone in their formative years having a quick fun afternoon with this, and their parents having a laugh every now and again with some of the jokes!

game_name: Duck Detective: The Ghost of Glamping
review_text:
I would mostly agree with others on here and say it is a short game (I like to take my time and look at/enjoy everything but 1-2.5 hrs is reasonable I think) BUT was fun beginning to end, for about the price of a regular movie ticket (not to suggest that that is cheap just pointing it out). 
No grinding, no distriactions, all story.
The voice acting was excellent as alwasy (honestly better than a few triple A games I've played).
As for game mechanics I think it's one of the best designed dectives games I've come across, simple, intuitive but demands just enough of your attention and memory with a few mini games and the deducktions to feel your progress without a leveling system or anything over the top. Plus you can save & exit at literally anytime.
The story is fun and has the same tone and humour as the first one; they are worldbuilding as well. 
[spoiler]I like that they give you the stats of who got arrested at the end. [/spoiler]
If you have some extra cash (especially if it's on sale) I really don't think you can go wrong.

game_name: Secret Agent Wizard Boy and the International Crime Syndicate
review_text:
I want to emphasize something out of the gate, this should be played with friends and would mainly recommend this for people who love creative and chaotic sandboxes. This is very much a work in progress, and at the time of this review, it does not have much content beyond a few hours.

With that out of the way, I had so much fun with this game playing around and being absolutely stupid with a friend, so much so that there were multiple times my jaw started hurting with how much I was laughing. I won't say much about what stupid things we did so that you can explore and experiment for yourself, but it felt like a chaotic fever dream. I can't wait to come back after a few updates or for the full release to see what new shenanigans we can pull.

game_name: Secret Agent Wizard Boy and the International Crime Syndicate
review_text:
[h1] [b] IPad baby version (Still to long for them) [/b] [/h1]

This game is great, got the OG PS1 and 2 Harry Potter look, felt like playing those old games a bit but better, lot's of secrets to find which I love, great spells to mess around with and break the game in more fun ways, it's in early access but what the game has already makes it way worth the price, seen update for outdoor expansion which I am supper excited, I 100% recommend this game if you loved the OG Harry Potter games, and if not, if you love games where you find secrets, explore and there's multiple ways to get to the solution and a lot of using your head, then I 100% recommend this game still! Plus it has multiplayer which makes it even better.

[h1] [b] Good attention span version [/b] [/h1]

[h1] [b] The Game [/b] [/h1]

[h2] Gameplay [/h2]

(I would recommend keeping your playthrough spoiler free as that will give the best experience if you love figuring things out yourself and exploring, I didn't get that fully as I watched Oneyplays play the game, but there was a hell of a lot more to explore they didn't)
[h3] The main overall sandbox [/h3]
The game is a sandbox with a main objective but you can go about that how you like, the main focus of the game is just finding ways to do things, you can just be reckless and go using spells on people, but the school is full of things and the characters pathing is something to keep an eye on.
You will find that the game is very exploitable in the best way, and that you will be rewarded for doing so, you can also just break the game and have fun doing that, the school as it is now is crammed full of things to use or mess around with, there are a lot of potion types, spells and abilities you can upgrade your character with, if your creative you will find multiple ways to get around or do something.

[h3] The objectives [/h3]
(most of this won't be spoilers but I think going in blind completely with this part is best)
[spoiler] There are 6 strange shapes you need to collect to open a door down in the dungeon they can be located around the map in different places, you can go about collecting these whatever way you want or find, there are also Emblems to collect, 5 will let you upgrade your character, for things like jump, health, fall damage and so on, as well as gadgets, there is also little challenges that give you emblems for doing things, they can get really creative and tell you to just break the game or to discover stuff in the game, the game does require you to try things and be observant to discover how to solve puzzles. [/spoiler]

[h1] [b] Thoughts and feedback [/b] [/h1]

[h2] The gameplay [/h2]

[h3] Spells [/h3]
Love them, I can't wait to see what else the devs come up with, the two best spells are [spoiler] Duplo and Biggerfi 1 duplicates things and the other makes things bigger, there is a good exploit with those two spells [/spoiler]. I have spent ages messing around with the spells in the game finding new ways to get around things or explore the map more.

[h3] The School [/h3]
It feels like how the OG Harry Potter games felt but more open and free, right now the school is small and I think there is only 1 dorm room, the other seems more like a club room, I do hope to see rooms for all with lots to find in them, same with the Classrooms as I think there's about 3 or so, I do want a bigger school, specially with multiple floors, what there is is already great, and I have seen in one of the update posts that it seems the school is getting bigger, or more added to it, as well as an outside which I can't wait.

[h3] The Sandbox [/h3]
I have just been messing around in the game and exploring, I love that the game lets me try out all my ideas and they can just work, it's so rewarding getting somewhere or finding ways around problems that feel like they aren't intended, but with how this game is, all of it probably is.

[h2] Future content [/h2]

[h3] The School [/h3]
As I said before, I hope to see the school expanded and bigger, I understand the whole thing of having a small map with so much to do inside it compared to a large map with nothing to do, but the game looks like it's doing what I feel games should do, and that is start off small, make that small space jammed pack full of things, and expand out repeating the process, I don't expect a enormous map, just a good sized school with all dorms, a bunch of class rooms, club rooms and dinning hall, some stuff to make it feel like it's a school Wizard kids would go to.

[h3] The Sandbox [/h3]
I hope to see more potions, spells and hazards, with more ways to get around them, and use them, more secret areas that are well hidden and secrets that mess with the school when activated.

[h3] Mod support [/h3]
Always going to put it, it always improves and extends the life of games, not much to be said, just hope near release or something the game gets mod support, or is open to modding and has a steam workshop.

[h3] Character customisation [/h3]

game_name: Secret Agent Wizard Boy and the International Crime Syndicate
review_text:
[h1] [b] IPad baby version (Still to long for them) [/b] [/h1]

This game is great, got the OG PS1 and 2 Harry Potter look, felt like playing those old games a bit but better, lot's of secrets to find which I love, great spells to mess around with and break the game in more fun ways, it's in early access but what the game has already makes it way worth the price, seen update for outdoor expansion which I am supper excited, I 100% recommend this game if you loved the OG Harry Potter games, and if not, if you love games where you find secrets, explore and there's multiple ways to get to the solution and a lot of using your head, then I 100% recommend this game still! Plus it has multiplayer which makes it even better.

[h1] [b] Good attention span version [/b] [/h1]

[h1] [b] The Game [/b] [/h1]

[h2] Gameplay [/h2]

(I would recommend keeping your playthrough spoiler free as that will give the best experience if you love figuring things out yourself and exploring, I didn't get that fully as I watched Oneyplays play the game, but there was a hell of a lot more to explore they didn't)
[h3] The main overall sandbox [/h3]
The game is a sandbox with a main objective but you can go about that how you like, the main focus of the game is just finding ways to do things, you can just be reckless and go using spells on people, but the school is full of things and the characters pathing is something to keep an eye on.
You will find that the game is very exploitable in the best way, and that you will be rewarded for doing so, you can also just break the game and have fun doing that, the school as it is now is crammed full of things to use or mess around with, there are a lot of potion types, spells and abilities you can upgrade your character with, if your creative you will find multiple ways to get around or do something.

[h3] The objectives [/h3]
(most of this won't be spoilers but I think going in blind completely with this part is best)
[spoiler] There are 6 strange shapes you need to collect to open a door down in the dungeon they can be located around the map in different places, you can go about collecting these whatever way you want or find, there are also Emblems to collect, 5 will let you upgrade your character, for things like jump, health, fall damage and so on, as well as gadgets, there is also little challenges that give you emblems for doing things, they can get really creative and tell you to just break the game or to discover stuff in the game, the game does require you to try things and be observant to discover how to solve puzzles. [/spoiler]

[h1] [b] Thoughts and feedback [/b] [/h1]

[h2] The gameplay [/h2]

[h3] Spells [/h3]
Love them, I can't wait to see what else the devs come up with, the two best spells are [spoiler] Duplo and Biggerfi 1 duplicates things and the other makes things bigger, there is a good exploit with those two spells [/spoiler]. I have spent ages messing around with the spells in the game finding new ways to get around things or explore the map more.

[h3] The School [/h3]
It feels like how the OG Harry Potter games felt but more open and free, right now the school is small and I think there is only 1 dorm room, the other seems more like a club room, I do hope to see rooms for all with lots to find in them, same with the Classrooms as I think there's about 3 or so, I do want a bigger school, specially with multiple floors, what there is is already great, and I have seen in one of the update posts that it seems the school is getting bigger, or more added to it, as well as an outside which I can't wait.

[h3] The Sandbox [/h3]
I have just been messing around in the game and exploring, I love that the game lets me try out all my ideas and they can just work, it's so rewarding getting somewhere or finding ways around problems that feel like they aren't intended, but with how this game is, all of it probably is.

[h2] Future content [/h2]

[h3] The School [/h3]
As I said before, I hope to see the school expanded and bigger, I understand the whole thing of having a small map with so much to do inside it compared to a large map with nothing to do, but the game looks like it's doing what I feel games should do, and that is start off small, make that small space jammed pack full of things, and expand out repeating the process, I don't expect a enormous map, just a good sized school with all dorms, a bunch of class rooms, club rooms and dinning hall, some stuff to make it feel like it's a school Wizard kids would go to.

[h3] The Sandbox [/h3]
I hope to see more potions, spells and hazards, with more ways to get around them, and use them, more secret areas that are well hidden and secrets that mess with the school when activated.

[h3] Mod support [/h3]
Always going to put it, it always improves and extends the life of games, not much to be said, just hope near release or something the game gets mod support, or is open to modding and has a steam workshop.

[h3] Character customisation [/h3]
I would like to be able to customise our character, choose how they look and so on, even if it's just their looks if the devs add down the line a clothe swapping system that you need to get into places, if not then just let us customise that to, I always love customisation in games, feel it's always lacking or watered down.

[h2] Bugs [/h2]
Once you finish the game there is a bug where your character will have all their upgrades set to default, it fixes if you buy and upgrade but you can't do that as you upgrade more and more so it causes this annoying bug.

That is all I can think of for now.

[h3] Love the game, best purchase, Highly recommend it, haven't spoken about the Multiplayer yet as I haven't done that yet, This is closer to what I wanted then what Hogwarts Legacy was, just wish that game went more into the learning and school stuff a bit more like an advanced and better version of Bully. [/h3]

quite a few farm sims haven't seen me in ages and i fear it's going to stay that way ! this game is the perfect blend of those typical "cutesy"/"cozy"/"girly" elements that make up everyone's faves without being yet another passenger on the cozy game hype train just pumping out soulless and overpriced products and labeling it the next "stardew valley like" game of the year. the devs genuinely listen and are soo sweet ! the art is adorable (and done by humans) the soundtrack is awesome, there's so many QOL features like the go home button, color accessibility options, proximity inventory, adjusting day length, overflow storage, sleeping anywhere after unlocking the box, crafting on the go (and multiple things at a time cough cough new horizons), and so much moreeee. i also like that it doesn't feel cheesy either ? like even the kart for example getting the attachments is relatively simple but you still put the work in and now rather than mining being some ridiculously repetitive activity you can enjoy feeling like the terrorist you desire to be and watch everything fall apart with that drill of yours >:) they utilize the risk-reward system very well in order for the game to actually be fun (instead of just yet another walking/decorating simulator with dialogue every now and then) while also making it customizable and not frustrating ! if combat rlly isn't ur thing u can make it as simple and as easy as you want or you can have fun and go ham with different spell combos and difficult enemies. And of course not to mention the genuinely interesting story and characters with like actually different personalities ! Overall i'm obsessed with this game~ it's scratched almost every itch for me when it comes to a chill game with cute atmosphere to wind down with and has erased every problem i've been having with cozy games lately and i can't wait to see more <3
*works on deck amazing too ! :) AND NOT 2 MENTION THE DEMO !!! BRING BACK GAMES HAVING DEMOS AHHH

This game is awesome! I’m a veteran cozy gamer and I feel like I’m kind of hard to impress but there was a lot about Snacko that genuinely blew me away. 

Snacko’s best qualities: 

-Vibes: Visuals are amazing, design is very cozy. You can build/design some really cute stuff and it feels nice to just run around and hang out. I love that you can see the sky and the clouds are nice. There are fireflies in the evenings and lighting items look very nice.

-The mines: TBH I usually dread the mines in farm sims. Most are not very innovative and I find them to be a total slog. Snacko’s take on the mines is genuinely so fresh and fun. They’re not top down which is a huge plus in my book. There are fun things to find (like logs! Journal entry things like you’d find in like, Mass Effect), you can get a drill, you can generate a new mine if you’re tired of the one you’ve been exploring. There’s this remnant loom thing too which is a fun and sort of addicting mechanic. I love the mines in Snacko.

-Many small random QoL things: You can sleep anywhere. You can get a car. Travel is smooth and fast. Most crafting pulls from universal storage, so you don’t have to move things around too much. You can also auto deposit items to existing stacks in a storage container just by walking up to it and pressing a button. You can save anywhere. Tilled land stays tilled unless you plant grass over it.

-Customization: While there is a little room for improvement as far as character customization (this is me saying i would love clothing options in this game), the farm/island design elements were really on point for me. The build mode is great and I find it easy to use. It’s pretty flexible in where you can place things, everything rotates (even diagonally). You can also craft furniture items directly from the build menu as long as you have the resources somewhere in storage. This is huge and makes the build mode feel much more relaxing and immersive since I don’t have to back out of the menu and run to a crafting station when I decide I want to place something. The build/crafting menus have a search bar. I think a lot of the craft able stuff in this game looks pretty appealing, too. 

-Characters: The characters in this game are bursting with personality. I do wish they had a little more dialogue but they have friendship events that are pretty fun. The portraits are very cute too. I love my angry son Nobu Jr. Also, the game is definitely geared toward teens/adults, which is something I really appreciate in farm sims.

There are some areas that I think feel a little unpolished. For example, one of the festivals was pretty broken for me and there are supposed to be tourists walking around your island daily but I only saw them for the first few days. These things feel really minor in comparison to everything I love about Snacko. It’s worth mentioning that it is a grind so many not appeal to those who aren’t into that. For example, it takes a while to be able to expand your house. I like this. It’s the perfect game for when you want to dive in and spend dozens of hours in a game. It’s a nice, slow grind. I really hope more people find this game, as it almost flew under my radar and I think it’s one of the best recent farm sims I’ve played. It feels like a breath of fresh air in a niche that I very much love.

AND THE LOOM! Resources in the mine can put into the loom, bigass machine that eats resources for specific refining recipes that essentially lets you try to gamble your resources for better/different ones. As my uncle Ron would know best, gambling everything never ceases to be so much ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ fun. What you walk out of the mine with isn't your whole bag that day, you can still put your crystal clusters and scrap ore in to see what comes of it. Such a fun mechanic.

The building and deco is fun and pretty in depth, characters are very charming, very visually pleasing and distinct, the dev's are still super active and supportive, and you can drive a car with a drill on it. Show them some love.

I bought the game and immediately played it for 7 hours. Safe to say it's really good.

Like a lot of cozy games nowadays, you can clearly see where the developers took inspiration from when designing the game (in this case, mainly Stardew Valley and Animal Crossing). Despite these similarities, I never felt like I was playing a ripoff at any point. Snacko takes common tropes from its genre and combines them with its own concepts to make a novel experience that clearly had a lot of love put into it. The gameplay is fun, the NPCs are absolutely adorable, and the art style is great to look at (I'm very partial to the mix of 2D and 3D). The game also seems to have a very big focus on accessibility. This includes visual tweaks like reduced flashing, dark UI, and even filters for the world itself to make it easier on the eyes. This also extends to making the gameplay itself accessible with toggles for mob spawning, longer days, and the ability to teleport home instantly if you want an even chiller-than-normal experience.

There are a few bits that are still rough around the edges, mainly when it comes to the mines. The combat can be a little wonky because of your character's physics, there's some weird knockback when mining large material deposits, and the procedural generation isn't all there yet... But the game seems to have a dedicated team who are still working to iron out these kinks, so I only expect the game to get better from here. I'm so excited to see how the game evolves from here :)

first review ever, because snacko is so amazing and i would really love to be the person to make someone purchase it. ^-^this game is genuinely one of the best purchases i've made on steam.
i thought the price was a little steep for one of  a million "cozy games" but it is SO MUCH MORE than just some lesser-than stardew-copy or mobile game being sold for steam users. you can tell the devs actually care about making it a long-term entertaining GAME and it is so nice to play knowing all the mechanics and gameplay is technical, polished, etc. while it does have some things similar to games you would think like stardew or animal crossing, it's just the consequence of being a survival farming game! 

some of the notable things about this game compared to other games that i enjoy alot:
> i am NOT someone who likes dialogue/story/character interactions or cutscenes in-game, i LOVE that their is a community/villager mechanic in this game. mMaking the villagers shopkeepers/chefs/etc and actually giving them something to do makes them soooo much more engaging and easy to like! + obviously the characters are genuinely all sooo adorable and individual. the cutscenes and storylines dont drag, the characters have funny dialogue and make sense in context, A+ this is genuinely the only game where ive actually wanted to gift the villagers and interact with them.

> the town building and general-creative freedomness is sooooooooooooooooooooooooo fun & nice to have in the game. i love being able to set up little cul-de-sacs with the same style houses almost like mini-neightborhoods or cities within the larger town, and being able to decorate the surroundings of their houses with the villager's personality is alot of fun. the furniture and different styles in the game are ADORABLE and all of them have such appealing designs, especially the way the villagers match their homes.

> i'm honestly not big on singleplayer games so idk really what to compare it to but the caves-system is alot of fun compared to something like stardew or minecraft/terraria. i love the drill and the item/recipe/upgrade system, i havent played a game with something similar and it was very fun to play the first few times but continued to be pretty engaging throughout later-game grinding.

ok this is kinda long sorry but one more note i totally thought this game would be kinda unfinished/short but im like 4 biomes deep into the story & it doesnt rly seem to be slowing down anytime soon, which was a HUGE surprise to me! very engaging even 30+ hours later!

i mean to just say if you're thinking about buying this game just do it. its so addicting like a flash-game, i would spend hours staying up grinding just chasing quest after quest in anticipation. So worth it. 10000/10 awesomest game ever

quite a few farm sims haven't seen me in ages and i fear it's going to stay that way ! this game is the perfect blend of those typical "cutesy"/"cozy"/"girly" elements that make up everyone's faves without being yet another passenger on the cozy game hype train just pumping out soulless and overpriced products and labeling it the next "stardew valley like" game of the year. the devs genuinely listen and are soo sweet ! the art is adorable (and done by humans) the soundtrack is awesome, there's so many QOL features like the go home button, color accessibility options, proximity inventory, adjusting day length, overflow storage, sleeping anywhere after unlocking the box, crafting on the go (and multiple things at a time cough cough new horizons), and so much moreeee. i also like that it doesn't feel cheesy either ? like even the kart for example getting the attachments is relatively simple but you still put the work in and now rather than mining being some ridiculously repetitive activity you can enjoy feeling like the terrorist you desire to be and watch everything fall apart with that drill of yours >:) they utilize the risk-reward system very well in order for the game to actually be fun (instead of just yet another walking/decorating simulator with dialogue every now and then) while also making it customizable and not frustrating ! if combat rlly isn't ur thing u can make it as simple and as easy as you want or you can have fun and go ham with different spell combos and difficult enemies. And of course not to mention the genuinely interesting story and characters with like actually different personalities ! Overall i'm obsessed with this game~ it's scratched almost every itch for me when it comes to a chill game with cute atmosphere to wind down with and has erased every problem i've been having with cozy games lately and i can't wait to see more <3

This game is awesome! I’m a veteran cozy gamer and I feel like I’m kind of hard to impress but there was a lot about Snacko that genuinely blew me away. 

Snacko’s best qualities: 

-Vibes: Visuals are amazing, design is very cozy. You can build/design some really cute stuff and it feels nice to just run around and hang out. I love that you can see the sky and the clouds are nice. There are fireflies in the evenings and lighting items look very nice.

-The mines: TBH I usually dread the mines in farm sims. Most are not very innovative and I find them to be a total slog. Snacko’s take on the mines is genuinely so fresh and fun. They’re not top down which is a huge plus in my book. There are fun things to find (like logs! Journal entry things like you’d find in like, Mass Effect), you can get a drill, you can generate a new mine if you’re tired of the one you’ve been exploring. There’s this remnant loom thing too which is a fun and sort of addicting mechanic. I love the mines in Snacko.

-Many small random QoL things: You can sleep anywhere. You can get a car. Travel is smooth and fast. Most crafting pulls from universal storage, so you don’t have to move things around too much. You can also auto deposit items to existing stacks in a storage container just by walking up to it and pressing a button. You can save anywhere. Tilled land stays tilled unless you plant grass over it.

-Customization: While there is a little room for improvement as far as character customization (this is me saying i would love clothing options in this game), the farm/island design elements were really on point for me. The build mode is great and I find it easy to use. It’s pretty flexible in where you can place things, everything rotates (even diagonally). You can also craft furniture items directly from the build menu as long as you have the resources somewhere in storage. This is huge and makes the build mode feel much more relaxing and immersive since I don’t have to back out of the menu and run to a crafting station when I decide I want to place something. The build/crafting menus have a search bar. I think a lot of the craft able stuff in this game looks pretty appealing, too. 

-Characters: The characters in this game are bursting with personality. I do wish they had a little more dialogue but they have friendship events that are pretty fun. The portraits are very cute too. I love my angry son Nobu Jr. Also, the game is definitely geared toward teens/adults, which is something I really appreciate in farm sims.

There are some areas that I think feel a little unpolished. For example, one of the festivals was pretty broken for me and there are supposed to be tourists walking around your island daily but I only saw them for the first few days. These things feel really minor in comparison to everything I love about Snacko. It’s worth mentioning that it is a grind so many not appeal to those who aren’t into that. I’ve seen some criticism of general quest progression and how it’s unclear how to unlock certain things. Like one quest might mention a crafting item that you can’t unlock until further down another quest line. One of the devs posted a really nice spoiler-free unlock guide which I think really helps for this. I’ve started to sort of sit back and just focus on completing quests I can and I’m finding that I sort of eventually find/unlock everything, it just might take a while. 

Something I really like about Snacko (but might understandably be off putting to some) is that it’s slow. You can’t blow through most of the story in a day and sometimes I play for a few hours and don’t make a ton of progress. Some things take a while to unlock. For example, it takes a while to be able to expand your house. I like this. It’s the perfect game for when you want to dive in and spend dozens of hours in a game. It’s a nice, slow grind. I really hope more people find this game, as it almost flew under my radar and I think it’s one of the best recent farm sims I’ve played. It feels like a breath of fresh air in a niche that I very much love.

A wonderful and vast farming sim with a massive quest tree and a unique cast of adorable characters. You can play this game at the pace that feels best to you. Most of the crops grow in multiple seasons so you don't have to worry about when you're planting. There are a ton of QoL updates compared to other similar sims. You can travel around in a cute little zoomin' kart that also has a drill attachment that you can blast through the mines with. You can select "Go Home" from the pause menu!!!! No more running in hopes you make it home. Speaking of which, you wouldn't have to do that anyway because you can craft yourself a box to sleep in anywhere! Done in the mines but its almost midnight? Plop down your sleeping box and resume where you were first thing in the morning. 

The cooking update is fun. You can experiment with different ingredients to discover recipes (or just look them up on the wiki that the dev actively updates) and even if you make a dubious dish, there is still a use for it in game.

There are 4 wildly different biomes to discover. I'm almost 20 hours in and I have only finished the first obelisk in the first biome. The different obelisks I would compare to something like the Community Center in Stardew Valley or the Offerings in Coral Island. Each biome in Snacko has their own obelisk with different items to offer. The nice thing about the obelisks is that there are 'OR...' items to offer. Say one slot wants 30 turnips but you don't want to give up your precious crops.. that offering slot usually has a few other items it will accept in its place so you have more flexibility in what you want to offer up or have readily available.

Build wherever you want! Think a la Animal Crossing.  The build mode is absolutely wonderful. You can snap build-ables to the grid or free form it. I love the visible grid in build mode so I can make objects, like benches, perfectly even in distance from each other. You can have clutter on surfaces since objects will just slide overlap with other objects. The freedom is awesome. You invite new townies in and build them homes and are free to decorate how you see fit.

There is so much more I could say but I fear I'd be writing a novel at that point. I'll leave it at this: the devs are passionate about improving the game and keeping the Snacko players updated. I mentioned it before, but the devs themselves update the wiki (alongside player input and updates). That's some next level dedication when a lot of wikis are left up to the players. If you like games like My Time at.., Stardew, Animal Crossing, Coral Island, etc etc, I'd wager you'd enjoy this as well. The only downside I can see for other players is the 2d/ 3d mesh of elements. I personally find them enjoyable so I absolutely recommend this game. Did I mention some of the main characters are based on the devs IRL cats? Precious. If you're still here reading this, wow, thanks. Bye!

Genuinely one of my favorite farming games! I've only been following the development for about a year, but there's constant updates and QoL changes that make the game better and better each time. The graphics are absolutely adorable, and being able to change the coloring+saturation is one of the bonuses imo.

Please play Snacko!!

This game solved my issues I had with other farming games like Stardew Valley and the numerous games inspired by it.

* The game doesn't make me talk to NPC daily or try to find gifts fast. The game has a VERY slow progressing day meaning the daily cycle and energy system that farming games typically have don't rush or bother me. You also get the option to further slow down the day cycle in a dedicated "tweaks" tab in the settings.
* Calendar and seasons don't seem very important or fast so again I don't feel rushed like I did in Stardew Valley.
* You have various mobility tech in the game even early on which makes exploring or moving from your farm into the city and vice versa not feel like a drag.
* Quests.. OMG I love the quests. In other games I sometimes felt like I wasn't guided enough but Quests just magically make me able to feel like I'm being guided even though there are a lot of quests and you can do whatever you want still essentially.
* Small things that people tend to change via mods like shops working without the npcs being present.
* You are a cat.

Edit: The devs also provide a google spreadsheet you can copy to track your community center donations out of game with wiki links included like come on how cool is that.

Very fun, very cute. The town building aspects are a lot of fun and, though there aren't too many vegetables or animals, the farming aspects are made a lot of fun with the ability to customize your farm however you like and how certain vegetables can be grown out of season. The locations are all very gorgeous and unique and given reasons to go back for the ability to grow vegetables in certain seasons and the unique resources.
The onyl major downside is the presence of combat. To put it bluntly it's unecessary as well as frustrating. It's thankfully not difficult but the wand is annoying to use and the enemies take too long to defeat. It's always frustrating when a farming/crafting sims feels the need to insert combat, especially in something liek the mines which already have to many other aspects to them like navigation and time consuming mining and fishing. Not helped by the sheer range they seem to follow you for and how they annoyingly interfere with drilling. There's also a sword in the game that is very clearly intended for combat but also forcefully justifies its existence by beign the only means to collect flowers and grass outside farmland, honestly I'd prefer the combat removes entirely and the sword replaced with a scythe. My minor issue is that the magic and scifi elements are rather jarring and don't aesthetically fit with anything else.
Otherwise it's a good game. There's so many townsfolk and I feel like every one is unique. I loved getting to know my favourites, especially Mack. I heard the characters are romanceable but thankfully I haven't run into that issue as far as I'm aware.

Snacko has so much to offer! I'm honestly blown away by the amount of content and features, and all of it is absolutely adorable. And it's not just a copy-paste of the farming game formula, there's a lot of really great innovation in there that improves on the genre norms, and the game has such a beautiful unique personality!

[u]Some key highlights for me:[/u]
~ The blend of pixel art and simple 3D is wonderful
~ Multiple excellent quality-of-life improvements over other games in the genre (THANK YOU for the cardboard box for sleeping anywhere!!)
~ The characters are overflowing with charm
~ The spellbook makes the combat super fun and fresh
~ The platforming challenges with optional speedrun-friendly objectives were a nice surprise!
~ The music BOPS

I've had so much fun already and I feel like I'm still only scratching the surface! I can't wait to keep playing!

TL;DR: A Fun take on something of an Animal Crossing/Harvest Moon hybrid. While there are a lot of QOL modernizations (I appreciate the truly "build anywhere" mechanic and the freeform approach to it), there are also some frustrating design choices ( and bugs galore )  - See; trying to talk to someone while a buildable is highlighted in your hotbar even though "A" says to talk/Try interacting with ANYTHING while holding a speed grow, you will frustratingly waste a few before realizing and they are expensive.. All-in-all a worthy pickup.

Lots of other reviews are singing the praises of this game so while I really enjoyed Snacko and do recommend, let me tell some you things you might not like:

Character creation/customization is pretty basic. Yes you play a cute cat and can change your body, ears and tail color but there are no fur patterns like stripes or spots and no clothing options at any time. Not even something simple like hats. 

Seasonal/holiday events are very basic. No games to play or special items to buy. Really just an excuse to talk to and gift your neighbors as they will all gather together for the event. 

Some important items/blueprints are hidden behind quests and friendships levels. It's not always clear what unlocks what and if you don't enjoying making friends in game then you may not enjoy the repetitive chatting/gifting each day while you try and unlock npc quests. While each npc does have their own personal story, their basic day to day dialog doesn't vary much even once you start a romance with them. I don't think marriage is in the game yet.

Took me a little over 60 hours to finish my first year. I did rush through winter a bit and by my second spring I was really just trying to complete a few more achievements. I did not 100% this game. 

You may get a lot more hours than I did especially if you really get into the building/decorating of your village and farm. Also if you enjoy building friendships then there are plenty of npcs each with their own story/quests. I ran into a couple minor bugs while playing, nothing game breaking.

10/10 - This game is a breath of fresh air! So many QoL features that eliminate the irksome grind of most farming games makes it a joy to play. RF4 meets Sandrock but you're a cat rebuilding the town (with the freedom of AC when placing/decorating). Dev is incredibly involved with the community too. Plays like it's made for the steam deck.

Best implementation of a fishing game? Yep.
Huge variety for crafting? Yep.
Fleshed out combat system? Yep.
Massive quest system? Yep.
Tons to do and doesn't punish you for running out of time? Yep.
Genuinely, I think this game is better than Animal Crossing and Stardew solely because it doesn't roadblock you, respects what you want to do in game, and is highly rewarding.

In Bloodthief you get a similar core loop to Neon White: speedrunning short levels to improve your time. It is a first person movement platformer where you have no guns and instead fight up close with a blade, a bit like Ghostrunner. The blood mechanic and overall tone also clearly take inspiration from Ultrakill.

The standout is the movement. It feels good almost immediately, but it keeps getting better the more you play. The basics click fast and let you pull off smooth, fast runs early on, yet there is enough depth that you keep finding new ways to go faster. With enough skill, you reach speeds that even Usain Bolt on crack could not have dreamed of.

The visuals are simple, but most levels have their own setpieces, theming, and even soundtrack, which makes them easy to remember. I would rather have this than prettier visuals with levels that all blur together. There are some rough difficulty spikes. Two levels (2-5 and 3-9) are just far harder than anything else in the main campaign, even after being nerfed, and they feel out of place. I am also not a big fan of the stone realm 1&3). In theory they are meant to teach advanced movement, but in practice they barely explain anything and left me confused and annoyed trying to figure them out. 

I am a huge fan of this subgenre of first person movement platformers where you grind short levels, chase medals, and climb leaderboards. Bloodthief is very much built around that loop. If that does not sound appealing, the game probably will not click for you. But if you enjoy that style of game like I do, this is an incredibly fun title that I can strongly recommend.

What a wild ride. I first found out about this game on Youtube, followed the creator a little bit, then saw it came out last year. Started it up last night, and once it clicked, it clicked. Fluid movement, slick retro 3D levels, and a bangin' soundtrack all work perfectly together. At its best moments, it reminds me of Neon White.

There is some clunkiness to the levels, however. Some areas require exact momentum, which if you mess up, means you're screwed. Mostly, though, the combat is a bit iffy. So many times I'd die to something shooting at me from across the map. One level near the end had so many projectiles the game slowed down!

The introduction of the crossbow that allowed you to home in on the arrow is what really elevated this game. So much expression in movement through that alone.

Be warned, this game is short. Not a problem for me, but unless you're looking to beat times (I am not), then you'll "beat" this game in under 4 hours.

Oh, and the final level, the boss fight, was some of the sickest ♥♥♥♥ I've played in a while. Well done, Blargis!

I haven't played too many speed based games but this one makes me want to play more.

Bloodthief has you playing as a vampire that you might think cant fly until you start learning the ins and outs of the game. The game eases you in with the first few levels not being so difficult but still have some challenge to them while giving you books that explain how certain things work. Once you start getting used to the mechanics and how the speed physics work you'll start flying all over the place without ever needing to touch the ground (or sliding if thats your thing!) You will go so fast that you will get in a type of "zone" where you just react without even having to think about your inputs. The game combines all of this with different unlockable weapons that can aid you with certain things such as your blood not running out or explosive parry attacks. Every Chapter then introduces a new mechanic that boost the enjoyment of the game tenfold. I especially LOVE the blood tethers, I think it was my favorite part of the whole game. 

Not only is your goal to get from point A to B as quickly as possible, but it is encouraged as the better score you get, the more unlockable secrets you will obtain. They also have many little secret collectibles you can acquire in every level for those that are curious and like to explore. Pair it up with certain DOOM style Sections where you must kill all the enemies in an arena while going ultra fast chaining combos, Boss fights, and a kick ass dark electronic Soundtrack, you have yourself an incredibly exciting, dopamine rushing experience.

And if beating the game was not enough for you, you can gain the best rating for all the maps to unlock an even harder mode PLUS community Workshop map integration, so you can even play levels made by Users or make your own!

BUY THIS GAME! Its not too long and I think the length of the game was perfect, its short and sweet and to the point, and you can honestly pour tons of hours into it if you're a perfectionist and want to go for all the achievements and all the highest ranks or you just want to better your time in certain maps. Completely Addicting high fuel gameplay. Another Solo Dev Project that sparks that joy of what video games used to be all about.

What a wild ride. I first found out about this game on Youtube, followed the creator a little bit, then saw it came out last year. Started it up last night, and once it clicked, it clicked. Fluid movement, slick retro 3D levels, and a bangin' soundtrack all work perfectly together. At its best moments, it reminds me of Neon White.

There is some clunkiness to the levels, however. Some areas require exact momentum, which if you mess up, means you're screwed. Mostly, though, the combat is a bit iffy. So many times I'd die to something shooting at me from across the map. One level near the end had so many projectiles the game slowed down!

The introduction of the crossbow that allowed you to home in on the arrow is what really elevated this game. So much expression in movement through that alone.

Be warned, this game is short. Not a problem for me, but unless you're looking to beat times (I am not), then you'll "beat" this game in under 4 hours.

Oh, and the final level, the boss fight, was some of the sickest ♥♥♥♥ I've played in a while. Well done, Blargis!
Love secrets, Love moving fast, Love using sword, Love jumping big. Good game
hard but fun. It's like Neon White but without the hint system (other than following other people's ghosts)

I grinded one level until I got a skip done perfectly, and it boosted me into the top 500 (for just that level lol). 10/10 dopamine hit, but some of the more obscure movement tech isn't told to you explicitly, which might be some peoples thing, but I preferred routing and secret hunting (usually secret hunting for shortcuts to improve routing lol). There isn't too much of a story, mostly in short blurbs between levels, and in artefact descriptions, which I didn't mind, as the game is very much optimized for movement and the story is more of a cool treat at the end. I haven't played too many speed based games but this one makes me want to play more.

This is a amazing game, that is perfect for people who want like to feel like a god while they run through levels studying all of those who are faster than them just to be a couple seconds faster. I highly recomend.
worth every cent, skill ceiling is almost unheard of for speed running tech, which is really what this game excels at, found this through blargis' devlog and instantly bought

Incredibly tight/reliable controls, satisfying movement tech, and level design that makes routing just as fun of a challenge as going fast - this is the best speed game I've played and it isn't even close.

I love this game. It is so addicting. I just rage cuz I suck at zoomy zoomy.

This game is fantastic. Here is what I liked about it:

1) I absolutely adore how the movement feels. it's so satisfying when trying to speed through each level
2) The art style is wonderful and faithfully retro
3) The level design is very fun and works well with your moveset

There were a couple things I didn't like so much also:

1) Some enemy animations felt a bit poor. For example, the first enemy you come across, the knight guys with swords, their running animation felt off because they swing their swords around like crazy, when in reality it would be too heavy to swing around like that while running. Personally, I think frame-animations would have looked better than keyframes, but that might just be me.
2) Parrying can seem a bit inconsistent sometimes, but that's just a nitpick.
3) I think the music sounds a little bit generic, and not always as impactful or grand as it could be. It feels a lot more like montage music than action music.
4) The cyclops looking enemies. I think due to the nature of your movement and health system, this game works better when you're fighting lots of weak enemies rather than one strong enemy. Since you're usually one hit away from death, trying to fight projectile enemies at the same time as those cyclops enemies is sometimes inconsistent and you end up dying out of nowhere.

Overall, I really like this game, and I'm eager to see if it develops any further.

The game that doesn't hold you back. All game mechanics work together to make you feel POWERFUL.
Pure fun and challenge.

Hello, this is a very weird game for me. I play a lot a lot of games but there is usually 1 game that I play when watching youtube videos and just want to turn my brain off. Usually it is Tetris 99 on the switch. Anyway long story short this has been that game the past few months. I've always struggled with drugs and alcohol and getting sober a couple of months back this game helped me quite a lot going thru withdrawals and whatnot as it really doesn't asks you for a lot.

As a game itself it is not like the best game ever. Half of the bosses are kind an annoying to be honest. On the intended difficulty it is pretty hard, I think I've only gotten to 3rd boss max (I know you gotta beat 4 or 5 because I beat it on the learner difficulty a few times.) It is possible it is harder because of playing it with m&k. There are meaningful choices I think with the upgrade paths and the upgrades themselves so there is variety. It is well balanced for the most part - I think the middle ship needs a little longer projectile length and the blue shielded enemies should die from being shot in the front, just make them take a lot of damage as third ship for example can almost never circle them around fast enough even with 5x speed upgrade and if for whatever reason you cannot strike them you'd die, this feels especially unfair in X map pattern. Or make the third ship a bit faster :) but that will make it op I think.

All in all one of the best purchases I've ever made so thank you to dev, you did good brother.

Now I'm regularly clocking in 30 minute runs, have my upgrade route and card preferences sorted, and am just locked in to Sektori's rhythm. Can't praise this game enough, and with multiple game modes other than just the campaign (including some returning Geometry Wars classics), it's also a very generous package. Strong recommend.
Game dev can be sued for narcotraffic, this is incredibly addictive

Sektori works because every part of it is pulling in the same direction. The gameplay loop is exploration, attempts, small failures, and steady improvement. The tutorials teach the basics, then the game keeps upping the challenge so you’re always applying what you learned in new ways. Controls feel tight, spaces are easy to read, and each session leaves you with one more “ohhh” moment to carry forward. The visuals are clean and bold without being busy, so patterns, routes, and timing cues pop quickly. The music is the glue: steady, hypnotic, and weirdly motivating, like it’s nudging you to take one more run. When everything clicks, it’s not a cutscene payoff... it’s your brain and hands syncing up. It just works, and it makes me want to keep pushing until I’m genuinely better. It's also perfect for bite-sized sessions - you can jump in, make a little progress, feel something click, and walk away satisfied without needing a long stretch of time.
Wow. What a blast. This is the first game that has given me the Tetris effect since the original Lumines. A truly sensational game.

Not only is it roguelike Geometry Wars, it has additional side modes that each focus on one cool mechanic that help you practice for the main mode. My runs have been 3 min - 15 min in the first few hours of gameplay, so there's an compulsive "just one more game" feeling. The most polished twin stick shooter in recent memory!
Bumpin' music and truly well-designed bosses. This is a hell of a flow state game.
One of the smoothest twin stick shooters I have ever played.  Flexible upgrade system lets you choose your playstyle and provides good variety for replay value.  Great lunch break game.
excellent and tight mechanics. every feature has clearly been thought through and it's all cohesive. it may be tough, but git good. you owe it to yourself
Incredible game with tight mechanics and a real challenge. It doesn’t hold your hand, but that’s part of the appeal. Beginner tip: prioritize upgrading missiles early, made a huge difference for me.
Quite addictive, and super difficult

Jaw-dropping visuals and a very addictive loop. Tight controls. Deep, build-based run system.

I'm not really into bullet hell games, but this absolutely got its hooks into me.

Highly recommend.

If you loved Geometry Wars then this game is an evolution of that genre. I might say it's even a perfected version of Geometry Wars. But damn, it's hard. Even on the lowest difficulty it bends you over a barrel while someone plays the banjo to your endless screams of torment. I love it though. I'm addicted. And just like crack, I'm never giving it up no matter how it destroys my relationships, and my family. I give it a solid 8.5/10. It ♥♥♥♥♥ hard.

This game is hypnotically awesome and if you've ever played any game that goes pew-pew you really, really should play this one. The depth is there and it's SO satisfying when you get into the flow. The graphics and sound are a perfect synthesis and I don't want to keep going because it'd almost be pretentious to rave any more. This is the perfect game
the kind of aggressive game that makes it physically impossible to think about anything else while you're playing, 10/10

You halt the spreading wildfire with a greater inferno,
you pacify the furious captives by being the most upset,
and you quiet the bedlam star collapsing and exploding in the mind of every Shoot 'em up enthusiast, Pinball wizard and casual Arena shooter enjoyer by making this god-forsaken game. 

It is the chaos of the synapses given shape in dizzying spectrums of light and black, accompanied by the unstopping pulse of its mesmer beat. It doesn't relent, it just gets louder in every way that matters until you're good at it or you've given up. Hypnotic really does feel like the only correct descriptor.

I carries the air of a game that was completed once in development, and then again and again, combing through the experience through finished prototypes in repetition looking for each of its myriad edges, before choosing not to file them away, and instead sharpen each one into a toothy point all its own. There's multipliers upon multipliers, modes upon modes, and (most importantly for me) bosses upon bosses - There are fewer than 10, but it feels like 30.

Like the original Snakes & Ladders, the mantra is born again: Successes are short-lived, require constant vigilance, and provide little progress to the overall goal, whilst failures are as infinitely easier to fall victim to as they are more punishing in relation, but you may always try again. The runs are short, especially for the additional modes, but they pass by seemingly in slow-motion, and restarting takes only a moment.

If you need a greater spice in your life to feel something, a challenge to be overcome again and again, or you just need a reminder that you aren't yet decrepit and can still handle the pressure, or you've got something to prove to yourself, then pick this game up and blast some shapes to pieces in glorious, neon rapture.
If not, then get it anyway and take a gander, even if just to see what all of us strangers are doing to keep our gears oiled. You might learn a little something about why we enjoy this so much, despite how miserable an all-too-close failure can sometimes be; It's always been worth it, anyway.

You'll have a blast, I promise, maybe even several.

As of writing this, I am 5th in the [i]world[/i] in this game's Surge mode, and yet I sit forlorn in my given glowing praise; unsatisfied, [b]hungry[/b],
and I will return to it.

You know the game is good when you buy it full-priced through a real-life recommendation. 

It's excellent, nothing is more relaxing to masculine mind than a relentless audio-visual onslaught; you better not blink or flinch, because then you're probably dead. No handholding, no liveservice nonsense, just pure hardcore great gameplay. 

Tap into your lizardbrain and enjoy the ultra-focused zen state where you have no bandwidth for simply anything else in the world.
The next evolution of twin-stick shooters. Draws on Geometry Wars but adds a lot on top. Soundtrack is perfection!

Hard but fair, probably better than Geometry Wars 2, so if you're looking for something to scratch the same itch, this is the one. Just needs more players, I'm way too high up the leaderboard for how terrible I am at it.

Sektori is an absolute fantastic twin-stick shooter in the vein of Geometry Wars, easily one of the best twin stick shooters I've played in years. 
Sektori is a must-buy.

I've had a thing for twin-stick shooters for a while. I've played a few that are great. This is definitely a fantastic addition to the genre. Lots to unlock, things to shoot, pretty as heck, great techno music, and it plays amazing on my  21:9 monitor... I get it all! if you like twin-stick shooters, then you HAVE to get this. You won't be disappointed.
Do you like flow states? Thumping beats? Perfectly designed games? If the videos look at all appealing and you're up for a challenge, get this.

Absolute masterpiece. I thought this pedigree of shmup was lost when Housemarque announced their departure from making arcade games. 
As Super Stardust was to Asteroids, Resogun was to Defender, and Nex Machina to Robotron, so Sektori is to Geometry Wars. 
Kimmo being a former Housemarque developer explains how the DNA of all those great games is here now. The bosses in particular are so very Super Stardust. 
The power up system is new and interesting, a blend of Gradius and light deck building, and there is even a bonus mode ala Geometry Wars 2's Pacifism. 
My GOTY for 2025, and it's great on the SteamDeck.
Just beat the campaign, this is a very fun bullet hell/shmup with a good soundtrack and very good mechanics.

hunghf hunfgh hurnggg more colors for my dopamine receptors more bassline please i need more dopamine for my fired receptors

adderall is a necessary dlc but you can only snort it otherwise its useless
Incredible! I cannot stop playing. Best twin-stick shooter I have ever played.
I went from "WTF this is impossible" to "I must get on all the leaderboards"! A very well made and balanced Geometry Wars-like.

Do you like to play your games in short bursts? Do you like Geometry Wars? Or arcade Shoot-'em-Ups in general? Do you like fast paced action and hard hittig techno music? Then don't waste your time and grab this gem.
I’ve only played this a bit so far but if you are into twin stick shooters even a little bit then this one is for you.

TLDR; highly recommend Sektori. it is both easy to pickup casually, and easy to really sink your feet into, with a high skill ceiling for mastery.

very fun and engaging game; it is the perfect evolution of geometry wars-style gameplay, with the addition of unique bosses, perks, and a dash mechanic that adds a lot of depth to gameplay, overall has a much higher skill ceiling all things accounted for (lots of replay-ability).

perfect type of game for handheld pc gaming, if u have a steam deck or something, and it will put you in a trance if you have an OLED screen; one of the best looking games i have played on an OLED screen.
